PROJECTS UNDER NCF IN ANDHRA PRADESH
*1 SHRI G LAKSHMINARAYANA
Will the Minister of CULTURE be pleased to state:
a) the details of projects of Andhra Pradesh State that have been
approved under the National Culture Fund (NCF) along with the year
of approval;
B the details of funds allocated, released and utilized for each approved
project under NCF from the said State;
c) the details of proposals submitted by the said state under NCF along
with their current status; and
d) whether the Government plans to fund additional cultural projects of
Andhra Pradesh State under NCF in future and, if so, the details
thereof?
स(cid:214)ं कृ ित एवं पय(cid:170)टन म(cid:253)ं ी (गज(cid:198)े (cid:254) िसहं शेखावत) :
(क) से (घ) िववरण सभा पटल पर रख िदया गया है।
िववरण
(क) से (घ) एनसीएफ को, देश भर से (cid:255)ा(cid:302) स(cid:214)ं कृित संबंधी िविभ(cid:198)न प(cid:229)रयोजना (cid:255)(cid:214)ताव(cid:332) क(cid:236) जांच
करने और िदशािनद(cid:165)श(cid:332) के अनुसार उ(cid:198)ह(cid:164) यो(cid:181)यता के आधार पर अनुमोिदत करने का अिधदेश है।
 The sign + marked above the name of a Member indicates that the Question was actually asked on the floor of the House
by that Member.
03.02.2025 13
िवगत पांच वष(cid:334) के दौरान एनसीएफ के अंतग(cid:170)त आ(cid:328)ं (cid:255)देश रा(cid:186)य से कोई भी प(cid:229)रयोजना (cid:255)(cid:214)ताव
(cid:255)ा(cid:302)/अनमु ोिदत नह(cid:233) ह(cid:242)आ है।
SHRI G. LAKSHMINARAYANA: Thank you hon. Speaker, Sir.
While the National Culture Fund (NCF) has successfully facilitated the
restoration of numerous historic monuments across the country, I would like to
bring to your attention the Gooty Fort located in my constituency of Anantapur.
This majestic fort, the largest in the State, has witnessed the rise and fall of
empires – from the Chalukyas to the Vijayanagara Empire, the Mughals, the
Marathas and even the British. ....(Interruptions)
11.04 hrs
At this stage, Sushri S. Jothimani, Dr. Amar Singh and some other hon.
Members came and stood on the floor near the Table.
Strategically located near NH-44, NH-67 and the Gooty railway junction,
it holds immense potential to become a key tourism hub. However, the fort is
currently in a state of neglect. A proposal of Rs.460 crore for its restoration has
already been submitted for its restoration under the NCF, which is still remains
pending. ....(Interruptions)
I would like to ask the hon. Minister if the Government will take up this
project at the earliest and expedite its approval to ensure the timely restoration
of Gooty Fort? Thank you, Sir. ....(Interruptions)
SHRI GAJENDRA SINGH SHEKHAWAT: Sir, under the provisions of the
National Culture Fund, the financial arrangements are being done out of the
donations that we receive or the money that we receive as a contribution from
the companies and PSUs through CSR. ....(Interruptions) So, primarily and
predominantly, the funding agencies take a call and give us the priority. We
03.02.2025 14
have already enlisted 150 projects on the website of NCF.
The said project, which the hon. MP has mentioned, is also a part of that.
It has been depicted on the website. Whenever adequate funding will be agreed
by any of the PSU or any of the company which is giving CSR fund, we will
certainly consider it on priority. … (Interruptions)
SHRI G. LAKSHMINARAYANA: Thank you, hon. Minister. However, I would
like to bring to your attention that the issue extends beyond Gooty Fort alone.
There are 20 other proposals from Andhra Pradesh that are also awaiting
approval under the National Culture Fund for the preservation, protection, and
promotion of the historical monuments. … (Interruptions)
Hon. Speaker, Sir, it is concerning to note that in the last five years, not
a single project from Andhra Pradesh has been approved under the NCF. I
kindly request the hon. Minister to provide clarity on the reasons behind the
delay in processing these proposals and the steps being taken to ensure their
timely approval and restoration. Thank you, Sir. … (Interruptions)

24 म(cid:164), िकसी कम(cid:170)चारी (क(cid:164) (cid:254) सरकार या रा(cid:186)य सरकार के कम(cid:170)चारी के अलावा) के मामल े म(cid:164)

लाख (cid:321)पये स े बढ़ाकर 25 लाख (cid:321)पये कर िदया गया।
(ii) धारा 80सीसीडी( 2) के तहत कम(cid:170)चा(cid:229)रय(cid:332) (क(cid:164) (cid:254) सरकार और रा(cid:186)य सरकार के अलावा)

बढ़ाकर 14% कर िदया गया है।
(iii) वेतनभोगी (cid:211)यि(cid:265)य(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा (cid:255)ा(cid:302) िन(cid:204)निलिखत भ(cid:176)(cid:332) पर कर छूट उपल(cid:202)ध है :

के संबंध म(cid:164) या(cid:253)ा क(cid:236) अविध के िलए,
 िकसी काया(cid:170)लय या रोजगार के कत(cid:170)(cid:211)य(cid:332) के िन(cid:213)पादन म(cid:164) प(cid:229)रवहन पर िकए गए (cid:211)यय को पूरा
करने के िलए िदया गया कोई प(cid:229)रवहन भ(cid:176)ा;

पूण(cid:170)तः होने वाले (cid:211)यय को पूरा करने के िलए िवशेष (cid:322)प से (cid:255)दान िकया जाता ह ै

03.02.2025 29
Participation of Youth in Viksit Bharat -2047
*3. †Smt. D K Aruna:
Shri Eatala Rajender:
Will the Minister of YOUTH AFFAIRS AND SPORTS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is encouraging youth to drive progress and make
the country a developed nation;
(b) if so, the efforts made so far in achieving resolutions of Young Leaders
Dialogue, 2025 and the larger goal of Viksit Bharat-2047 and the outcome
thereof;
(c) whether the Government has advised the youth to step out of their comfort
zones and take risks, so that Global agencies recognise their potential and also
to contribute significantly to boost India’s GDP;
(d) if so, the details thereof;
(e) the achievements made so far under the schemes like Nehru Yuva Kendra
Sangathan (NYKS) and National Young Leaders Programme (NYLP) and such
others, being implemented with a view to achieve the goal of Viksit Bharat-
2047;
(f) the funds sanctioned/spent under various schemes/programmes being
implemented to achieve the goal of Viksit Bharat-2047, during the last ten
years, State-wise; and
(g) the future action plan worked out/implemented in this regard along with the
status of participation of NGOs, State-wise?
युवक काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म और खेल म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ीमती र(cid:177)ा िनिखल खाडसे) :
(क) से (छ) िववरण सभा पटल पर रख िदया गया है ।
03.02.2025 30
िववरण
(क) जी हां । सरकार यवु ाओ ंको (cid:255)गित का वाहक बनने और देश को िवकिसत रा(cid:213)(cid:367) बनाने के िलए
(cid:255)ो(cid:194)सािहत कर रही है ।
(ख) “िवकिसत भारत 2047” पहल का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य भारत क(cid:236) (cid:214)वतं(cid:253)ता के शता(cid:202)दी वष(cid:170) तक एक
िवकिसत रा(cid:213)(cid:367) बनने क(cid:236) आकां(cid:177)ा को साकार करना है । चूंिक, वष(cid:170) 2047 म(cid:164) भारत क(cid:236) (cid:214)वतं(cid:253)ता के
100 वष(cid:170) परू ेहो रहे ह(cid:167), इस पहल के मा(cid:197)यम से (cid:211)यापक और सतत िवकास क(cid:236) (cid:255)ाि(cid:302) के िलए आिथ(cid:170)क,
सामािजक, पया(cid:170)वरणीय और शासन के (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) के (cid:255)यास(cid:332) को संगिठत करना है । यवु ाओ ं म(cid:164) रा(cid:213)(cid:367)
िनमा(cid:170)ण क(cid:236) (cid:177)मता है, इसिलए हम(cid:164) उ(cid:198)ह(cid:164) अवसर दने ा चािहए ।
रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय यवु ा महो(cid:194)सव (एनवाईएफ), रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय यवु ा िकशोर िवकास काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म (एनपीवाईएडी)
(cid:214)क(cid:236)म के (cid:255)मुख काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म(cid:332) म(cid:164) से एक है । इस वष(cid:170), एनवाईएफ का पुनग(cid:170)ठन िकया गया और इसे

FRAUD CASES IN BANKS
*4. †SHRIMATI DAGGUBATI PURANDESWARI:
DR. SHRIKANT EKNATH SHINDE:
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the number of bank-fraud related cases reported during the
last five years, year and bank-wise;
(b) whether the Government has data on the average investigation time in bank
fraud related cases and if so, the details thereof;
(c) the steps taken by the Government to expedite the investigation of such
cases since January 2020;
(d) whether the Government has taken any initiatives to prevent fraud and loan
defaults in public sector banks and if so, the details thereof along with the
extent to which these have been effective thereto; and
(e) whether the Government is considering to create a special investigation unit
for bank fraud related cases and if so, the details thereof?

करके साइबर अपराध के फोरि(cid:164) सक िव(cid:308)ेषण तथा जांच म(cid:164) सुिवधा के िलए रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय साइबर फोरि(cid:164) सक
लेबोर(cid:367)े ी का गठन िकया गया है।
उपय(cid:170)ु(cid:265) उपाय(cid:332) के कारण पीएसबी क(cid:236) सकल अनज(cid:170)क आि(cid:214)त जो माच(cid:170) 2019 म(cid:164) 7.40
लाख करोड़ (cid:321)पए (सकल एनपीए का 11.59%) थी, िसत(cid:204)बर 2024 (अनंितम आंकड़े) म(cid:164) कम
होकर 3.16 लाख करोड़ (cid:321)पए (सकल एनपीए का 3.09%) हो गया है तथा उ(cid:265) अविध के दौरान
चूक अनुपात 3.96% से कम होकर 1.13% हो गया है।
ब(cid:167)क धोखाधड़ी के िलए िवशेष अ(cid:198)वेषण इकाई गिठत करने के संबंध म(cid:164) सीबीआई के पास पहले से
ही ब(cid:167)क धोखाधड़ी संबंधी मामल(cid:332) के अ(cid:198)वेषण के िलए ब(cid:167)िकं ग सुर(cid:177)ा और धोखाधड़ी शाखा मौजूद
है तथा रा(cid:186)य पुिलस के पास भी आिथ(cid:170)क अपराध िवंग है, जो ब(cid:167)क धोखाधड़ी के मामल(cid:332) सिहत
अ(cid:198)वेषण का काय(cid:170) करता है। इसके अलावा, ब(cid:167)क धोखाधड़ी संबंधी मामल(cid:332) के िलए िवशेष अ(cid:198)वेषण
इकाई गिठत करने हेत ु कोई (cid:255)(cid:214)ताव वत(cid:170)मान म(cid:164) सरकार के िवचाराधीन नह(cid:233) है।

SHRIMATI DAGGUBATI PURANDESWARI: Hon. Speaker Sir, India today is
witnessing an increase in digital fraud cases, which is an area of deep concern
for us. Cybercriminals are actually resorting to advanced and sophisticated
techniques to outmanoeuvre the law enforcement and security systems. Sir, a
particularly alarming trend in this direction is the digital arrest scam wherein
fraudsters are impersonating as law enforcement officers and coercing public
to part with large sums of money. … (Interruptions) Additionally, the use of mule
bank accounts has also escalated wherein illicit money is seamlessly brought
03.02.2025 48
into the country and techniques such as phishing, malware and even sim
cloning are also further compromising the financial security of millions of Indian
citizens. … (Interruptions)
Sir, my question is: what are the measures the Government is taking to
curb the rising menace of digital arrest scam and the misuse of mule bank
accounts to safeguard the interests of Indian citizens from the financial fraud?
… (Interruptions)

रोकने के िलए हमारी सरकार (cid:255)ितब(cid:294) है ।…((cid:211)यवधान) अपराध और अपरािधय(cid:332) के (cid:255)ित हमारी
सरकार क(cid:236) जीरो टॉलरस(cid:164) नीित है ।…((cid:211)यवधान) सरकार के िविभ(cid:198)न िवभाग और रा(cid:186)य सरकार (cid:164)
आपस म(cid:164) सम(cid:198)वय कर इन घटनाओ ंको रोकने और अपरािधय(cid:332) को पकड़ने म(cid:164) लगी ह(cid:242)ई ह(cid:167) ।
म(cid:167) इस िबंद ु पर सदन को बताना चाहता ह(cid:243)ं िक हमारी रोकथाम हेत ु गिठत आईफोरसी,
इंिडयन साइबर (cid:248)ाइम कोऑिड(cid:170)नेशन स(cid:164)टर (cid:300)ारा दरू संचार िवभाग के साथ सम(cid:198)वय कर 17 हजार

KENDRIYA VIDYALAYA, TARAKESWAR
*6. SHRI JAGANNATH SARKAR:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) the status of the construction of the permanent campus for Kendriya
Vidyalaya (KV) Tarakeswar, West Bengal and the reasons for the delay,
if any;
(b) whether the Government has allocated funds and resources to expedite
the construction process for the above campus and if so, the details
thereof;
(c) whether KV, Tarakeswar is currently operating from a temporary
accommodation and meets the required standards for quality education;
and
(d) the steps being taken by the Government to ensure that KV, Tarakeswar
receives adequate facilities to expand its educational offerings including
introduction of Commerce and Humanities streams?

UNCLAIMED INSURANCE FUNDS
*7. †SHRI VIJAYAKUMAR ALIAS VIJAY VASANTH:
SHRI MANICKAM TAGORE B:
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has any data on the total amount of unclaimed
insurance funds in the country and if so, the details thereof along with the
amount claimed by beneficiaries from that amount during the last three years;
(b) the number of life insurance policies which are currently unclaimed along
with the average amount due to each policy holder;
03.02.2025 73
(c) whether the Government has taken any steps to raise awareness about
unclaimed insurance funds and if so, the proposed legislation or regulatory
changes made to simplify the process of claiming unclaimed insurance funds
and reduce unclaimed policies;
(d) whether the nominees are required to search each insurer's website
individually to locate unclaimed funds and if so, the reasons therefor along
with the steps taken by the Government to create a centralized database to
facilitate the beneficiaries;
(e) the number of insurance companies which are currently complying with
IRDAI guidelines to list unclaimed amounts of 1000 or more on their websites;
(f) whether the Government plans to address concerns around data privacy
and security in the process of claiming unclaimed insurance funds along with
the number of e-insurance accounts opened through CDSL or NSDL and the
way by which it has impacted the number of unclaimed insurance funds;
(g) the measures taken to prevent insurance companies from rejecting claims
for unclaimed funds due to minor discrepancies in documentation; and
(h) the average time taken by the insurers to process claims for unclaimed
funds along with the details of penalties defined for delays in processing
claims?
िव(cid:176) म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी पंकज चौधरी) :
(क) से (ज) िववरण सभा पटल पर रख िदया गया है।
िववरण
(क) और (ख) इरडाई ने भारत म(cid:164) बीमाकता(cid:170)ओ ंके पास मौजूद अदावाकृत बीमा िनिध का (cid:202)यौरा
िन(cid:204)नानुसार उपल(cid:202)ध कराया है:-

िदनांक 31.3.2022 31.3.2023 31.3.2024
रािश (करोड़ (cid:321)पए म(cid:164)) 25,403 23,699 21,718
इरडाई ने यह भी सूिचत िकया है िक यह अदावाकृत रािश से लाभािथ(cid:170)य(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा दावा क(cid:236) गई रािश से
संबंिधत आंकड़े, वत(cid:170)मान म(cid:164) अदावाकृत जीवन बीमा पॉिलिसय(cid:332) क(cid:236) स(cid:180)ं या और (cid:255)ित जीवन पॉिलसी
अदावाकृत रािश क(cid:236) औसत रािश से संबंिधत आंकड़े नह(cid:233) रखता है।
(ग) इरडाई (cid:300)ारा अदावाकृत िनिध को कम करने और इस संबंध म(cid:164) पॉिलसीधारक(cid:332) के बीच
जाग(cid:322)कता फै लाने के िलए िविभ(cid:198)न िविनयामक(cid:236)य उपाय िनधा(cid:170)(cid:229)रत िकए ह(cid:167)। इनम(cid:164) से कुछेक
िन(cid:204)नानुसार ह(cid:167):-
(i) बीमा कं पिनय(cid:332) म(cid:164) पॉिलसीधारक का संर(cid:177)ण, िशकायत िनवारण और दावा िनगरानी
सिमित, जो बोड(cid:170) (cid:214)तरीय सिमित है, को ितमाही आधार पर दाव(cid:332) क(cid:236) स(cid:180)ं या और
रािश तथा कं पनी (cid:300)ारा पॉिलसीधारक(cid:332) या लाभािथ(cid:170)य(cid:332) को अिभिचि(cid:313)त करने के िलए
उठाए गए कदम सिहत अदावाकृत रािश के िनपटान क(cid:236) समी(cid:177)ा करने के िलए
अिधदेिशत िकया गया है।
(ii) इरडाई का बीमा भरोसा पोट(cid:170)ल सभी बीमाकता(cid:170)ओ ं के वेबसाइट िलंक तक पह(cid:242)ंच
(cid:255)दान करता है। जहां उनके (cid:300)ारा धा(cid:229)रत अदावाकृ त रािश के (cid:202)यौर े (cid:255)दिश(cid:170)त िकए
जाते ह(cid:167)।
(iii) बीमाकता(cid:170)ओ ंके पास अदावाकृत रािश के संचयन म(cid:164) कमी सिु नि(cid:306)त करने के िलए
इरडाई ने पािलसी जारी करते समय नािमती का (cid:202)यौरा जैसे नािमती का नाम और
उसका मोबाइल नं., ई-मेल आईडी, पता (cid:255)ा(cid:302) करने को अिधदेिशत िकया है।

क(cid:236) गई है तािक वे अपने (cid:255)ित देय अदावाकृत रािश को पॉिलसीधारक के नाम और ज(cid:198)मितिथ,

सक(cid:164) । उपय(cid:170)ु(cid:265) सूचना के आधार पर वह (cid:211)यि(cid:265) रािश के दावे के िलए बीमाकता(cid:170) से संपक(cid:170) कर सकता
है।

(ङ) इरडाई ने यह पिु (cid:310) क(cid:236) है िक सभी बीमा कं पिनयां इसके िदशािनद(cid:165)श(cid:332) का अनुपालन कर रही ह(cid:167)।
(च) इरडाई ने िदनांक 24.4.2023 के ‘इरडाई सूचना और साइबर सरु (cid:177)ा िदशािनद(cid:165)श’ जारी िकए

31 माच(cid:170), 2024 क(cid:236) ि(cid:214)थित के अनुसार, कुल 1.83 करोड़ इल(cid:179)े (cid:367)ॉिनक बीमा खाते (ईआईए) सिृ जत
िकए गए ह(cid:167)। इरडाई ने पॉिलसीधारक(cid:332) के िहत(cid:332) का संर(cid:177)ण, बीमाकता(cid:170)ओ ंके प(cid:229)रचालन और स(cid:204)ब(cid:294)
काय(cid:170) िविनयम, 2024 के मा(cid:197)यम से ई-बीमा जारी करना अिनवाय(cid:170) िकया है। ई-बीमा पॉिलिसयां
पॉिलसीधारक(cid:332) के िलए अिधक सलु भ ह(cid:167), जो बीमाधारक(cid:332) को अपनी पॉिलिसय(cid:332) के बारे म (cid:164) जाग(cid:322)क
होने क(cid:236) संभावनाओ ंम(cid:164) पया(cid:170)(cid:302) विृ (cid:294) करती ह(cid:167) और आव(cid:212)यकता पड़ने पर दावा करने म(cid:164) उ(cid:198)ह(cid:164) स(cid:177)म
बनाती ह(cid:167)।

for the said purpose?

(एसएएससीआई) – वैि(cid:309)क (cid:214)तर पर (cid:255)िति(cid:311)त पय(cid:170)टक क(cid:164) (cid:254)(cid:332) का िवकास' नामक अपनी योजना के

तहत िव(cid:176)ीय वष(cid:170) 2024-25 म(cid:164) देश के 23 रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) म (cid:164) 3295.76 करोड़ (cid:321). क(cid:236) 40 प(cid:229)रयोजनाओ ंको

(cid:179)या (cid:174)म और रोजगार मं(cid:253)ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृ पा कर(cid:164)गे िक:
(क) (cid:255)धानमं(cid:253)ी (cid:174)म योगी मान-धन (पीएम-एसवाईएम) योजना के तहत म(cid:197)य (cid:255)देश, छ(cid:253)पित

गए ह(cid:167);

ASSAM

thereof;

enhance infrastructure and funding for cultural institutions and events

inclusivity and representation of all cultural and ethnic communities in

मचं कला (cid:202) यूरो, स(cid:214)ं कृ ित म(cid:253)ं ालय (cid:300)ारा सचं ािलत (cid:214) क(cid:236)म(cid:164)
1. ग(cid:322)ु -िश(cid:213) य परपं रा के सवं ध(cid:170)न के िलए िव(cid:194) तीय सहायता (रपे ट(cid:170)री अनुदान)
इस (cid:214) क(cid:236)म का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212) य नाट्य समूह(cid:332), रंगमंच समूह(cid:332), सगं ीत मंडिलय(cid:332), बाल रंगमंच आिद जैसे
मंचकला काय(cid:170)कलाप(cid:332) क(cid:236) सभी शैिलय(cid:332) तथा ग(cid:321)ु -िश(cid:213) य परपं रा के अन(cid:322)ु प कलाकार(cid:332) को उनके
03.02.2025 109
संबंिधत ग(cid:321)ु (cid:300)ारा िनयिमत आधार पर (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण दने े के िलए िव(cid:176)ीय सहायता (cid:255)दान करना है। इस
(cid:214) क(cid:236)म के अनुसार, रंगमंच (cid:177)े(cid:253) म(cid:164) 1 ग(cid:321)ु और अिधकतम 18 िश(cid:213) य(cid:332) को सहायता और संगीत और
न(cid:194)ृ य (cid:177)े(cid:253) म(cid:164) 01 ग(cid:322)ु और अिधकतम 10 िश(cid:213) य(cid:332) को सहायता (cid:255)दान क(cid:236) जाती है। कलाकार क(cid:236) आयु
के आधार पर सहायता क(cid:236) रािश- ग(cid:321)ु के िलए 15000/- (cid:321). (cid:255)ितमाह, िश(cid:213) य के िलए 2000-
10000/- (cid:321)पए (cid:255)ितमाह है।
2. कला और स(cid:214)ं कृ ित के सवं ध(cid:170)न के िलए िव(cid:194) तीय सहायता: इस (cid:214) क(cid:236)म म(cid:164) िन(cid:204) निलिखत घटक
शािमल ह(cid:167) :
i. रा(cid:213) (cid:367)ीय मह(cid:194) व के सां(cid:214)क ृ ितक सगं ठन(cid:332) को िव(cid:194) तीय सहायता
देश म(cid:164) कला और सं(cid:214) कृित के संवध(cid:170)न म(cid:164) शािमल रा(cid:213) (cid:367)ीय मह(cid:194) व के सां(cid:214) कृितक संगठन(cid:332) का
संवध(cid:170)न और उ(cid:198) ह(cid:164) सहायता (cid:255)दान करना है। यह अनदु ान उन संगठन(cid:332) को िदया जाता है िजनका
एक सुगिठत (cid:255)बंधन िनकाय हो, जो भारत म(cid:164) पंजीकृत ह(cid:332), जो अिखल भारतीय (cid:214) तर पर (cid:255)चालन
करते ह(cid:242)ए रा(cid:213) (cid:367)ीय मह(cid:194) व के ह(cid:332) और िजनके पास पया(cid:170)(cid:200) त काय(cid:170)बल हो और िज(cid:198) ह(cid:332)ने िवगत पांच (5)
वष(cid:334) म(cid:164) से 3 वष(cid:334) के दौरान सां(cid:214) कृितक काय(cid:170)कलाप(cid:332) के िलए 1 करोड़ (cid:321)पये या उससे अिधक का

ने अपनी सि(cid:248)य आयु म (cid:164) कला, सािह(cid:194) य आिद के अपने िवशेष (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म(cid:164) मह(cid:194) वपूण(cid:170) योगदान िदया हो

6. सेवा भोज योजना
'सेवा भोज योजना' क(cid:236) (cid:214) क(cid:236)म के अंतग(cid:170)त धमा(cid:170)थ(cid:170)/धािम(cid:170)क स(cid:214)ं थाओ ं को जनता को म(cid:201)ु त

03.02.2025 117
THE MINISTER OF FINANCE; AND MINISTER OF CORPORATE AFFAIRS
(SRIMATI NIRMALA SITHARAMAN):
(a) to (d) A Statement is laid on the Table of the House.
STATEMENT
(a) and (b) The Prime Minister’s Internship Scheme (PMIS) was announced in
the Budget 2024-25. It aims to provide internship opportunities to one crore
youth in top 500 companies in five years. As an initiation to this Scheme,
Ministry of Corporate Affairs has launched a Pilot Project of the Scheme on
3rd October, 2024, which is targeted to provide 1.25 lakh internship
opportunities to the youth in the Financial Year 2024-25. Guidelines
containing details for Prime Minister’s Internship Scheme - Pilot Project are
available at https://pminternship.mca.gov.in.
The top 500 companies have been selected on the basis of the average
Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) expenditure in the last 3 years. Apart
from these, any other company/ bank/ financial institution desirous of
participating in the Scheme, may do so with the approval of the Ministry of
Corporate Affairs (MCA), which would take a view keeping in view
underrepresented sectors and areas in the above mentioned 500
companies. The list of the partner companies is available at
https://pminternship.mca.gov.in. Participation of the companies in this
scheme is voluntary. The Ministry is working in active collaboration with
industry associations for the implementation of the Pilot Project.
(c) The Guidelines of the PM Internship Scheme Pilot Project provide that the
duration of the Internship shall be 12 months, and at least half of the
internship period must be spent in the actual working experience/ job
03.02.2025 118
environment, and not in the classroom. Thus, the PM Internship Scheme
provides an opportunity to the youth to get training, gain experience and
skills within the real-life environment of the businesses or organizations that
helps in bridging the gap between academic learning and industry
requirements, in turn, assisting enhancement of her/his employability.
(d) In Round I of the PM Internship Scheme Pilot Project about 1.27 lakh
Internship Opportunities were offered by the partner companies across the
country including in the states of Uttar Pradesh, Jharkhand and Haryana.
State wise internship opportunities provided under the scheme is enclosed
at Annexure.

TAX COLLECTION AFTER REDUCTION IN CORPORATE TAX RATES
*18. †Shri Bhartruhari Mahtab
Shri Balabhadra Majhi:
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state:
(a) whether there has been an overall increase in tax collection and foreign
investment inflows after reduction in corporate tax rates, if so, the details
thereof, year-wise;
(b) the industry-wise details of tax benefits extended and their impact on
economic growth;
(c) the specific incentives being considered by the Government to attract
more international corporations like Apple, Google and Tesla to establish
03.02.2025 121
manufacturing unit in the country; and
(d) the manner in which these initiatives impact tax revenue and economic
growth to achieve the India’s vision of becoming $5 trillion economy?
THE MINISTER OF FINANCE; AND MINISTER OF CORPORATE AFFAIRS
(SMT. NIRMALA SITHARAMAN) :
(a) to (d) A statement is laid on the Table of the House.
Statement
(a) Yes.
There has been an overall increase in direct tax collections after reduction of the
corporate tax rates with effect from AY 2020-21 (except in FY 2020-21 being covid
affected year). The year-wise details of the net direct tax collection in the last five
years are as under:
(Rs. In Crore)

16,14,572*

Source: Pr. CCA (CBDT)

03.02.2025 122
The Net Foreign Investment Inflows have increased from USD 44,417 (in
Million) in FY 2019-20 to USD 53,105 (in Million) in FY 2023-24 [As per data
available on the website of Reserve Bank of India (https://data.rbi.org.in/)].
(b) The domestic companies are provided certain tax benefits under different
provisions of the Income-tax Act in the Old tax regime which interalia are as
under:
S.No Nature of tax benefit
1. Deduction of export profits to units/undertakings located in SEZs
2. Deduction/weighted deduction for expenditure on scientific research
3 Deduction for a specified business with fulfillment of certain conditions
related to Petroleum & Natural Gas
4. Deduction of profits of undertakings engaged in development of
infrastructure facilities, generation, transmission and distribution of
power and undertakings engaged in revival of power plant or providing
telecommunication services
5. Deduction of profits of industrial undertakings derived from the
following-
 production of mineral oil and natural gas
 housing projects
 integrated business of handling, storage and transportation of
food grains
 processing, preservation and packaging of fruits and vegetables
 collecting and processing of bio- degradable waste
6. Deduction for Offshore Banking Units [OBUs] and International
Financial Services Centre [IFSC]
7.
Deduction for undertakings setup in North Eastern states and Sikkim
8.
Deduction for employment of new workmen
03.02.2025 123
9.
Deduction for eligible start-ups
10.
Deduction for Dividend received
The total revenue impact on account of tax benefits extended to companies
was Rs. 96,892.39 Crores and Rs. 1,09,333.38 Crores (projected) in FY 2021-
22 and FY 2022-23 respectively (Source: Receipt Budget 2024-25). The above
tax benefits have the impact of making the corporates competitive and
encouraging investment and therefore economic growth.
(c) The specific incentives to be provided in the Income-tax Act as
considered appropriate form part of the Finance Bill.
(d) In order to create a globally competitive business environment for
domestic companies, attract fresh investment and create employment
opportunities, section 115BAA and section 115BAB were introduced in Income-
tax Act through Taxation Laws (Amendment) Act, 2019. The impact of section
115BAB is reflected in a significant growth of new manufacturing companies
from 2,928 in AY 2022-23 to 7,185 in AY 2024-25.
To encourage start-ups, initiatives taken have resulted into an increase in the
number of start-ups claiming deduction under section 80IAC from 328 in AY
2022-23 to 877 in AY 2024-25. Further, the number of companies covered
under section 80JJAA in respect of employment of new employees has
increased from 2,838 in AY 2022-23 to 3,644 in AY 2024-25.
The initiatives taken have led to generation of employment, increase in tax
revenue and overall economic growth.

WRITTEN ANSWERS TO QUESTIONS
((i) Starred Question Nos. 19 and 20
CULTURAL FUNCTION AND PRODUCTION GRANT SCHEME
*19. SHRI ANURAG SHARMA:
Will the MINISTER OF CULTURE be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the Cultural Function and Production Grant Scheme (CFPGS)
including eligibility criteria, types of activities supported and the quantum of
assistance provided thereunder;
(b) the details of the number of applications received and approved under the
said scheme during the last three years, State-wise;
(c) the measures taken by the Government to promote and support cultural
functions and productions particularly in rural and underserved areas;
(d) the details of the steps taken by the Government to ensure transparency and
accountability in the selection process and utilization of funds under the said
scheme; and
(e) the plans of the Government to expand and strengthen the CFPGS including
increasing funding and supporting more diverse range of cultural activities
thereunder?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI GAJENDRA
SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) The scheme of Cultural Function and Production Grant (CFPG) covers all ‘not-
for-profit’ organizations, NGOs, Societies, Trusts and Universities for supporting the
Seminars, Conference, Research, Workshops, Festivals, Exhibitions, Symposia,
Production of Dance, Drama-Theatre, Music etc. and small research projects on
03.02.2025 126
different aspects of Indian Culture. As per the guidelines of CFPG scheme, the
following is the eligibility criteria:-
(i) The Organization should have been functioning and registered under the
Societies Registration Act (XXI of 1860), Trusts Act, Companies Act or any
Central or State Act for at least three years.
(ii) The applicant organizations that are voluntary organizations or NGOs,
should, in order to qualify for the grant, have a properly constituted
managing body with its powers, duties and responsibilities clearly defined
and laid down in the form of a written constitution.
(iii) The applicant organizations that are voluntary organizations or NGOs must
register themselves on the NGO Darpan Portal of NITI Aayog and obtain
a Unique ID from the Portal. The organizations are required to intimate the
Unique ID as obtained from NGO Darpan Portal and PAN Number of the
organization while applying under the Scheme.
(iv) The Organization must have tied up or planned the matching resources at
least to the extent of 25 % of the project cost.
(v) The Organization should have facilities, resources, personnel and
experience to take up the event/ project for which a grant is required.
(vi) Past experience of holding such functions, as applied for, would be given
preference.
Quantum of financial assistance under the scheme is restricted to 75% of the
expenditure of the proposed project, subject to a maximum of Rs.5.00 lakhs.
However, the Ministry may in exceptional circumstances increase the assistance to
any project of outstanding merit and relevance upto Rs. 20.00 lakhs, with the
03.02.2025 127
approval of Competent Authority. The grant is being released in two installments of
75% and 25%.
(b) Details of number of applications received and approved under the said
scheme during the last three years; State-Wise is enclosed as STATEMENT-I.
(c) and (d) In order to promote and support cultural functions and productions
particularly in rural and underserved areas, Ministry of Culture is administering two
schemes namely ‘Cultural Function and Production Grant’ and ‘Financial Assistance
to Cultural Organizations with National Presence’ which envisages staging of
production, festivals etc. in different genres of arts and culture across the country
including rural and underserved areas. Brief of these schemes is given at
STATEMENT-II. Under these schemes, applications are invited yearly for fresh
production, festival from cultural organizations for placing the same before the duly
constituted Expert Committee. The Expert Committee evaluates these applications
and recommends financial assistance based on their cultural profile, quality of
proposal and submission of other requisite mandatory documents by these
organizations as laid down in the guidelines of these schemes. Thereafter,
recommendation given by the Expert Committee are approved by the Competent
Authority in the Ministry.
The financial assistance to the approved beneficiaries is released in two
instalments i.e. 1st Instalment (75%) provided in advance for staging the
production/festival/seminar etc. as recommended by the Expert Committee and 2nd
Instalment (remaining 25%) is provided on submission of requisite mandatory
documents viz. utilization certificate of the 1st received instalment, photographs of
the staged, press clipping etc.
(e) No such proposal is under consideration of the Government.
03.02.2025 128

STATEMENT-II
Details of Cultural Function and Production Grant Scheme and Financial
Assistance to Cultural organizations with National Presence
1. Cultural Function and Production Grant (CFPG): The objective of this
scheme component is to provide financial support to NGOs/ Societies/ Trusts/
Universities etc. for Seminars, Conference, Research, Workshops, Festivals,
Exhibitions, Symposia, Production of Dance, Drama-Theatre, Music etc. The
maximum grants provided under CFPG is Rs.5 Lakh for an organization which
can be increased to Rs. 20.00 lakhs in exceptional cases. Details of year-wise
release of fund along with number of beneficiaries supported under the scheme
during last 5 and current financial year are given in the table below:
Sl. No. Financial Year Fund Released (Rs. In Number of
crore) beneficiaries
1. 2019-20 11.99 975
2. 2020-21 16.51 1471
3. 2021-22 23.30 1615
4. 2022-23 32.75 2135
5. 2023-24 24.55 2242
6. 2024-25 20.21 999
(as on 30.01.2025)
03.02.2025 131
2. Financial Assistance to Cultural organizations with National Presence:
To promote and support cultural organisations with national presence involved in
promotion of art and culture throughout the country, this grant is given to such
organisations that have a properly constituted managing body, registered in
India; having a pan-India character with national presence in its operation;
adequate working strength; and have spent 1 crore or more during 3 of the last 5
years on cultural activities. The quantum of grant under this scheme is Rs. 1 crore
which can be increased to Rs. 5 crore in exceptional cases. Details of year-wise
release of fund (alongwith funds released to R.K. Mission) along with number of
beneficiaries supported under the scheme are given in the table below:
Sl. No. Financial Year Number of Fund Released
1. 2019-20 beneficiaries (Rs. in C rores)

29.01.2025)
03.02.2025 132
Brief of Cultural Function and Production Grant
Objective: The objective of the scheme component is to provide financial
assistance to ‘not-for-profit’ organizations, NGOs, Societies, Trusts, Universities by
giving financial assistance for conduct of Festivals, Seminars, Function, Conference,
Research, Workshops, Exhibitions, Symposia, Production of Dance, Drama-
Theatre, Music etc. and small research projects on different aspects of Indian
Culture.
Quantum of Assistance: The quantum of financial assistance under the
scheme is restricted to 75% of the expenditure, subject to a maximum of Rs.
5.00 lakh per project. However, the Ministry in exceptional circumstances,
increase the assistance to any project of outstanding merit and relevance upto
Rs. 20.00 lakh with the approval of Hon’ble Minister of Culture. The Grant is
released in two instalments of 75% and 25%.

WELFARE AND DEVELOPMENT OF PERFORMING ARTISTS
*20. DR. T SUMATHY ALIAS THAMIZHACHI THANGAPANDIAN:
Will the MINISTER OF CULTURE be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has taken adequate measures for the welfare and
development of performing artists of traditional art, culture, dance, drama and
songs from Tamil Nadu and if so, the details thereof;
(b) the total funds sanctioned and released for the welfare of performing artists
from the said State during each of the last ten years; and
(c) the total number of artists who have gone abroad during the last ten years
under the cultural exchange programmes and other schemes of the Ministry?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI GAJENDRA
SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) Yes, Sir. The Ministry of Culture administers various Central Sector Schemes
under which Financial Assistance is provided to eligible cultural
organizations/individual artists working in the field of promotion and
development of performing arts including traditional art, dance, drama, music
03.02.2025 135
etc. across the country including the state of Tamil Nadu. The brief of these
schemes is given at STATEMENT– I
(b) Under these Central Sector Schemes, there is no provision for state wise
funds allocation. Instead, financial assistance is released directly to the
approved cultural organizations and individual artists. The year-wise, scheme-
wise funds released to the cultural organizations / individual artists under
various schemes in the state of Tamil Nadu during the last ten years are given
at STATEMENT-II.
(c) Under ‘Global Engagement Scheme’ of the Ministry, 263 Troupes with 2348
Artists have participated in ‘Festivals of India’ abroad during the last ten years.
Out of these, 1 Troupe with 3 Artists from Tamil Nadu was deputed to perform
in Festival of India held in Russia (2018). In addition, 2 Troupes with 13 Artists,
from Tamil Nadu, were deputed to perform in Freedom-70 events held in
Czech Republic (2017) and Spain (2017).
STATEMENT – I
Details of various central sector schemes being administered by the
Ministry of Culture under which Financial Assistance is provided to eligible
cultural organizations/individual artists working in the field of promotion
and development of performing arts including traditional art, dance, drama,
music etc. across the country including the state of Tamil Nadu
1. FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE FOR PROMOTION OF GURU-SHISHYA
PARAMPARA (REPERTORY GRANT)
The objective of this scheme component is to provide financial support for all
genres of performing arts activities like dramatic groups, theatre groups, music
03.02.2025 136
ensembles, children theatre etc. for imparting training to artists by their respective
Guru on regular basis in line with Guru–Shishya Parampara. As per the scheme,
support is provided to 1 Guru and maximum 18 Shishyas in the field of theatre
and 1 Guru and maximum 10 Shishyas in the field of music and dance.
The Amount of Assistance for Guru is Rs.15000/- per month and for Shishya, the
same is Rs.2000-10000/-per month depending upon the age of artist.
2. FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE FOR PROMOTION OF ART AND CULTURE: This
scheme has following sub-components:
i. Financial Assistance to Cultural organizations with National Presence
To promote and support cultural organisations with national presence
involved in promotion of art and culture throughout the country, this grant is
given to such organisations that have a properly constituted managing body,
registered in India; having a pan-India character with national presence in its
operation; adequate working strength; and have spent 1 crore or more during
any 3 of the last 5 years on cultural activities. The quantum of grant under this
scheme is upto Rs. 1 crore which can be increased upto Rs. 5 crore in
exceptional cases.
ii. Cultural Function and Production Grant (CFPG)
The objective of this scheme component is to provide financial support to
NGOs/ Societies/ Trusts/ Universities etc. for Seminars, Conference,
Research, Workshops, Festivals, Exhibitions, Symposia, Production of Dance,
Drama-Theatre, Music etc. The maximum grants provided under CFPG is
Rs.5 Lakh for an organization which can be increased to Rs. 20.00 lakhs in
exceptional cases
03.02.2025 137
iii Financial Assistance for the Preservation and Development of Cultural
Heritage of the Himalayas
The objective of this scheme component is to promote and preserve the
cultural heritage of the Himalayas through research, training and dissemination
through audio visual programmes. The financial support is provided to the
organizations in the States falling under the Himalayan Region i.e. Jammu and
Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, Uttarakhand, Sikkim and Arunachal Pradesh.
The quantum of funding is upto Rs.10.00 lakhs per year for an organization
which can be increased upto Rs.30.00 lakhs in exceptional cases.
iv Financial Assistance for the Preservation and Development of
Buddhist/Tibetan Organization
Under this scheme component financial assistance is provided to the
voluntary Buddhist/Tibetan organizations including Monasteries engaged in
the propagation and scientific development of Buddhist/Tibetan Cultural and
tradition and research in related fields. The quantum of funding is upto Rs.
30.00 lakhs per year for an organization which can be increased upto 1.00
crore in exceptional cases
v. Financial Assistance for Building Grants including Studio Theatres
The objective of this scheme component is to provide financial support to
NGO, Trust, Societies, Govt. Sponsored bodies, University, College etc. for
creation of Cultural infrastructure (i.e. studio theatre, auditorium, rehearsal hall,
classroom etc.) and provision of facilities like electrical, air conditioning,
acoustics, light and sound systems etc. Under this scheme component, the
03.02.2025 138
maximum amount of grant is Rs.50 Lakh in metro cities and Rs.25 Lakh in non-
metro cities.
vi. Domestic Festivals and Fairs
The objective of this scheme is to provide assistance for holding the
‘Rashtriya Sanskriti Mahotsavs’ organized by Ministry of Culture. Rashtriya
Sanskriti Mahotsavs (RSMs) are conducted through Zonal Cultural Centres
(ZCCs) where a large number of artists from all over the country are engaged to
showcase their talents. From November, 2015 onwards, fourteen (14) RSMs
have been organized by Ministry of Culture in the country. During last three years,
Rs. 38.67 Cr. has been released under this scheme.
3. FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE FOR CONSTRUCTION OF TAGORE CULTURAL
COMPLEXES (TCC)
The aim of the scheme component is to provide financial support to NGO,
Trust, Societies, Govt. Sponsored bodies, State / UT Govt. University, Central/State
Govt. agencies/bodies, Municipal Corporations, reputed not-for-profit organizations
etc. for creation of new Large Cultural Spaces such as Auditorium with facilities and
infrastructure for stage performances (dance, drama and music), exhibitions,
seminars, literary activities, green rooms, etc. The Scheme component also
provides support for restoration, renovation, extension, alteration, up-gradation,
modernization of existing cultural facilities (Rabindra Bhawans, Rangshalas)
etc. Under this scheme component, the financial assistance for any project will
normally be up to a maximum of Rs. 15.00 crore. Central financial assistance would
be 90% of the total approved project cost and remaining 10% of the total approved
project cost will be borne by the recipient State Govt./NGO or concerned
03.02.2025 139
organization for NER Projects and except NER, there is 60:40 ratio for central
assistant and state share (matching share).
4. SCHEME OF SCHOLARSHIP AND FELLOWSHIP FOR PROMOTION OF ART
AND CULTURE: The scheme consists of following three components:
i. Scheme for the Award of Fellowship to Outstanding Persons in the
field of Culture
Upto 400 fellowships (200 Junior and 200 Senior) are awarded in a batch
year to outstanding persons in the age group of 25 to 40 years (Jr.) and above
40 years (Sr.) in different cultural fields @ of Rs. 10,000/- p.m. and 20,000/-
p.m. for the period of 2 years for cultural research. The Fellowship is released
in four equal six-monthly instalments.
ii. Scheme for Scholarships to Young Artistes in Different Cultural
Fields
Upto 400 Scholarships are awarded in a batch year. Under this scheme
financial assistance is given to young artistes of outstanding promise in the age
group of 18-25 years for advanced training within India in the field of Indian
Classical Music, Indian Classical Dance, Theatre, Mime, Visual art, Folk,
Traditional and Indigenous Arts and Light Classical Music etc. @ Rs. 5,000/-
p.m. for 2 years. The Scholarship is released in four equal six-monthly
instalments.
iii. Tagore National Fellowship for Cultural Research
The purpose of the Scheme Component is to invigorate and revitalize
the various institutions under the Ministry of Culture (MoC) and other identified
cultural institutions in the country, by encouraging scholars/ academicians to
affiliate themselves with these institutions to work on projects of mutual interest.
03.02.2025 140
Upto 15 Fellowships (Rs.80,000/- p.m. + Contingency Allowance) and 25
Scholarships (Rs.50,000/- p.m. + Contingency Allowance) for a maximum
period of 2 Years. The Fellowship is released in four equal six monthly
installments.
5. FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE FOR VETERAN ARTISTS
The objective of this Scheme is to provide financial assistance maximum upto
Rs.6.000/- p.m. to the old artistes and scholars aged 60 years above having annual
income not exceeding Rs.72,000/- who have contributed significantly in their
specialized fields of arts, letters etc. In the event of death of the beneficiary, the
financial assistance is transferred to his/her spouse.

KHELO INDIA CENTRES IN TAMIL NADU
2. SHRI MALAIYARASAN D:
Will the Minister of YOUTH AFFAIRS AND SPORTS be pleased to state:
(a) the total number of Khelo India Centres established under the 'Khelo India
Scheme' within Tamil Nadu;
(b) the number of youth athletes identified and provided with training and financial
assistance under the said Scheme within said State during the last three years;
(c) the total amount of financial assistance allocated under the said Scheme to the
said State so far;
(d) the details of the progress made in the implementation of the Khelo India
University Games and Khelo India School Games, including the number of
participants and the regions involved;
(e) the steps being taken by the Government to improve sports infrastructure in rural
and underserved areas, particularly through the said Scheme, and the number of
new facilities established in these regions; and
(f) the measures being taken to ensure to provide comprehensive training in both
traditional and modern sports?
THE MINISTER OF LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT; AND MINISTER OF YOUTH
AFFAIRS AND SPORTS (DR. MANSUKH MANDAVIYA):
(a) and (b) The details of total number of Khelo India Centres established and
number of youth athletes identified and provided with training and financial
assistance across the country, including in the state of Tamil Nadu, are available in
public domain on the dashboard of the Ministry at
https://dashboard.kheloindia.gov.in/state-wise-khelo-india-centers.
03.02.2025 144
(c) Funds are allocated Scheme-wise and not State-wise in this Ministry. Details of
funds allocated under the Khelo India Scheme, including the State of Tamil Nadu,
during the last three years are as under:
(in ₹ crore)
S. No. Financial Year Funds allocated
1 2021-22 869.00
2 2022-23 600.00
3 2023-24 880.00
(d) Under the “Sports Competition and Talent Search” component of the Khelo India
Scheme, six editions of Khelo India School/Youth Games and four editions of Khelo
India University Games have been conducted so far. Details such as number of
participants and the regions involved in these games, are at STATEMENT.
(e) Under the “Creation and upgradation of sports infrastructure” component of the
Khelo India Scheme, this Ministry renders financial support for creation of basic
sports infrastructure such as sports complex, synthetic athletic tracks, synthetic
hockey fields, synthetic turf football grounds, multipurpose halls, swimming pools,
etc. along with sports equipment. The details of the sports infrastructure sanctioned
under Khelo India Scheme, across the country, including in rural and underserved
areas, are available in public domain on the dashboard of the Ministry at
https://mdsd.kheloindia.gov.in.
(f) Under the Khelo India Scheme, 33 Khelo India State Centres of Excellence and
1046 Khelo India Centres have been notified and 302 sports academies have been
accredited to provide comprehensive training in both traditional and modern sports.

(ग) सड़क प(cid:229)रवहन और राजमाग(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ालय (एमओआरटीएच) ने िदनांक 04.10.2021 के सा.का.िन.

इसके अलावा, सड़क प(cid:229)रवहन एवं राजमाग(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ालय ने क(cid:164) (cid:254)ीय मोटर वाहन (चौबीसवां संशोधन) िनयम,

िकया है।

इसके अित(cid:229)र(cid:265), सड़क प(cid:229)रवहन एवं राजमाग(cid:170) म(cid:253)ं ालय ने िव(cid:299)तु वाहन(cid:332) और पया(cid:170)वरण अनुकू ल िवक(cid:208)प(cid:332)
को अपनाने को बढ़ावा देने के िलए िन(cid:204)निलिखत अिधसूचनाए ं या परामिश(cid:170)काए ं जारी क(cid:236) ह(cid:167):

आव(cid:212)यकताओ ंसे छूट दी गई।

उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य से शु(cid:208)क के भगु तान से छूट दी गई है।

4. सा.का.िन. 749 (अ) िदनांक 7.8.2018 के तहत प(cid:229)रवहन वाहन(cid:332) के िलए बैटरी चािलत

हरे रगं क(cid:236) प(cid:311)ृ भूिम पर सफे द रगं म(cid:164) जारी िकया जाना है।
इसके अलावा, भारी उ(cid:299)ोग मं(cid:253)ालय ने देश म(cid:164) िव(cid:299)तु प(cid:229)रवहन को बढ़ावा देने के िलए िदनांक 29 िसतंबर,

DISCONTINUANCE OF GRANTS TO THEATRE GROUPS
4. SHRI DEEPAK ADHIKARI (DEV):
Will the MINISTER OF CULTURE be pleased to state:
(a) whether it is true that the Government has discontinued/likely to discontinue
matching and production grant of some theatre groups; and
(b) if so, the reasons therefor along with the details of the grant discontinued to the
theatre groups during each of the last two years, State-wise?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI GAJENDRA
SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) and (b) No sir. Ministry of Culture is administering two schemes namely ‘Cultural
Function and Production Grant’ and ‘Financial Assistance to Cultural
Organizations with National Presence’ which envisages staging of production,
festivals etc. in different genres of arts and culture including theatre. Under these
schemes, applications are invited yearly for fresh production, festival from
cultural organizations for placing the same before the duly constituted Expert
Committee. The Expert Committee evaluates these applications and
03.02.2025 152
recommends financial assistance based on their cultural profile, quality of
proposal and submission of other requisite mandatory documents by these
organizations as laid down in the guidelines of these schemes. During the last
3 financial years, following number of organizations working in the field of theatre
have been recommended financial assistance under the said schemes:-

No. 2021-22 2022-23 2023-24
1. Cultural Function and Production 889 868 700
Grant (CFPG)
2. Financial Assistance to Cultural 02 07 04
Organizations with National
Presence

(कौशल, पनु ः कौशल और कौशलो(cid:198)नयन) (cid:255)दान करता है, िजसम(cid:164) छ(cid:176)ीसगढ़ के रायगढ़, जशपुर और
सारगं ढ़ िजले भी शािमल ह(cid:167)। इन (cid:255)मुख योजनाओ ं के अलावा, एमएसडीई पीएम िव(cid:309)कमा(cid:170) योजना के
कौशल घटक को भी काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत कर रहा है। कुशल भारत िमशन का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य भारत के यवु ाओ ंको भिव(cid:213)य
के िलए तैयार और उ(cid:299)ोग के िलए तैयार कौशल (cid:255)ा(cid:302) करने म(cid:164) स(cid:177)म बनाना है।
(i) पीएमके वीवाई के तहत, देश भर म(cid:164) (cid:248)मशः 15-45 वष(cid:170) और 18-59 वष(cid:170) आयु वग(cid:170) के यवु ाओ ं को
अ(cid:208)पकािलक (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण (एसटीटी) के मा(cid:197)यम से कौशल िवकास (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण और पूव(cid:170) िश(cid:177)ण क(cid:236) मा(cid:198)यता
(आरपीएल) के मा(cid:197)यम से कौशलो(cid:198)नयन और पुनः कौशल (cid:255)दान क(cid:236) जाती है, िजसका उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य युवाओ ं
को उ(cid:299)ोग से संबंिधत कौशल य(cid:265)ु करके उनक(cid:236) रोजगार (cid:177)मता को बढ़ाना है। पीएमके वीवाई योजना के
तहत, योजना के पहले तीन सं(cid:214)करण(cid:332) म(cid:164) अ(cid:208)पकािलक (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण (एसटीटी) घटक म(cid:164) (cid:200)लेसम(cid:164)ट को (cid:367)ैक
िकया गया था, जो िक पीएमके वीवाई 1.0, पीएमके वीवाई 2.0 और पीएमके वीवाई 3.0 ह(cid:167), िज(cid:198)ह(cid:164) िव(cid:176) वष(cid:170)
2015-16 से िव(cid:176) वष(cid:170) 2021-22 तक लागू िकया गया है। पीएमके वीवाई 4.0 के तहत, हमारे (cid:255)िशि(cid:177)त
उ(cid:204)मीदवार(cid:332) को अपने िविवध क(cid:229)रयर पथ चुनने के िलए सश(cid:265) बनाने पर (cid:197)यान क(cid:164) ि(cid:254)त िकया गया है और
वे इसके िलए उपयु(cid:265) (cid:322)प से उ(cid:198)मुख ह(cid:167)। वष(cid:170) 2015-16 से 2024-25 (31.12.2024 तक) तक छ(cid:176)ीसगढ़
रा(cid:186)य म(cid:164) पीएमके वीवाई के तहत (cid:255)िशि(cid:177)त उ(cid:204)मीदवार(cid:332) क(cid:236) कुल सं(cid:180)या 2,02,882 है। (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण क(cid:164) (cid:254)(cid:332) क(cid:236)
स(cid:180)ं या, (cid:255)िशि(cid:177)त/उ(cid:198)मुख, (cid:255)मािणत और िनयोिजत उ(cid:204)मीदवार(cid:332) क(cid:236) सं(cid:180)या का िजलावार (cid:202) यौरा िववरण -
I म(cid:164) िदया गया है।
(ii) जेएसएस योजना का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य भारत सरकार से 100 (cid:255)ितशत अनुदान के साथ पजं ीकृत सोसायिटय(cid:332)

आय म(cid:164) विृ (cid:294) करना है। जेएसएस का म(cid:180)ु य ल(cid:224)य 15-45 वष(cid:170) क(cid:236) आय ु के गैर-सा(cid:177)र, नव-सा(cid:177)र और 8व(cid:233)

जनजाितय(cid:332) (एसटी), अ(cid:198)य िपछड़ा वग(cid:170) (ओबीसी), अ(cid:208)पस(cid:180)ं यक(cid:332) और समाज के अ(cid:198)य वंिचत समूह(cid:332) को

IMPLEMENTATION OF NEP 2024
7. SHRI KODIKUNNIL SURESH:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) the current status of the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP)
2024 across the country, including timelines for key phases of rollout at school and
higher education levels;
(b) the primary focus areas prioritized in the initial stages of policy implementation,
especially with respect to skill development, multilingual education and digital
learning infrastructure;
(c) the steps being taken by the Government to ensure smooth integration of policy
guidelines into State education system and whether State Governments and
stakeholders have been consulted in this regard;
(d) the allocation of funds and resources dedicated to policy for current financial year
along with provisions for teacher training, curriculum development and infrastructure
upgrades; and
03.02.2025 166
(e) the specific challenges identified during the early stages of implementation and
the measures being taken to address them?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF EDUCATION; AND MINISTER
OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF DEVELOPMENT OF NORTH EASTERN
REGION. (DR. SUKANTA MAJUMDAR):
(a) to (e) A number of transformative changes have taken place both in school and
higher education after announcement of National Education Policy (NEP) 2020. In
School Education several initiatives have been taken such as PM SHRI (PM
Schools for Rising India) for upgradation of schools; Samagra Shiksha for providing
quality education with an inclusive and equitable classroom environment for all
children; National Initiative for Proficiency in Reading with Understanding and
Numeracy (NIPUN Bharat) for ensuring foundational literacy and numeracy by the
end of Grade 3; Vidya-Pravesh–Guidelines for Three-month Play-based School
Preparation Module; PM e-VIDYA to unify all efforts related to digital/online/on-air
education to enable coherent multi-mode access to education, DIKSHA (Digital
Infrastructure for Knowledge Sharing) as One Nation One Digital Platform having e-
Books and e-Contents, launch of National Curriculum Framework for Foundational
Stage (NCF FS) and Jadui Pitara for play-based learning teaching material tailored
for children between the age group of 3 to 8 years; PARAKH (Performance
Assessment, Review, and Analysis of Knowledge for Holistic Development);
NISHTHA (National Initiative for School Heads' and Teachers' Holistic
Advancement) 1.0, 2.0 and 3.0; Vidya Samiksha Kendra; Integrated Teacher
Education Programme; National Professional Standards for Teachers (NPST);
National Digital Education Architecture (NDEAR) for creating a unifying national
03.02.2025 167
digital infrastructure to energise and catalyse the education ecosystem,
implementation of a scheme “New India Literacy Programme or ULLAS” targeting
all non-literates age 15 years and above, etc.
In Higher Education, various initiatives / reforms have been carried out
such as National Credit Framework (NCrF) and National Higher Education
Qualification Framework (NHEQF) in conjunction with guidelines / regulations like
Curriculum and Credit Framework for Undergraduate Programme; Multiple Entry
and Exit in Academic Programme offered by Higher Education Institutions;
Transforming HEIs into multi-disciplinary Institutions; pursuing two Academic
Programme simultaneously; Automated Permanent Academic Account Registry
(APAAR ID) of individual student which will act as lifelong identity to track their
educational journey and achievements from, pre-primary to higher education;
introduction of PM-Vidyalaxmi scheme to provide financial support to meritorious
students inter-alia enabling collateral free, guarantor free loan through a simple,
transparent and student-friendly and entirely digital application process; revised
regulation of ODL / Online Education; permitting up to 40% credits in regular courses
using SWAYAM platform; launch of new SWAYAM Plus portal with aim to enhance
skill and upskilling and reskilling of existing manpower employed in different sectors;
Integration of technology in administration of HEIs from admission to grant of degree
through SAMARTH; guidelines on Professor of Practice to enable HEIs to work with
industry experts; guidelines for supernumerary seats for admitting students from
abroad in Indian HEIs and permitting HEIs for two admission cycles in an academic
year; enhanced reputation of the Indian HEIs in international ranking; embedding of
Indian Knowledge system in the education etc.
03.02.2025 168
To create awareness and discuss innovative ideas for NEP implementation, a series
of workshops / consultation-cum-review meetings have been held with States/UTs,
Educational Institutions, other stakeholders from time to time. Implementation of
NEP 2020 was discussed in National Education Ministers' Conference held in
Gujarat in June 2022; National Conference of Chief Secretaries held in June 2022;
7th Governing Council Meeting of NITI Aayog held in August 2022; Akhil Bharatiya
Shiksha Samagam 2022, 2023 and 2024; 9th Governing Council Meeting of NITI
Aayog held on 27th July 2024. A two-day National Workshop on Higher and
Technical Education was organized with Secretaries of States/UTs on 12th and 13th
November 2024 to disseminate various approaches and methodologies to
implement NEP 2020; effectively articulate the roadmap and implementation
strategies, fostering knowledge exchange; provide a common platform for all
stakeholders to come together and network for effective and smooth implementation
of the NEP 2020 and encourage its adoption in state institutions.
For Outreach and Awareness of Implementation of NEP 2020, a program involving
84 Centrally Funded Higher Education Institutions (CFIs) and 404 State Public
Universities, each CFI mapped with the 5-7 State Universities located in the nearby
geographies, have been started.
Various schemes/projects/programmes run by Ministry of Education have been
aligned with NEP 2020. As far as Central Government is concerned, Budget
allocation from the year 2021-22 to 2024-25 is given below:-
03.02.2025 169
Year 2021-22 2022-23 2023-24 2024-25
Budget 93,224.31 1,04,277.72 1,12,899.47 1,21,117.77
Allocation (Rs.in
Crore)
A National Mission to improve learning outcomes at the elementary level through an
Integrated Teacher Training Programme called NISHTHA-National Initiative for
School Heads’ and Teachers’ Holistic Advancement has been launched under the
Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Samgara Shiksha in 2019-20. NISHTHA is a
capacity building programme for teachers at all levels in Schools for “Improving
Quality of School Education through Integrated Teacher Training”.
Also, in Higher Education, in order to address issues related to teachers/faculty and
their professional development, Malaviya Mission Teacher Training Program has
been launched. It covers several components such as NEP Orientation and
Sensitization Programme; Faculty Induction Program; Short Term Program;
Refresher Courses; Nurturing Future Leadership Program; Capacity Building
Program on Specific Learning Disabilities; Capacity Building program on the themes
like Design and Entrepreneurship, Development of Leadership, Promoting Positive
Mental Health Resilience and Wellbeing in HEIs, Sensitisation about Specific
learning Disabilities, Cyber Security, Artificial Intelligence, STEM (Science,
Technology, Engineering and Mathematics) Subjects, Science, Communication etc.
Third phase of the Rashitrya Uchchatar Shiksha Abhiyan (RUSA) as Pradhan Mantri
Uchchatar Shiksha Abhiyan (PM-USHA), a centrally sponsored scheme aimed at
03.02.2025 170
funding of specific State Government universities and colleges, so as to improve
their quality has been launched in July 2023 with an outlay of Rs. 12926.10 crore for
the period 2023-24 to 2025-26.
NEP 2020 provides different timelines as well as principles and methodology for its
implementation. It also provides that in the decade of 2030-40, the entire policy will
be in an operational mode, following which another comprehensive review will be
undertaken. Education being in the concurrent list of Constitution, Centre and States
are equally responsible for providing quality education to all. Accordingly, Ministry of
Education, State Governments, education-related Ministries, the regulatory and
implementing bodies of school and higher education such as University Grants
Commission, All India Council for Technical Education, National Council for Teacher
Education, National Council of Educational Research and Training, Central Board of
Secondary Education, Universities/Colleges/Schools etc. have started taking
initiatives for implementation of NEP 2020.
CSR FUND FOR CONSERVATION AND MAINTENANCE WORK
8. SHRI Y S AVINASH REDDY:
Will the Minister of CULTURE be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has been receiving the proposals for release of CSR Funds
for conservation and maintenance work of historical monuments in the country;
(b) if so, the details thereof including Andhra Pradesh; and
03.02.2025 171
(c) the action plan formulated for conserving and maintaining the monuments and other
historical sites in Andhra Pradesh along with the funds allocated and released so
far?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI GAJENDRA
SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) and (b) Yes, Sir. Government has received Corporate Social Responsibility
(CSR) funding through National Culture Fund (NCF) for conservation and
maintenance work of historical monuments in the country. NCF has received Rs.
1861.60 Lakh during the last three years. However, no CSR fund has been
received/spent through NCF in Andhra Pradesh during the last three years. The
details of projects undertaken through NCF in last three years are given in
STATEMENT.
Further, Adopt A Heritage Program 2.0 has been launched by the Ministry of Culture
exclusively for protected monuments of national importance to create a framework for
engaging with private/public sector companies and NGOs/Trusts/Societies, etc. to
develop/provide amenities through CSR funds to enhance visitors’ experience. As of
date, a total of 21 MoUs have been executed across various states. However, no MoU
has been signed for any monument from Andhra Pradesh.
(c) Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) takes up conservation and maintenance work of
centrally protected monuments/sites in the country, including state of Andhra Pradesh,
as per requirement and availability of resources. The detail of funds allocated for
conservation and maintenance of monuments/sites in the state of Andhra Pradesh during
last three years is given as under:

INCREASING OF MAXIMUM WORKING HOURS
9. SHRI RAJMOHAN UNNITHAN:
Will the Minister of LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has taken note of the recent proposals by corporate
leaders to increase the maximum working hours to 70 or 90 hours a week and if
so, the details thereof along with the Government's stance on it;
(b) the measures being considered to protect workers' rights in this context; and
(c) the details of assessment by the Government for the potential impact of extended
working hours on workers' physical and mental health and the manner in which to
ensure that the workers' well-being is prioritized?
03.02.2025 175
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM
ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF LABOUR AND
EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a) to (c) Labour being a subject under the Concurrent List, the enforcement of labour
laws is done by the State Governments and the Central Government in their
respective jurisdictions. While in the Central sphere, the enforcement is done
through the inspecting officers of the Central Industrial Relations Machinery (CIRM),
the compliance in the state sphere is ensured through the state’s labour enforcement
machinery.
As per the existing labour laws, working conditions including working
hours and overtime etc. are regulated through the provisions of the Factories Act,
1948 and the Shops and Establishments Acts of the respective State Governments.
Most of the establishments, including the corporate sector, are governed by the
Shops and Establishments Act, for which the appropriate government is the State
Government.
No such proposal to increase the maximum working hours to 70 or 90 hours a week
is under consideration of Government.
TEACHER TRAINING PROGRAMMES UNDER SAMAGRA SHIKSHA ABHIYAN
10. SHRI A MANI:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the types of training programmes available for teachers under the
Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan and the measures followed by the Government to
ensure that teachers are equipped with modern teaching methodologies;
03.02.2025 176
(b) the details of the number of teachers trained in the various programmes and
its impact on teaching quality;
(c) the content and methodologies used in the teacher training programmes under
the Abhiyan;
(d) the measures being taken by the Government to ensure that teachers in rural
and remote areas have access to quality training programmes under the
Abhiyan;
(e) the budget allocated for teacher training programmes under the Abhiyan and
the increase in funding for these programmes over the years; and
(f) whether the Government has conducted any assessments on the impact of
these training programmes on teaching quality and student learning outcomes?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDARY):
(a) to (e) Strengthening of teacher training/education programmes is supported
under the centrally sponsored scheme of Samagra Shiksha in alignment with the
recommendations of the National Education Policy (NEP), 2020. The interventions
under Samagra Shiksha are aimed at strengthening of physical infrastructure in
Teacher Education Institutions (TEIs); support to State Councils of Educational
Research and Trainings (SCERTs) so that they can effectively and efficiently carry
out quality teacher education, research and develop state-specific curriculum
framework, as a follow up to the National Curriculum Framework for School
Education (NCF SE); capacity building of District Institute of Education and Training
(DIET) functionaries; continuous academic support to schools through BRC/CRC
etc.
03.02.2025 177
Financial provision is provided under this scheme as per state specific proposals
for in-service training of teachers, school Heads, subject teachers, resource
persons, master trainers, Education Administrators, Teachers Educators, key
resource persons, induction training of newly recruited teachers including those in
rural and remote areas at elementary and secondary levels through SCERTs and
DIETs, as the nodal agencies.
The details of teacher training allocated under Samagra Shiksha in the last three
years are available in STATEMENT at https://www.education.gov.in/parl_ques.
The Department of School Education and Literacy has also launched a National
Mission to improve learning outcomes at the elementary level through an
Integrated Teacher Training Programme called NISHTHA – National Initiative for
School Heads’ and Teachers’ Holistic Advancement under the Centrally
Sponsored Scheme of Samagra Shiksha. NISHTHA is a capacity building
programme for "Improving Quality of School Education through Integrated
Teacher Training". NISHTHA online was launched using DIKSHA platform in
October, 2020. NISHTHA training has been extended to secondary stage teachers
in 2021-22. Subsequently, NISHTHA also extended to Foundational Literacy and
Numeracy and Early Childhood Care and Education (ECCE) for training of master
trainers.
The Government has decided to develop all functional 613 District Institutes of
Education and Training (DIETs) into Centers of Excellence under the centrally
sponsored scheme of Samagra Shiksha in a phased manner starting from FY
2023-24. Based on proposals submitted by States/UTs for the FY 2023-24, the
Government has sanctioned a financial assistance of Rs. 92,320.18 Lakhs for
03.02.2025 178
upgradation of 125 DIETs of 33 States/UTs out of which a total of Rs. 30,559.13
Lakhs have been released to 29 States/UTs as central share.
Further, in terms of NEP, 2020, the Government has released a Guiding Document
on the National Professional Standards for Teachers (NPST) and Bluebook on the
National Mission for Mentoring (NMM). NPST defines competencies of teachers
at different stages/levels. The Bluebook on NMM contains the various modalities
for mentoring of schoolteachers for enhancing their capacity building.
(f) In order to diagnose learning gaps and determine interventions necessary in
policies, teaching practices and learning, the Government conducts National
Achievement Survey (NAS) in a cycle of three (03) years. NAS (now referred to as
PARAKH Rashtriya Sarvekshan) is a national level assessment about learning
achievements in schools and was recently conducted on 03rd December, 2024.

LACK OF FORMAL SERVICE RULES FOR FOREST RANGERS IN ASSAM
11. SHRI GAURAV GOGOI:
Will the Minister of ENVIRONMENT, FOREST AND CLIMATE CHANGE be pleased
to state:
03.02.2025 180
(a) whether the Government is aware that there are currently no formal service
rules governing the Forest Rangers in the State of Assam;
(b) if so, the steps proposed to be taken to formulate and implement formal
service rules for Forest Rangers in the said State, including the timeline
fixed for the same;
(c) the measures adopted to address the grievances of Forest Rangers in
Assam, especially concerning their service conditions, career progression
and other employment-related issues; and
(d) whether the Government has undertaken any consultations with relevant
stakeholders including Forest Ranger associations, to ensure that their
concerns are adequately addressed?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT, FOREST
AND CLIMATE CHANGE; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EXTERNAL AFFAIRS (SHRI KIRTI VARDHAN SINGH):
(a) to (d) As per the report received from the State Forest Department, Government
of Assam, a draft of the Service Rules for Forest Ranger has been
prepared and the process of finalization of the same is underway.
The concerns raised by the Forest Rangers regarding their service
conditions are suitably considered by the Department, while finalising the
draft Service Rules.
STATUS OF PM-SYM YOJANA
12. SHRI C N ANNADURAI:
SHRI G SELVAM:
SHRI NAVASKANI K:
Will the Minister of LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT be pleased to state:
03.02.2025 181
(a) the details of the original enrollment targets set for the Pradhan Mantri Shram
Yogi Maandhan (PM-SYM) Yojana and the details of strategies to achieve these
targets;
(b) the steps are being taken by the Government to ensure wider participation of
eligible workers in the unorganized sector;
(c) whether the Government has partnered with local bodies, NGOs or community
organizations to improve outreach for the Yojana and if so, the details thereof;
(d) the details of the monthly or annual contribution amount required from
beneficiaries under the PM-SYM Yojana for different age groups;
(e) the details of the total amount disbursed as pension benefits under the PM-SYM
Yojana so far;
(f) the manner in which the Government addressing the issue of low enrollment and
irregular contributions among informal sector workers;
(g) the details of the percentage of women beneficiaries under the PM-SYM Yojana;
and
(h) whether there are any specific measures to encourage women workers and
socially marginalized groups to enroll in the Yojana and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM
ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF LABOUR AND
EMPLOYMENT. (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a) to (h) The Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM-SYM) scheme was
launched in February, 2019 in order to provide old age protection to the workers of
unorganised sector. This is a voluntary and contributory pension scheme. Under the
scheme, a monthly minimum assured pension of Rs. 3000/- is provided to the
unorganised workers after attaining the age of 60 years. The workers in the age
03.02.2025 182
group of 18-40 years whose monthly income is Rs. 15000/- or less and not a member
of EPFO/ESIC/NPS (Govt. funded) and not an income tax payer are eligible to join
the scheme. The monthly contribution by the beneficiary ranges from Rs. 55/- to Rs.
200/- depending upon the entry age of the beneficiary. Under the scheme, equal
matching contribution is paid by the Central Government. Enrolment to the Scheme
is done through the Common Service Centres, with its network of about 4 lakh
Centres across the country. In addition, eligible unorganised workers can also self-
enroll through visiting the portal www.maandhan.in.
As the pension to the beneficiaries will start after attaining the age of 60 years, the
disbursement of pension will start from 2039.
The enrollment of women beneficiaries under the PM-SYM Yojana is about 53
percent.
In order to address the issue of enrolment and irregular contributions, the
Government inter-alia has allowed extension of revival of dormant accounts from 1
year to 3 years, launched Donate-a-Pension Module to encourage the employer to
pay the premium of their staff under PM-SYM pension scheme and increase the
enrolment.
The Government has taken following steps to register more unorganized workers in
the PM-SYM scheme:-
(i) holding periodic review meeting with States/ UTs.
(ii) regular meeting with state Common Services Centre (CSC) heads.
(iii) launch of new features such as Voluntary Exit, Revival Module, Claim Status and
Account Statement.
03.02.2025 183
(iv)Two-way integration of PM-SYM and eShram.
(v) SMS campaign to create awareness.
(vi) communication with Chief Secretaries of States/UTs regarding enrolment under
PM-SYM scheme.
(vii) Interaction with Department of Financial Services, Pension Fund Regulatory and
Development Authority, National Institute of Public Finance and Policy to increase
the outreach of the pension scheme.
APPLICANTS ELIGIBLE AND APPLICATIONS RECEIVED UNDER PMIS
13. SHRI BASTIPATI NAGARAJU:
SHRI ABHISHEK BANERJEE:
SHRI TARIQ ANWAR
SHRI BALASHOWRY VALLABHANENI
SUSHRI MAHUA MOITRA
Will the Minister of CORPORATE AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) The total number of applications received and accepted under the PM Internship
Scheme since its inception along with the number of people enrolled under the
scheme, State-wise including Bihar and district-wise details of Andhra Pradesh.
(b) the number of companies empanelled under the scheme to offer internships and
the sector-wise breakdown of these companies along with the details of the
companies in which internships are being provided to the selected applicants;
(c) the number of internships provided by each company under the scheme;
(d) the details of the selection process and the time by which the selection is likely
to be completed:
(e) whether the persons who have been selected have started the internship and if
so, the details thereof;
03.02.2025 184
(f) whether the Government aims to provide internships to 1 crore individuals in a
year and if so, the details thereof; and
(g) the steps taken by the Government for the effective implementation of the PM
Internship Scheme, 2024 to bridge the skill gap in the corporate sector?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CORPORATE AFFAIRS; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND
HIGHWAYS (SHRI HARSH MALHOTRA)
(a) to (e) The Prime Minister’s Internship Scheme (PMIS) was announced in the
Budget 2024-25. It aims to provide internship opportunities to one crore youth in
top 500 companies in five years. As an initiation to this Scheme, Ministry of
Corporate Affairs has launched a Pilot Project of the Scheme on 3rd October,
2024, which is targeted to provide 1.25 lakh internship opportunities to the youth
in the Financial Year 2024-25. The Scheme is being implemented through an
online portal https://pminternship.mca.gov.in.
In the round I of the PM Internship Scheme Pilot Project, the partner companies
have posted more than 1.27 lakh internship opportunities on the Portal across the
country. Against this, more than 6.21 lakh applications were received from about
1.81 lakh candidates. Based on the requirements sought by the Companies in the
internship opportunities offered and the qualifications of the applicants, in the first
round of the Pilot Project, partner companies have made 82,077 internship offers
to 60,866 candidates, out of which 28,141 candidates have accepted offers to join
the internship, as on 29th January, 2025.
In the round I, approximately 3.38 lakh candidates have completed their profiles
on the Portal. State wise list of candidates who have completed their profiles on
the PMIS Portal is enclosed at STATEMENT-I. District wise details of candidate
03.02.2025 185
who have completed their profiles on the portal from different districts of Andhra
Pradesh is attached at STATEMENT-II.
The second round of the PM Internship Scheme Pilot Project has started from
9th January, 2025, and the companies are in the process of posting new as well
as editing unfilled internship opportunities.
The list of the top 500 companies selected on the basis of average Corporate
Social Responsibility (CSR) expenditure in the last 3 years, covers a large number
of diverse sectors including Aviation and Defence, Automotive, Banking and
Financial Services, Chemical Industry, Oil, Gas and Energy etc. Apart from these,
any other company/ bank/ financial institution desirous of participating in the
Scheme, may do so with the approval of the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA),
which would take a view keeping in view underrepresented sectors and areas in
the above mentioned 500 companies. The list of the Partner Companies is
available at https://pminternship.mca.gov.in. Participation of the companies in this
scheme is voluntary. Details of the companies which have posted internship
opportunities in the Round I of the PM Internship Scheme Pilot Project are
provided at STATEMENT-III.
As per the Guidelines of the PM Internship Scheme- Pilot Project, a pool of
candidates based on the number of applications received from all parts of the
country, is shortlisted for each internship opportunity through the Portal by
matching the qualifications of the applicants with the qualifications sought by the
company for that internship opportunity. This shortlist is sent to the concerned
company for further selection as per their respective processes and criterion.
Further, it is stated that the timelines for the various stages of PMIS
implementation vary across different rounds.
03.02.2025 186
(f) and (g) The Prime Minister’s Internship Scheme (PMIS) announced in the Budget
2024-25 aims to provide internship opportunities to one crore youth in top 500
companies in five years. PM Internship Scheme provides an opportunity to the
youth to get training and gain experience and skills within the real-life environment
of various businesses or organizations with the intention of enhancing their
employability by bridging the gap between academic learning and industry
requirements. At least half of the internship period of 12 months is to be spent in
the actual working experience/ job environment. Further, Ministry of Corporate
Affairs is actively working with various stakeholders such as other central
government ministries and departments, state governments, industry
associations etc. for promotion and implementation of the scheme.
