NEW EDUCATION POLICY (NEP) 2020
63. DR. AMOL RAMSING KOLHE:
SHRI NILESH DNYANDEV LANKE:
SHRI DHAIRYASHEEL RAJSINH MOHITE PATIL:
SHRI AMAR SHARADRAO KALE:
SHRI BAJRANG MANOHAR SONWANE:
SHRI SANJAY DINA PATIL:
PROF. VARSHA EKNATH GAIKWAD:
SHRI BHASKAR MURLIDHAR BHAGARE:
SHRIMATI SUPRIYA SULE:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) the current status of the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP)
2020, across the country especially in Maharashtra;
03.02.2025 420
(b) whether the Government has set any timeline for the complete implementation
of NEP 2020;
(c) the impact of NEP in school education, particularly with respect to foundational
literacy and numeracy in the State of Maharashtra;
(d) the measures being taken by the Government to integrate vocational education
into the school curriculum as per the NEP 2020 guidelines;
(e) whether the Government has introduced any steps to implement a flexible and
multidisciplinary curriculum at the school level, if so, the details thereof;
(f) the steps being taken by the Government to implement reforms in teacher training
as per NEP 2020;
(g) the steps being taken by the Government to promote education in regional
languages and mother tongues as per NEP 2020; and
(h) the manner in which the NEP 2020 addressing issues of inclusion and equity,
particularly for marginalized and disadvantaged communities?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) A number of transformative changes have taken place both in school and
higher education after announcement of National Education Policy (NEP 2020). In
School Education several initiatives have been taken such as alignment of Samagra
Shiksha with the recommendation of the NEP; National Initiative for Proficiency in
Reading with Understanding and Numeracy (NIPUN Bharat) for ensuring
foundational literacy and numeracy by the end of Grade 2; Vidya-Pravesh
Guidelines for Three-month Play-based School Preparation Module; PM e-VIDYA to
unify all efforts related to digital/online/on-air education to enable coherent multi-
03.02.2025 421
mode access to education; DIKSHA (Digital Infrastructure for Knowledge Sharing)
as One Nation One Digital Platform having e-Books and e-Contents; launch of
National Curriculum Framework for Foundational Stage (NCF-FS) and Jadui Pitara
for play-based learning teaching material tailored for children between the age group
of 3 to 8 years; National Curriculum Framework for School Education (NCF-SE) to
address the entire educational journey uptil secondary stage. PARAKH
(Performance Assessment, Review, and Analysis of Knowledge for Holistic
Development); NISHTHA (National Initiative for School Heads' and Teachers'
Holistic Advancement) Elementary, Secondary, FLN (Foundational Literacy and
Numeracy) and ECCE (Early Childhood Care and Education); Vidya Samiksha
Kendra; Integrated Teacher Education Programme; National Professional
Standards for Teachers (NPST); National Digital Education Architecture (NDEAR)
for creating a unifying national digital infrastructure to energise and catalyse the
education ecosystem, implementation of a scheme New India Literacy Programme
or ULLAS (Understanding of Lifelong Learning for All in Society) targeting all
nonliterates age 15 years and above, etc.
Pradhan Mantri Schools for Rising India (PM SHRI) Scheme has been
launched with an aim to develop more than 14,500 selected schools as exemplar
schools that offer leadership to other schools in the neighbourhood, showcasing all
the initiatives of NEP 2020.
The current status of the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP),
2020, across states Maharashtra is as follows:
 A total of 209 tasks are for NEP implementation for Maharashtra
 Out of these total 209 tasks, 67 tasks have been completed at present
03.02.2025 422
 There are 74 tasks which are still in progress.
 There are 20 tasks on which work is yet to begin.
 There are 28 tasks that have been completed but will continue on a
continuous basis.
 2 tasks are policy tasks
(b) NEP 2020 provides different timelines as well as principles and methodology
for its implementation. It also envisions operationalization of the entire policy in the
decade of 2030-40, following which another comprehensive review will be
undertaken. Education being in the concurrent list of Constitution, Centre and States
are equally responsible for providing quality education to all. Accordingly, Ministry of
Education, State Governments, education-related Ministries, the regulatory and
implementing bodies of school and higher education such as University Grants
Commission, All India Council for Technical Education, National Council for Teacher
Education, National Council of Educational Research and Training, Central Board of
Secondary Education, Universities/Colleges/Schools etc. have taken several
initiatives for implementation of NEP 2020.
Further, Education is in the concurrent list of the Constitution and major
responsibility of implementation of NEP 2020 following prescribed norms and
standards falls under the domain of the States and UT Governments.
(c) The Scheme of Samagra Shiksha has been aligned with National Education
Policy, 2020 with focus on improving the quality of educationthrough various
measures such as introduction of new pedagogical and curricular structure
(5+3+3+4), Early Childhood Care and Education and Foundational Literacy and
Numeracy, among others and covers the entire continuum of Education from 3 years
03.02.2025 423
of Balvatika (pre school) to class 12. All the 36 States/UTs are implementing NIPUN
Bharat Mission.
National Initiative for Proficiency in Reading with Understanding and Numeracy
(NIPUN Bharat) has been launched under Samagra Shiksha for ensuring that every
child in the country necessarily attains foundational literacy and numeracy (FLN) by
the end of Grade 2. Guidelines for Three-month Play-based School Preparation
Module for Grade-I Children- Vidya Pravesh has been launched on 29th July, 2021.
All the 36 States/UTs are implementing Vidya Pravesh Programme.
Maharashtra issued Government resolution on 27 Oct. 2021 for smooth
implementation of mission. In the GR, all the targets have been informed to all stake
holders. According to this, State Programming Unit and Steering committee has
been constituted. All the districts have constituted DISTRICT Steering committee
and District PMU and they are working smoothly at field level. Revised lakshyas
have also been conveyed to related stakeholders. Academic task force and District
task force have been formed at state and district level.
 Nipunotsav program: Under this programme, to declare as a NIPUN Block
or NIPUN Cluster, parameters have been conveyed to all stakeholders.
Schools were instructed to organise different activities for FLN.
 Training of teachers: Teachers of Grades-I to V are being trained (including
new teachers) in order to ensure the requisite capacity to deliver high quality
instruction for the foundational stage. All 3,25,000 teachers (100 %) from
Govt. and aided schools for the Grade 1 to 5 have received training on
Foundational Literacy and Numeracy named “Adhyayan Prakriyeche
Vyavsthapn” (Management of Learning Process). This training was based on
03.02.2025 424
activity and technology based. Also refresher training was given to teachers
at cluster meetings.
 FLN Workbooks for the students: To strengthen Pedagogy and teaching
learning process, the TLMs for Grade 1 to 5 have been distributed to all Govt.
schools in the academic year 2022-23.
 FLN NISHTHA Training:
- Online orientation of the teachers and FLN NISHTHA training has been
given to all primary teachers with the help of DIKSHA Portal.
- The NISHTHA 3.0 training has been conducted during the period of 1st
January 2022 to 31st March 2022 and 1st April 2022 to 9th May 2022.
- Total 12 Modules were created under NISHTHA 3.0 program
- Total 2,39,172 trainees have taken this training by completing the 45-60
hours of training on DIKSHA portal for completing the NISHTHA 3.0 (FLN)
Training Program.
 National Conference on FLN: The state has been actively participating in
national conferences and seminars focused on FLN, sharing best practices
and learning from other regions to strengthen its educational strategies. For
instance, during the two-day National Conference on FLN held from June 17-
18, 2023, Maharashtra, along with other states and union territories,
showcased its initiatives aimed at improving teaching-learning approaches
and pedagogy in the context of multilingualism.
 Teachers Activity Handbook: Teacher supportive material in the form of
Teachers Activity Handbook has been developed and aligned with goals and
03.02.2025 425
Learning Outcomes (Los) of FLN for Languages and Maths and distributed to
the school.
 Posters: To create print rich environment, posters have been designed
in different languages for Language and Maths.
 Vidyapravesh Programme:
i. School preparation module of three months for Grade 1 students has
been implemented in 2022-23,2023-24 and 2024- 25 in the form of
Teacher Guidebook and Student activity book for smooth transaction of
the children from pre-schooling to Grade 1.
ii. A total of 65,689 government schools participated in this, and 7,21,291
students were enrolled.
NEP 2020 has significantly influenced school education in Maharashtra,
particularly concerning foundational literacy and numeracy. The state's
initiatives, aligned with national missions like NIPUN Bharat, reflect a
commitment to building a strong educational foundation for its young learners,
thereby contributing to their long-term academic and personal success.
(d) The Government has adopted several steps to make school education more
job-oriented and to increase the employable skill set of students. Under the
Vocational Education component of the Centrally Sponsored Scheme ‘Samagra
Shiksha’, financial assistance is given to States for offering National Skill
Qualification Framework (NSQF) compliant vocational courses to the students
from classes 9th to 12th in the schools covered under the scheme to develop
knowledge and practical skills required for gainful employment.
03.02.2025 426
At the Secondary level i.e., Class IX and X, skill modules are offered to the students
as an additional subject. At Sr. Secondary level, i.e., Class XI and XII, skill courses
are offered as a compulsory (elective) subject. So far 138 Job Roles (JRs) / Skill
subjects have been approved for implementation in States/UTs as per the Skill Gap
Analysis. Employability Skill modules including Communication Skills, Self-
Management Skills, Information and Communication Technology Skills,
Entrepreneurship Skills, and Green Skills are included in the curriculum of JRs.
Assistance for setting up Vocational or Skill labs in schools and DIETS, with state-
of-the-art facilities, is also provided. The existing scheme of Samagra Shiksha has
been revamped and various new interventions relating to Vocational Education like
internships, bagless days, exposure to vocational education at the Upper Primary
level etc. are being supported. The guidelines for 10 bagless days for students was
launched on the 4th Anniversary of NEP 2020.
In coordination with the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship, the
Department of School Education and Literacy (DoSEL) is implementing PMKVY 4.0
in the school education sector. Further, the Department of School Education and
Literacy in partnership with industry is enhancing the knowledge and exposure of
students in emerging technologies like Artificial Intelligence and Virtual Reality.
The National Curriculum Framework for School Education, developed in pursuance
of National Education Policy 2020, has laid down the objectives of approaches to
Vocational Education. One of the objectives is that vocational capacities,
knowledge, and relevant values will be developed for all students, and this will
create the possibility of them joining the workforce after school if they choose to.
03.02.2025 427
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 emphasises on the significance of
counselling in schools as a key component of the educational framework. The policy
advocates a holistic approach to career guidance, considering the individual needs,
interests, and strengths of each student. NEP 2020 also promotes the use of
technology and online resources to enhance career guidance services. DoSEL has
launched Career Guide book for School Students on the 4th Anniversary celebration
of NEP 2020. This Career Guide Book consists of 500 career cards in 21 domains.
Each career card contains work profile, essential Personal Traits, Course fee,
Scholarships, Loan prospects, Entry Pathway, Details about course providing
Institutions (Govt./Private/Online course) and example of pioneers in the field. A
copy of this book is available at https://dsel.education.gov.in/careers/index.html
Career counselling at Secondary and Senior Secondary schools is considered of
utmost importance which enables students to know the various career opportunities
available as per their choice, need and strength. The revamped scheme of Samagra
Shiksha provides for provisioning of one Academic Resource Person for career
counselling at each Block/ULB in the Block Resource Centre (BRC). DoSEL has
also finalised the Guidelines for Provisioning of Academic Resource Person for
career counselling in the BRCs under Samagra Shiksha which are available at
https://dsel.education.gov.in/sites/default/files/update/guidelines_brc_1707.pdf.
(e) Based on the principles of National Education Policy 2020, Ministry of
Education have released the National Curriculum Framework for Foundational
Studies (NCF-FS) and National Curriculum Framework for School Education (NCF-
SE), on 20th October, 2022 and 23rd August, 2023 respectively. NCF- FS and NCF-
SE has been placed in public domain at
03.02.2025 428
https://ncert.nic.in/pdf/NCF_for_Foundational_Stage_20_October_2022.pdf and
https://dsel.education.gov.in/sites/default/files/update/ncf_2023.pdf respectively.
(f) The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 states that "Teachers truly shape
the future of our children - and, therefore, the future of our nation. The motivation
and empowerment of teachers is required to ensure the best possible future for our
children and our nation''. NEP 2020 envisage that teachers are to be given
continuous opportunities for self-improvement and to learn the latest innovations and
advances in their professions. These are offered in multiple modes, including in the
form of local, regional, state, national, and international workshops as well as online
teacher development modules. Platforms (especially online platforms) are to be
developed so that teachers may share ideas and best practices. Each teacher is
expected to participate in at least 50 hours of CPD (continuous Professional
Development) opportunities every year for their own professional development,
driven by their own interests.
In order to provide continuous professional development opportunities to the
teachers’ initiatives like NISHTHA (National Initiative for School Heads’ and
Teachers’ Holistic Advancement) program developed by National Council of
Education, Research and Training (NCERT) are implemented by the Department. It
aligns with NEP's focus on enhancing teacher competency, fostering innovative
pedagogical practices, and integrating technology into education. NISHTHA plays a
pivotal role in improving teaching quality, fostering equity, and achieving the NEP's
goal of creating a vibrant, inclusive, and future-ready education system.
In addition, for pre-service training programmes, Norms and Standards for
Integrated Teacher Education Program (ITEP), a four-year undergraduate course
03.02.2025 429
designed to prepare teachers comprehensively for the demands of the 21st century
has been notified by National Council for Teacher Education (NCTE) on 26th
October 2021. As outlined in NEP 2020, ITEP is a pioneering initiative that combines
education with a specific discipline and stage-specific specialization. This dual major
programme spans four years and is structured to cater to different school stages:
foundational, preparatory, middle, and secondary. The ITEP Curriculum
Framework and stage-specific syllabi is aligned with NEP 2020, National Higher
Education Qualification Framework (NHEQF) and National Credit Framework
(NCrF) and emphasizes on 21st-century skills and abilities.
National Professional Standards for Teachers (NPST) and the National
Mission for Mentoring (NMM) have been developed to support the professional
development of teachers. The NPST outlines the competencies required for effective
teaching at different career stages. The NMM focuses on creating a robust network
of mentors to provide professional and personal support to teachers. NPST guiding
Document and NMM Bluebook have been shared with States/UTs for wider
dissemination.
Further, under the integrated centrally sponsored scheme of Samagra
Shiksha, financial assistance is provided to the States/UTs as per the programmatic
and financial norms of the scheme for implementing the different interventions/
components of the scheme, including teacher training as also for physical
upgradation of all 613 functional DIETs in the country in a phased manner in order
to strengthen the in-service training imparted by DIETs.
(g) The National Education Policy (NEP), 2020 has placed a strong emphasis on
multilingual and multicultural education. NEP 2020 inter-alia, provides that wherever
possible, the medium of instruction until at least Grade 5, but preferably till Grade 8
03.02.2025 430
and beyond, is to be the home language/mother tongue/local language/regional
language. Thereafter, the home/local language shall continue to be taught as a
language wherever possible.
To promote language learning among young and adult learners; primers—
instructional materials in book form (print or digital), is being designed. Such
resources facilitate reading and writing—which are crucial inputs for strengthening
foundational literacy and numeracy among young and adult learners in a specific
language.
 In the first phase Kuwi and Desia Primers were launched on 17 August 2023
at Bhubaneswar, Odisha for its wider dissemination and use.
 In second phase 52 Primers in Indian Languages were released on 9th March
2024.
 As on Date a total number of 104 primers have been launched. These Primers
have been uploaded on NCERT web portal:
https://ncert.nic.in/primers.php?ln=en and DIKSHA portals for wider
dissemination and print copies have been shared with SCERTs in States /
UTs
In addition, National Initiative for School Heads and Teachers for Holistic
Advancement - NISHTHA 3.0 online has been launched to train the teachers from
Early Childhood care and Education (ECCE) up to grade V. It has 12 training
modules including a dedicated module on Multilingual teaching in foundational
years, which also covers usage of mother tongue / home language in teaching.
(h) The integrated Samagra Shiksha Scheme has been aligned with the
recommendations of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and aims to ensure
03.02.2025 431
that all children have access to quality education with an equitable and inclusive
classroom environment which should take care of their diverse background,
multilingual needs, different academic abilities and make them active participants in
the learning process.
Under Samagra Shiksha, financial assistance is provided to States and UTs
for providing various facilities to the children including marginalized and
disadvantaged children for implementing the Right of Children to Free and
Compulsory Education (RTE) Act, 2009 as also for implementation of various
provisions of Samagra Shiksha Scheme which include free uniform to eligible
children at elementary level, free textbooks at elementary level, development of
primers/textbooks for tribal language, teaching learning materials, transport/escort
facility up to secondary level, special training for age appropriate admission of out of
school children and residential as well as non-residential training for older children,
seasonal hostels / residential camps, special training centers, age-appropriate
residential and non-residential training, support of Rs. 2000 per child per grade to
Out of School Children (16 to 19 years) for completion of education through
NIOS/SIOS, Holistic Progress Card, bilingual teaching material and books, support
to Children with Special Needs (CWSN). Further, under the student-oriented
component for the children with special needs, financial assistance is provided for
identification and assessment of children with special needs, aids and appliances,
braille kits and books, appropriate teaching learning material and stipend to girl
students with disability etc.
Also, under the Scheme financial assistance is provided to the States and UTs
for Universalization of School Education including opening/strengthening of new
schools upto senior secondary level, construction of school buildings and additional
03.02.2025 432
classrooms, development/strengthening of school infrastructure in northern border
areas under Vibrant Village Programme, setting up, up-gradation and running of
Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalayas, construction of hostels for PVTGs under PM-
JANMAN (Pradhan Mantri Janjati Adivasi Nyaya Maha Abhiyan), setting up of Netaji
Subhash Chandra Bose Avasiya Vidyalayas, construction of hostels under Dharti
Aaba Janjatiya Gram Utkarsh Abhiyan for unsaturated ST population,
reimbursement under RTE Act, various qualitative components, strengthening of
teacher education and strengthening of DIETs/BRCs/CRCs, provision of ICT and
digital interventions.

PROVISION OF FACILITIES AT TOURISM HOTSPOTS
65. SHRI PUTTA MAHESH KUMAR:
Will the Minister of TOURISM be pleased to state:
(a) the number of monuments, locations and areas identified by the Government
to have heavy tourist (domestic and international) footfall and identified as
tourism hotspots across the country, particularly in Andhra Pradesh;
(b) whether the Government has undertaken any recent survey/study regarding
the status of infrastructure and facilities at such tourism hotspots in the
country, particularly in Andhra Pradesh;
(c) if so, the total number of such areas which have been identified to be lacking
basic facilities such as toilets (male, female and others), ramps, rest-rooms,
registered guides and drinking water facilities; and
(d) the details of steps taken by the Government to resolve these issues during
the last five years along with the funds allocated/utilised, State-wise,
particularly in Andhra Pradesh?
03.02.2025 434
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI GAJENDRA
SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) to (d) The Ministry of Tourism has conducted a study on “Assessment of
Tourist Destinations in Areas of Infrastructure and Cleanliness” to evaluate 185
tourist sites across 53 destinations in 29 States and Union Territories including
Andhra Pradesh. The sites and destinations were evaluated based on four key
elements, namely: accessibility, amenities, hygiene and safety and security.
The above mentioned study covered total 8 sites in Andhra Pradesh across various
themes. The sites assessed include:
1. Mallikarjuna Swamy Temple (Srisailam)
2. Bhramaramba Devi Temple (Srisailam)
3. Srisailam Wildlife Sanctuary (Srisailam)
4. Chandavaram Buddhist Site (Amaravati)
5. Amareswara Temple (Amaravati)
6. Kanaka Durga Temple (Amaravati)
7. Nagarjunkhonda (Amaravati)
8. Undavalli Caves (Amaravati)
The Ministry of Tourism under the schemes of ‘Swadesh Darshan’, ‘National Mission
on Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual Heritage Augmentation Drive (PRASHAD)’
and ‘Assistance to Central Agencies for Tourism Infrastructure Development’
provides financial assistance to State Governments/Union Territory
Administrations/Central Agencies for the development of tourism-related
infrastructure and facilities at various tourism destinations in the country, including
the State of Andhra Pradesh.
03.02.2025 435
Ministry of Tourism revamped its Swadesh Darshan Scheme as Swadesh Darshan
2.0 (SD2.0) with the objective to develop sustainable and responsible tourism
destinations following a tourist and destination centric approach.
The details of projects sanctioned under the above-mentioned schemes are given
in the STATEMENT.

PF PENSIONS ON HIGHER WAGES
66. ADV. ADOOR PRAKASH:
Will the Minister of LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT be pleased to state:
03.02.2025 461
(a) whether the Government is aware that majority of employees and pensioners
across the country who opted for PF pensions on higher wages that have not
been granted the higher pension by the Employees' Provident Fund Organization;
(b) if so, the reasons for delay even after lapse of two years after Supreme Court
ordered for higher EPF pension in proportion to salary;
(c) the number of applications received for higher pension and granted as on date,
State-wise; and
(d) whether the Government has taken any immediate measures to process the
pending applications without delay?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM
ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF LABOUR AND
EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a) to (d) The cases of Pension on Higher Wages are being processed on the
basis of the decision of the Hon'ble Supreme Court dated 04.11.2022.
A Functionality was developed by EPFO for filing online applications
for validation/joint option. The online facility was extended to
members/pensioners/employers on 26.02.2023 which was extended till
11.07.2023. The date for employers for forwarding the joint application for higher
pension was extended 30.09.2023 and subsequently till 31.05.2024. Further, a last
chance was provided to all employers to forward the applications for pension on
higher wages by 31.01.2025.
A total of 17,48,768 applications were submitted by Members/
Pensioners. Out of these, as on 28.01.2025, demand notices i.e. intimation to
members to deposit balance amounts for higher pension eligibility, have been
03.02.2025 462
issued in 1,65,621 cases and 21,885 Pension Payment Orders (PPO) have been
issued out of the said number of applications.
The disposal of the outstanding cases is being closely monitored and
clarifications to Field Offices to dispose cases have been issued to expedite the
process.
DEATH OF ENDANGERED OLIVE RIDLEY TURTLES
67. SHRI A RAJA:
Will the Minister of ENVIRONMENT, FOREST AND CLIMATE CHANGE be
pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is aware of the carcasses of endangered Olive
Ridley Turtles reaching along the shores of Vizag Coast in Bay of Bengal,
in recent months;
(b) the details of the concern expressed by Marine Environment Experts on
the issue;
(c) whether any inquiry/study has been made in this regard, if so, the details
thereof along with the reasons for death of turtles; and
(d) the steps taken/proposed to be taken by the Government to prevent such
incidents in future?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT, FOREST
AND CLIMATE CHANGE; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EXTERNAL AFFAIRS (SHRI KIRTI VARDHAN SINGH):
03.02.2025 463
(a) to (d) As informed by the State Government of Andhra Pradesh, carcasses of
Olive Ridley Turtles have been found along the shores of Vizag coast in Bay of
Bengal. Enquiry made by the Andhra Pradesh Forest Department indicate
entanglement of turtles in trawler nets as the main cause of death of turtles.
However, such details are not maintained in the Ministry. The important steps
taken by the Government for the conservation and protection of marine turtles
include the following:
i. Five species of marine turtles viz., Olive ridley turtle (Lepidochelys olivacea),
Green turtle (Chelonia mydas), Hawks bill turtle (Eretmochelys imbricata),
Leatherback turtle (Dermochelys coriacea) and Loggerhead turtle (Caretta
caretta) reported to be found in India are listed in Schedule I of the Wild Life
(Protection) Act, 1972 thereby providing them the highest degree of
protection.
ii. The Ministry provides financial and technical support to the States for
conservation and management of wildlife including marine turtles and its
habitat under the Centrally Sponsored Scheme- Development of Wildlife
Habitats.
iii. The Ministry has included marine turtles as one of the 22 species for taking
up focused conservation programme for recovery of critically endangered
species under the Centrally Sponsored Scheme – Development of Wildlife
Habitats.
iv. The Ministry through amendment of Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972 during
2022 has empowered the India Coast Guard for entry, search and
03.02.2025 464
detention in case of any violations in relation to marine species listed in
schedules of the Act, including marine turtles.
v. The Ministry has released a National Marine Turtle Action Plan with the aim
to conserve marine turtles and their habitats in India. The Action Plan
focusses on reducing threats, conserving critical habitats, exchanging
scientific data, increasing public awareness and emphasizes on a
participatory approach in conservation of marine turtles and its habitat.
vi. The Ministry has released ‘Marine Megafauna Stranding Management
Guidelines’ in 2021 for management of stranding and entanglement of
marine mega fauna including marine turtles.
vii. A network of coastal and marine protected areas under the Wild Life
(Protection) Act, 1972 has been created for the conservation of marine
species, including turtles.

ESI HOSPITALS IN KERALA
69. SHRI HIBI EDEN:
Will the Minister of LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT be pleased to state:
(a) the details of funds allocated/likely to be allocated for the Employees State
Insurance (ESI) hospitals functioning at Ernakulam district, Kerala, hospital-
wise, for the last years and the current year;
(b) whether the Government proposes to upgrade and modernize ESI hospitals at
Ernakulam district, Kerala and if so, the details thereof;
(c) whether the Government is planning/has any proposal to upgrade the facilities in
all Employees’ State Insurance (ESI) hospitals functioning at Ernakulam district,
Kerala, if so, the details thereof;
(d) whether the Government planned to open one super speciality hospital in each
State under ESIC, if so, the details thereof; and
(e) whether the Government has received any requests to establish new hospitals
in Kerala, if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM
ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF LABOUR AND
EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a) and (b) At present, two Employees’ State Insurance (ESI) hospitals are
functioning in Ernakulam district i.e. (i) state run ESI hospital at Ernakulam and (ii)
ESIC hospital, Udyogmandal. The details of funds sanctioned for ESIC hospital,
Udyogmandal and funds allocated for annual repair and maintenance in respect of
state run ESI hospital, Ernakulam is enclosed as STATEMENT. ESIC releases
funds to State Government in case of State run hospital, under the ‘On Account’
03.02.2025 467
payment for making adequate provision of medical services to ESI beneficiaries.
Hospital-wise fund is provisioned by State Government.
(c) ESI Corporation has given approval for upgradation of ESIC Hospital,
Udyogmandal from 100 to 150 beds.
(d) and (e) Setting up of ESI Hospital is an ongoing process. ESI Corporation has
accorded ‘in-principle’ approval for setting up of two new 100 bedded ESI hospitals
i.e. one each at Perambavoor (District: Ernakulam) and at Kattappana (District:
Idukki) based on the request received from State Government of Kerala in order to
improve delivery of medical services to ESI beneficiaries.

GST ON INSURANCE PREMIUM
70. SHRI S VENKATESAN:

THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FINANCE (SHRI PANKAJ
CHAUDHARY):
(a) to (c) During the 55th meeting of the GST Council held on 21st December, 2024
in Jaisalmer, the Convener of the Group of Ministers (GoM) on Life and Health
Insurance sought more time for finalizing the GoM’s recommendations and placing
03.02.2025 469
the same before the GST Council. The Council agreed to give more time to GoM to
finalize its recommendations.
SCHEMES FOR UPSKILLING OF YOUTHS UNDER PMKVY
71. DR. KIRSAN NAMDEO:
Will the Minister of SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP be
pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has undertaken any schemes for upskilling of youths
under Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY), if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether the Government has undertaken skill training programmes at
Gadchiroli Lok Sabha Constituency in the State of Maharashtra, if so, the details
thereof;
(c) whether the Government plans to conduct Rozgar Mela for unemployed
youths in the country especially in Maharashtra State, if so, the details thereof;
(d) whether any programmes are being implemented at Gadchiroli, Maharashtra
State through the National Skill Development Agency, if so, the details thereof; and
(e) whether the Government has initiated any schemes for training of youngsters
keeping its vision of developing the country, if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a), (b) and (e) Under the Govt. of India’s Skill India Mission (SIM), the Ministry of
Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) is implementing its flagship
scheme, Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY). PMKVY has two training
03.02.2025 470
components, namely, Short Term Training (STT) and Recognition of Prior Learning
(RPL). Under PMKVY-STT, candidates are being trained in short duration courses
through empanelled training centres (TCs) across the country, whereas, under
PMKVY-RPL component, candidates having prior skill are assessed after
reskilling/upskilling and certified through orientation or bridge courses.
MSDE has undertaken skill training programmes in Gadchiroli Lok Sabha
Constituency (Gadchiroli, Gondia, Chandrapur districts) in the State of Maharashtra.
Under PMKVY, since 2015 as on 31.12.2024, around 53 thousand candidates have
been trained and out of which around 40 thousand candidates have been certified
in Gadchiroli Lok Sabha Constituency (Gadchiroli, Gondia, Chandrapur districts) of
the State of Maharashtra.
(c) National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC), the project implementing
agency for PMKVY, conducts Rozgar Melas on regular basis across the country. So
far, a total of 64 Rozgar Melas have been organized in the State of Maharashtra.
(d) The National Skill Development Agency (NSDA) was an autonomous body
registered as a society under the Societies Registration Act, 1860, under the Ministry
of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship. Consequent upon its merger with
National Council for Vocational Training (NCVT), the National Council for Vocational
Education and Training (NCVET) was established as a regulatory body. It functions
as an overarching national regulator for setting standards and formulating
comprehensive regulations for the vocational education, training, and skilling
ecosystem for improving quality and outcomes.
PROJECTS TO DEVELOP LESSER KNOWN DESTINATIONS
72. SHRI SHRIRANG APPA CHANDU BARNE:
03.02.2025 471
SHRIMATI BHARTI PARDHI:
Will the Minister of TOURISM be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Union Government has approved tourism projects to develop
lesser known destinations into iconic sites;
(b) if so, the details of the sites identified therefor, State-wise, particularly in
Maharashtra and Balaghat of Madhya Pradesh;
(c) whether any time line has been fixed for completion of these projects;
(d) if so, the details thereof along with the funds sanctioned and released therefor,
State-wise, particularly in Maharashtra and Balaghat of Madhya Pradesh; and
(e) the details of steps taken by the Union Government to distribute tourists more
evenly, promote local economies and ensure sustainable growth in tourism?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI GAJENDRA
SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) to (d) While development and promotion of tourism destinations and products
is primarily undertaken by the concerned State Government (SG)/Union Territory
Administration (UTA), the Ministry of Tourism through its central sector schemes of
‘Swadesh Darshan (SD)’ and ‘Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual, Heritage
Augmentation Drive (PRASHAD)’ complements the efforts of tourism infrastructure
development by extending financial assistance to the SGs/UTAs in synergy with the
scheme guidelines, availability of funds etc.
The Swadesh Darshan and PRASHAD schemes were launched in the year 2014-
15. Since its inception a total number of 76 projects under different thematic circuits
of Swadesh Darshan scheme, have been sanctioned in the country for a revised
sanction amount of Rs.5287.90 Crore out of which Rs.4956.39 Crore has been
released/authorized. Out of these 76 projects, 75 projects are reported physically
03.02.2025 472
complete. Since its launch the Ministry has sanctioned 47 projects in 27 States/UTs
under the PRASHAD scheme with estimated cost of Rs.1594.40 crore and a total
amount of Rs.1036.96 crore has been released for these project.
Ministry of Tourism has revamped the Swadesh Darshan Scheme as Swadesh
Darshan 2.0 (SD 2.0) with the objective to develop sustainable and responsible
tourism destinations, following a destination and tourist-centric approach.
Under ‘Scheme for Special Assistance to States/Union Territories for Capital
Investment (SASCI) - Development of Iconic Tourist Centers to Global Scale’,
Government of India has recently sanctioned 40 Projects in the country for
Rs.3295.76 Crore. Under the Scheme the projects are to be completed by
31.03.2026.
The details of the projects sanctioned under the Swadesh Darshan, SD 2.0,
PRASHAD and SASCI schemes in Maharashtra and Madhya Pradesh are enclosed
as STATEMENT.
(e) Ministry of Tourism has taken several steps to distribute tourists more evenly,
promote local economies and ensure sustainable growth in tourism.
Under various schemes of the Ministry, tourism facilities are developed in various
parts of the country including lesser-known destinations.
The Ministry advises States/UTs to ensure due consultation with the local
communities in preparing the projects and emphasis is given on adoption of
principles of sustainable tourism including environmental sustainability, Socio-
cultural sustainability and economic sustainability.
Ministry of Tourism has also formulated a National Strategy for Sustainable Tourism
and launched ‘Travel for LiFE’, a sectoral program under Mission Lifestyle for
Environment (LiFE). The objective of Travel for LiFE is to promote mainstreaming of
03.02.2025 473
sustainability in the tourism sector, create awareness and nudge tourists and tourism
businesses to adopt sustainable practices synchronous with nature.
The Ministry of Tourism has also revamped its Swadesh Darshan scheme in the
form of Swadesh Darshan 2.0 with the mission to create a robust framework for
integrated development of tourism destinations.

OUTSTANDING LOANS TOWARDS STUDENTS
75 SHRI ARUN NEHRU:
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state:
03.02.2025 480
(a) whether the Government proposes to bring a one time settlement policy for
student loans;
(b) if so, the details thereof; and
(c) the action taken by the Government on outstanding loans taken by students
who are not able to repay?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FINANCE (SH. PANKAJ
CHAUDHARY):
(a) and (b) Currently, no such proposal is under consideration.
(c) All Scheduled Commercial Banks (SCBs) have been advised by Reserve Bank
of India vide circular RPCD.PLNFS.BC.NO.83/06.12.05/2000-01 dated April 28,
2001 to adopt Model Education Loan Scheme (MELS), formulated by Indian Banks’
Association (IBA). The Scheme has since been revised by IBA from time to time and
its latest revision is MELS, 2022. The scheme inter-alia provides Moratorium period
upto study period plus one year and repayment period (after moratorium) is available
upto 15 years.
In addition, the scheme provides that Banks may also:
i. extended moratorium period taking into account spells of under-employment/
unemployment, say two or three times (maximum of 6 months at a time) during
the life cycle of the loan;
ii. consider ‘Telescoping of repayment with stepped up instalments with passage
of time’, in certain cases (e.g. professionals like doctors).
ADOPT A HERITAGE SCHEME
76. SHRI ASADUDDIN OWAISI:
Will the Minister of CULTURE be pleased to state:
03.02.2025 481
(a) whether the Government has launched the second phase of the Adopt A
Heritage scheme and if so, the details thereof;
(b) the list of heritage sites being considered for adoption under this scheme;
(c) the list of heritage sites which have been adopted till date, along with the date
of adoption and the name of the organisation/agency involved; and
(d) the list of modifications proposed by the adopting organisation / agency for
the adopted monuments?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI GAJENDRA
SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) The “Adopt A Heritage” programme was first launched by the Ministry of Tourism
in September 2017. A revamped version titled “Adopt A Heritage 2.0” was launched
by the Ministry of Culture in September 2023. The program seeks to provide a
framework for collaboration with private and public sector
companies/NGOs/trusts/societies, etc. to develop/provide amenities in Protected
Monuments through their CSR funds to enhance the visitors’ experience and make
the adopted monument visitor-friendly. The amenities are categorized into four
broad categories: Hygiene, including toilets, drinking water, waste management,
baby care rooms, etc.; Accessibility, including pathways, barrier-free access, battery
operated vehicles, signage, landscaping, Wi-Fi facility, parking, etc.; Safety,
including CCTV, lighting, illumination, cloakrooms, first aid kits, etc.; and Knowledge,
including publications, souvenir kiosks, cultural/light and sound shows, AR/VR tools,
cafeteria, etc.
(b) and (c) The program is open for all protected monuments. Under this program,
21 MoUs have been executed between Archaeological Survey of India and
03.02.2025 482
partnering entities so far across various states. Details of the monuments adopted
so far are attached in STATEMENT.
(d) The adopting organisation/agency is not permitted to carry out any modification
in the structure of the adopted monuments. They are only permitted to
develop/provide defined amenities for visitors with the approval of the
Archaeological Survey of India to enhance the visiting experience.

ENTRANCE COACHING INSTITUTES
77. DR. K SUDHAKAR:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has noticed that a large number of entrance coaching
institutes without any credentials operate across the country, if so, the details
thereof;
(b) whether the Government has noticed that the entrance coaching institutes mint
money in the name of assured ranks in entrance examinations often looting the
general public, if so, the steps taken by the Government to regulate the sector;
(c) the steps taken by the Government to ensure that space of public educational
institutions is not utilised by private institutions for conducting coaching classes for
entrance examination;
(d) the steps taken by the Government to ensure that the State provides coaching
to students for various competitive entrance examinations;
(e) the steps/measures being taken by the Government to ensure that such
coaching institutes are prohibited from using successful children to market their
institutions; and
(f) whether the Government has any data of such coaching institutes and if so, the
details thereof, State-wise?
03.02.2025 486
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF EDUCATION; AND MINISTER
OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF DEVELOPMENT OF NORTH EASTERN
REGION (DR. SUKANTA MAJUMDAR):
(a) to (f) Keeping in view the growth in number of unregulated private coaching
centres in the country in the absence of any laid down policy or regulation; instances
of such centers charging exorbitant fees from students; making misleading promises
or guarantee of rank or good marks to parents/students for enrolling them in the
coaching center and many other malpractices being adopted by these centres,
Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Education (MoE) has circulated a
Guidelines for Regulation of Coaching Center to States/UTs on 16.01.2024. This
has been followed up with another letter to the States/ UTs on 16.07.2024. Education
being in the concurrent list, the State and UT Governments need to take further
action by way of appropriate legal framework. The guidelines for regulation of
coaching centres are accessible at
https://www.education.gov.in/sites/upload_files/mhrd/files/Guideliens_Coaching_C
entres_en.pdf
The Guidelines encompasses several key aspects, including defining coaching
centers, specifying conditions and necessary documents for registration; outlining
infrastructure prerequisites for establishing coaching centers; the fee charged for
courses / curriculum shall be fair and reasonable; under no circumstances the fee
shall be increased during the currency of the course; easy exit policy and refund of
fee on pro-rata basis; coaching classes for those students who are also studying in
institutions / schools shall not be conducted during their institutions / schools’ hours
so that their regular attendance in such institutions / schools remains unaffected and
03.02.2025 487
also to avoid dummy schools. The guidelines stipulate that no coaching center shall
publish any misleading advertisement. The guidelines, further stipulate continuous
monitoring of activities of the coaching centers; introducing a complaint mechanism
and disposal thereof; penalties; process for cancellation of registration and appeals
etc.
SATHEE platform has been launched by MoE to provide high-quality content for the
competitive examinations like JEE and NEET, along with providing an online
assessment module for students. It leverages Web Platform, Mobile Application,
YouTube live sessions and DTH channels for delivery. More than 10,000 Hours of
Learning Content is available across topics and subjects for the learners.
Under the provisions of the Consumer Protection Act, 2019, the Central Consumer
Protection Authority is mandated to prevent and regulate false or misleading
advertisements which are prejudicial to the public interest. Central Consumer
Protection Authority (CCPA) has taken action against misleading advertisement by
coaching centers. In this regard, CCPA has issued 46 notices to various coaching
centers for misleading advertisement and unfair trade practice. The CCPA has
imposed a penalty of 72 lakhs 60 thousands on 23 coaching institutes and directed
them to discontinue the misleading advertisements.
To protect the rights of consumers and maintain transparency in the coaching sector,
the CCPA has issued comprehensive guidelines to address the issue of misleading
advertisements in the coaching sector. Guidelines for Prevention of Misleading
Advertisement in Coaching Sector 2024, aim to safeguard students and the public
from deceptive marketing practices commonly employed by coaching centers.
The CCPA guidelines also provides that any person who is engaged in coaching
03.02.2025 488
shall not use name, photographs, testimonials or videos of successful candidate in
an advertisement without candidate’s written consent. The guidelines are available
on the Department of Consumer Affairs website
https://consumeraffairs.nic.in/sites/default/files/file-
uploads/latestnews/Guidelines%20for%20Prevention%20of%20Misleading%20Ad
vertisement%20in%20Coaching%20Sector%2C%202024.pdf
KGBVs IN JHARKHAND
78. SHRI DULU MAHATO:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) the number of Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalaya’s (KGBVs) established in
Jharkhand, district-wise;
(b) the number of girls enrolled in these Vidyalayas;
(c) the steps taken by the Government to set up smart classrooms and ICT labs in
these vidyalayas in Jharkhand, district-wise specifically in Dhanbad;
(d) the role of ICT in enhancing the education of girls from SC, ST, OBC and BPL
categories in KGBV, Dhanbad;
(e) the details of the changes observed in digital literacy and educational activities
in KGBVsin Jharkhand due to the implementation of smart classrooms and ICT
labs, district-wise; and
(f) the details of financial assistance extended for the establishment of ICT labs
and smart classrooms in KGBVs under the Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan in
Jharkhand, district-wise?
03.02.2025 489
THE MINISTER OF STATEOF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP;AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANTCHAUDHARY):
(a) and (b) Jharkhand has 203 KGBVs with an enrolment of 77, 850 girl students.
District wise details are at STATEMENT.
(c) to (e) Under Information and Communication Technology (ICT) Component of
Samagra Shiksha Scheme, financial assistance is provided to States/UTs for
establishing Smart Classrooms and ICT labs in all the functional KGBV Hostels. It
aims to provide basic infrastructure to the students and transform the classroom into
tech driven class. The students increase engagement with multimedia content and
interactive activities, while availing wider range of resources. ICT Lab has opened a
new learning area where girls are now learning computers and other allied things
attached to this learning. This fosters deeper understanding, collaboration, and
digital literacy for students, enriching their overall educational experience. At present,
ICT labs and Smart classrooms are available in all the 203 KGBVs functional in
Jharkhand. District wise details, including in Dhanbad, are enclosed at
STATEMENT.
Further, State Government of Jharkhand has also provided additional 28 ICT
Lab and 190 Smart Class rooms, including one ICT Lab and 28 Smart Class rooms
in Dhanbad district, to selected KGBVs under Adarsh Vidyalaya Scheme and School
of Excellence Scheme.
(f) Under ICT Component of Samagra Shiksha Scheme, Rs.136.22 lakhs have been
allocated for establishing 49 smart classrooms across 24 districts in Jharkhand in the
FY 2024-25. District wise details are enclosed at STATEMENT.
03.02.2025 490

(31.03.2024 तक के आकं ड़(cid:164)) ((cid:261)ोत: कारपोरेट डाटा (cid:255)बंधन (cid:255)को(cid:311))
* कं पिनय(cid:332) ने उन (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) के नाम िनिद(cid:170)(cid:310) नह(cid:233) िकए जहां प(cid:229)रयोजनाए ं शु(cid:322) क(cid:236) गई थ(cid:233)।
पीएम (cid:174)ी योजना के अंतग(cid:170)त (cid:214)तरो(cid:198)नत िकए गए िव(cid:299)ालय
80. (cid:174)ी निलन सोरने :
(cid:174)ी जुगल िकशोर:
(cid:179)या िश(cid:177)ा म(cid:253)ं ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृपा करग(cid:164) े िक:
(क) (cid:179)या (cid:255)धान म(cid:253)ं ी (cid:214)कू ल फॉर राइिजंग इंिडया (पीएम (cid:174)ी) योजना को झारखंड और ज(cid:204)मू क(cid:212)मीर
रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा समझौता (cid:178)ापन पर ह(cid:214)ता(cid:177)र िकए जाने के बाद वहां काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत िकया जा रहा है;
(ख) यिद हां, तो त(cid:194)संबंधी िजलावार (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है;
(ग) पीएम (cid:174)ी िव(cid:299)ालय(cid:332) को (cid:250)ीन (cid:214)कू ल के (cid:322)प म(cid:164) िवकिसत करने के िलए सरकार (cid:300)ारा क(cid:236) गई पहल का
(cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है और पहले से ही (cid:250)ीन (cid:214)कू ल के (cid:322)प म(cid:164) शािमल िकए गए िव(cid:299)ालय(cid:332) क(cid:236) रा(cid:186)यवार स(cid:180)ं या
िकतनी है;
(घ) सरकार (cid:300)ारा (cid:255)ारिं भक बा(cid:208)याव(cid:214)था प(cid:229)रचया(cid:170) िवकास के िलए क(cid:236) गई पहल का (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है और
इससे रा(cid:186)यवार िकतने िव(cid:299)ालय लाभाि(cid:198)वत ह(cid:242)ए ह(cid:167); और
(ङ.) सरकार (cid:300)ारा पीएम-(cid:174)ी िव(cid:299)ालय म(cid:164) शत-(cid:255)ितशत िडिजटल िश(cid:177)ण को शािमल करने के िलए उठाए
गए कदम(cid:332) का (cid:202)यौरा (cid:179)या है और इससे लाभाि(cid:198)वत ह(cid:242)ए िव(cid:299)ालय(cid:332) क(cid:236) रा(cid:186)यवार सं(cid:180)या िकतनी है?
कौशल िवकास और उ(cid:299)मशीलता म(cid:253)ं ालय के रा(cid:186) य म(cid:253)ं ी; तथा िश(cid:177)ा म(cid:253)ं ालय म(cid:164) रा(cid:186)य म(cid:253)ं ी ((cid:174)ी
जयंत चौधरी):
03.02.2025 497
(क) और (ख) जी हां, पीएम (cid:174)ी योजना झारखंड और ज(cid:204)मू क(cid:212)मीर म(cid:164) इन रा(cid:186)य(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा समझौता (cid:178)ापन
(एमओयू) पर ह(cid:214)ता(cid:177)र िकए जाने के बाद काया(cid:170)ि(cid:198)वत क(cid:236) जा रही है। झारखंड रा(cid:186)य से कुल 345 (cid:214)कू ल(cid:332)
का चयन िकया गया ह,ै तथा ज(cid:204)मू और क(cid:212)मीर संघ रा(cid:186)य (cid:177)े(cid:253) से पारदश(cid:234) चुनौती प(cid:294)ित के मा(cid:197)यम स े
कुल 396 (cid:214)कू ल(cid:332) का चयन िकया गया है। झारखंड रा(cid:186)य और ज(cid:204)मू और क(cid:212)मीर संघ रा(cid:186)य (cid:177)े(cid:253) म (cid:164)
चयिनत पीएम (cid:174)ी (cid:214)कू ल(cid:332) क(cid:236) िजलावार कुल स(cid:180)ं या का (cid:202)यौरा िववरण -I म(cid:164) सलं (cid:181)न है।
(ग) स े (ड.) पीएम (cid:174)ी योजना का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य पया(cid:170)वरण अनुकू ल पहलओु ंको शािमल करके पीएम (cid:174)ी (cid:214)कू ल(cid:332)
को ह(cid:229)रत (cid:214)कू ल(cid:332) के (cid:322)प म (cid:164)िवकिसत करना है, जैसे सौर पैनल(cid:332) और एलईडी (cid:255)काश (cid:211)यव(cid:214)था का उपयोग
करके ऊजा(cid:170) द(cid:177) बनाना, (cid:255)ाकृ ितक खेती के साथ पोषण उ(cid:299)ान, अपिश(cid:310) (cid:255)बंधन, (cid:200)लाि(cid:214)टक म(cid:265)ु , जल
संर(cid:177)ण और संचयन, पया(cid:170)वरण संर(cid:177)ण से संबंिधत परपं राओ/ं(cid:255)थाओ ंका अ(cid:197)ययन, जलवाय ु प(cid:229)रवत(cid:170)न
से संबिं धत हैकथॉन और जैिवक जीवन शैली को शािमल करने के िलए जाग(cid:322)कता पैदा करना। इन
उपाय(cid:332) का ल(cid:224)य देश भर के (cid:214)कू ल(cid:332) म(cid:164) एक (cid:214)थायी और पया(cid:170)वरण-अनकु ू ल िश(cid:177)ण वातावरण बनाना है।
पीएम (cid:174)ी योजना का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य बालवािटका के मा(cid:197)यम से मूलभूत सा(cid:177)रता और सं(cid:180)या(cid:194)मक(cid:178)ान
पर (cid:197)यान क(cid:164) ि(cid:254)त करके पीएम (cid:174)ी (cid:214)कू ल(cid:332) म(cid:164) (cid:255)ारिं भक बचपन क(cid:236) देखभाल िवकिसत करना है। पीएम (cid:174)ी
योजना म(cid:164) छोटे ब(cid:184)च(cid:332) म(cid:164) मूलभूत कौशल िवकिसत करने के िलए िश(cid:177)क(cid:332) को िविश(cid:310) िश(cid:177)ण-अिधगम
साम(cid:250)ी (टीएलएम) उपल(cid:202)ध कराने का (cid:255)ावधान है। इन पहल(cid:332) का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य (cid:214)कू ल(cid:332) को एक मजबूत (cid:255)ारिं भक
िश(cid:177)ा ढांचा तैयार करने के िलए आव(cid:212)यक संसाधन(cid:332) और (cid:255)िश(cid:177)ण स े सुसि(cid:186)जत करना है, िजससे यह
सिु नि(cid:306)त हो सके िक ब(cid:184)च(cid:332) को बुिनयादी (cid:214)तर से ही गणु व(cid:176)ापूण(cid:170) िश(cid:177)ा िमले।
पीएम (cid:174)ी योजना का उ(cid:293)े(cid:212)य पीएम (cid:174)ी (cid:214)कू ल(cid:332) म(cid:164) आईसीटी-स(cid:177)म (cid:214)माट(cid:170) क(cid:177)ाओ ंऔर शैि(cid:177)क
साम(cid:250)ी से (cid:255)ी लोडेड टैबलेट से सुसि(cid:186)जत िडिजटल प(cid:214)ु तकालय(cid:332) क(cid:236) (cid:214)थापना करके 100% िडिजटल
िश(cid:177)ाशा(cid:318) को शािमल करना है। दी(cid:177)ा जैसे (cid:200)लेटफॉम(cid:170) का उपयोग उ(cid:184)च गुणव(cid:176)ा वाले िडिजटल िश(cid:177)ण
संसाधन(cid:332) का भंडार उपलबध कराने करने के िलए िकया जा रहा है, िजससे िश(cid:177)क(cid:332) और छा(cid:253)(cid:332) के बीच
संसाधन साझा करना संभव हो रहा है। इसके अित(cid:229)र(cid:265), िश(cid:177)क(cid:332) और छा(cid:253)(cid:332) के बीच भाषा संबंधी बाधाओ ं
को दूर करने और यो(cid:181)यता-आधा(cid:229)रत िश(cid:177)ण को बढ़ाने के िलए तकनीक(cid:236) उपाय शु(cid:322) िकए जा रहे ह(cid:167)।
इसम(cid:164) िश(cid:177)क(cid:332) क(cid:236) (cid:177)मता िनमा(cid:170)ण का भी (cid:255)ावधान है, तािक वे अपने िश(cid:177)ण काय(cid:170) म(cid:164) िडिजटल िश(cid:177)ण को
03.02.2025 498
(cid:255)भावी ढंग से एक(cid:236)कृत कर सक(cid:164) , िजससे छा(cid:253)(cid:332) के िलए सम(cid:294)ृ और आकष(cid:170)क िश(cid:177)ण अनुभव सुिनि(cid:306)त
हो सके ।
पीएम (cid:174)ी योजना के तहत पीएम (cid:174)ी (cid:214)कू ल(cid:332) का के िवसं/निवस/एनसीईआरटी सिहत रा(cid:186)य/संघ
रा(cid:186)य-वार (cid:202)यौरा िववरण -II म(cid:164) संल(cid:181)न है। इसके अलावा, उपरो(cid:265) सभी पहल िविभ(cid:198)न रा(cid:186)य(cid:332), संघ रा(cid:186)य
(cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) और के िवसं/निवस/एनसीईआरटी के िविभ(cid:198)न पीएम (cid:174)ी (cid:214)कू ल(cid:332) म(cid:164) काया(cid:170)(cid:198)वयन के िविभ(cid:198)न चरण(cid:332) म(cid:164)

OPENING OF NEW KENDRIYA VIDYALAYAS
81. SHRI T.M. SELVAGANAPATHI:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) whether it is true that the Government has approved opening of 85 new
Kendriya Vidyalayas across the country and if so, the details thereof;
(b) whether it is true that the total funds for setting up of these schools are
estimated to be Rs. 6000 crores over a period of eight years from 2025-26;
(c) whether the opening of 85 new Kendriya Vidyalayas would benefit nearly
783,000 more students; and
(d) if so, the details thereof, State-wise?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) to (d) The Government of India has recently approved the proposal for opening
of 85 new Kendriya Vidyalayas (KVs) under Civil/Defence Sector across the country
and expansion of one existing nearby KV i.e. KV Shivamogga, District Shivamogga,
Karnataka by adding 2 additional Sections in all the classes with an total estimated
outlay of approx. Rs. 5872.08 crore. Establishment of these 85 new KVs and
expansion of one existing KV would additionally benefit approximately 82560
03.02.2025 503
students across the country to get opportunity to avail of affordable quality education.
State/UT-wise list of these KVs are enclosed as STATEMENT.

FLNAT UNDER ULLAS
82. SHRI BASAVARAJ BOMMAI:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) the number of learners who have been registered and appeared for the
Foundational Literacy and Numeracy Assessment Test (FLNAT) conducted under
Understanding of Lifelong Learning for All in Society (ULLAS) - Nav Bharat
Saaksharta Karyakram;
(b) the number of participants who have been certified in FLNAT, State-wise and
district-wise, especially in Karnataka; and
(c) whether any initiatives have been taken by the Government under the ULLAS
programme to support multilingual testing and preserve linguistic diversity in
alignment with New Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and if so, the details thereof?
03.02.2025 508
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) to (c) The New India Literacy Programme (NILP) popularly known as ULLAS -
Nav Bharat Saaksharta Karyakram, is being implemented from FY 2022-23 to 2026-
27. The scheme aligns with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 and targets
non-literates aged 15 years and above who have missed formal schooling and
provide educational opportunities to them to make them literate.
A total number of 1,10,69,400 learners have been registered and appeared in
the FLNAT under the scheme of ULLAS – Nav Bharat Saaksharta Karyakram till
now. The Statements regarding number of participants who have been certified in
FLNAT, both state-wise and district-wise especially in Karnataka are annexed at
STATEMENT-I and STATEMENT-II respectively.
Under the ULLAS scheme, the ULLAS Primer i.e. the learning teaching
material has been developed in 26 languages. The FLNAT (Foundational Literacy
and Numeracy Assessment Test) takes place in regional languages.

STRENGTHENING SPORTS INFRASTRUCTURE IN RAEBARELI
83. SHRI RAHUL GANDHI:
Will the Minister of YOUTH AFFAIRS AND SPORTS be pleased to state:
(a) whether any initiatives are being taken by the Government to strengthen the
sports infrastructure in Raebareli;
(b) if so, the details thereof;
(c) whether any proposals are under consideration of the Government to augment
sports facilities in Raebareli and if so, the details thereof;
(d) whether any initiatives are being taken by the Government to promote sports
among school-children, women and differently abled persons in Raebareli; and
(e) if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT; AND MINISTER OF YOUTH
AFFAIRS AND SPORTS (DR. MANSUKH MANDAVIYA):
03.02.2025 513
(a) and (b) ‘Sports’ being a State subject, the responsibility of development of sports,
including strengthening of sports infrastructure in the country, rests primarily with the
State/Union Territory Governments. The Union Government supplements their
efforts by bridging critical gaps. However, one project, namely, Construction of Multi-
Purpose Hall at Jaitpur Police line road District Raebareli, Uttar Pradesh, has been
sanctioned under the Khelo India Scheme in Financial Year 2020-21. The details of
the sports infrastructure sanctioned under Khelo India Scheme across the country,
including in Raebareli, Uttar Pradesh, are available in public domain on the
dashboard of the Ministry at https://mdsd.kheloindia.gov.in.
(c) There is no such proposal is under consideration in this Ministry.
(d) and (e) The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports implements the following
schemes to promote sports in the country, including among school-children, women
and differently abled persons in Raebareli:
(i) Scheme of “Khelo India- National Programme for Development of Sports”; (ii)
Assistance to National Sports Federations; (iii) Special Awards to Winners in
International Sports Events and their Coaches; (iv) National Sports Awards; (v)
Pension to Meritorious Sportspersons; (vi) Pandit Deendayal Upadhyay National
Sports Welfare Scheme; (vii) National Sports Development Fund; and (viii) Sports
Training Centres through Sports Authority of India.
Details of the above schemes are available in the public domain on the websites of
this Ministry and the Sports Authority of India.

84. SHRI THARANIVENTHAN M S:
Will the Minister of YOUTH AFFAIRS AND SPORTS be pleased to state:
03.02.2025 514
(a) whether the Government has planned to establish National Centres of
Excellence especially for women across the country;
(b) if so, the details thereof;
(c) the details of the funds estimated for these centres;
(d) whether the Government has identified the locations for these centres across the
country;
(e) if so, the details thereof including Tamil Nadu; and
(f) the time by which these are likely to be established?
THE MINISTER OF LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT; AND MINISTER OF YOUTH

athletes.
(d) to (f) Does not arise.
ILLICIT TRAFFICKING OF CULTURAL PROPERTY
85.SHRI K C VENUGOPAL:
Will the Minister of CULTURE be pleased to state:-
(a) whether Government has taken necessary steps to reduce illicit trafficking of
cultural property by 2030, regulate online trading platforms and raise public
awareness about restitution laws and conventions;
(b) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor; and
03.02.2025 515
(c) the efforts made/being made by the Government to protect and restore cultural
property?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI GAJENDRA
SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) and (b) The Government is taking necessary steps towards the prevention of illicit
trafficking of cultural property. Besides regular watch and ward staff, Private Security
Guards and Central Industrial Security Force have been deployed as per
requirement at monuments, sites and museums. Whenever any theft of antiquity is
reported, FIR is lodged and ‘Look Out Notice’ is issued to law enforcement agencies
including Custom Exit Channels to keep vigil to trace stolen antiquity and prevent its
export. A Cultural Property Agreement (CPA) has also been signed with USAon 26th
July 2024 that will make easy retrieval of antiquities.
Exhibitions and workshops are organized for public awareness. Recently,
exhibitions titled, “Re(ad)dress: Return of Treasures” was held during the 46th
Session ofWorld Heritage Committee Meeting at New Delhi and exhibition titled,
‘Journey beyond the Borders: Return of Treasures’ was organized in Chennai as a
part of the workshop on ‘Antiquities Trafficking Prevention’. India was also
represented during UNESCO Regional Capacity Building Workshop on ‘Fighting
Illicit Trafficking of Cultural Property’.
(c) Archaeological Survey of India is committed to the protection of cultural property.
Government has retrieved 655 antiquities from foreign countries from the year 1976
to 2024, of which 642 antiquities have been retrieved since 2014.
03.02.2025 516
PARTICIPATION OF SMCS AND COMMUNITY IN EDUCATION
86. DR. AMAR SINGH:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is cognizant that strengthening School Management
Committees (SMCs) and community participation in education is vital for the
successful execution of educational policies; and
(b) if so, the details thereof and the steps proposed to be taken by the Government
in this regard?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) and (b) National Education Policy (NEP), 2020 emphasizes the participation of
community and parents in helping the child to learn appropriately and in
achievement of learning outcomes. Section 21 of Right to Education (RTE) Act, 2009
mandates the formation of School Management Committees (SMCs) in all
government and government-aided schools, ensuring 50% representation of women
and adequate representation of parents from disadvantaged groups. Para 5.11 of
NEP, 2020 states that in collaboration with parents and other key local stakeholders,
teachers will also be more involved in the governance of schools/school complexes,
including as members of the School Management Committees/School Complex
Management Committees.
Under Samagra Shiksha, a flagship scheme of the Department of School
Education and Literacy, Ministry of Education, there is a provision of Community
Mobilization under Quality and Innovation interventions which states that community
mobilization and close involvement of community members in implementation of
03.02.2025 517
Education is extremely critical as it fosters ‘bottom-up approach’ not only in effective
planning and implementation of interventions in the schools but also in effective
monitoring, evaluation and ownership of the Government programmes by the
community.
One of the major activities of Community Mobilization component is
constitution of SMC/School Management Development Committee (SMDC) in every
school which includes representative of local authorities, academicians, subject
experts, officials, representative of disadvantaged groups, women and
parents/guardians of students, in accordance with the RTE Act. In addition, following
initiatives have also been taken by the Department to connect communities with
Schools:
Vidyanjali 2.0: It encourages individuals, civil societies and corporates sector

to support schools through volunteering, mentorship, infrastructure
development, and co-scholastic activities.
Parental Awareness and Engagement Programs: Under these programs,

regular parent-teacher meetings (PTMs) and community interactions are
organized to ensure participation in student learning and school improvement.
ULLAS (Understanding Lifelong Learning for All in Society): The initiative

aims to promote literacy and skill-building by involving the community in
learning activities.
Community participation is being regularly enhanced as Jan Andolan for
various initiatives like Foundational Literacy and Numeracy, Eco clubs, Ek Ped Maa
03.02.2025 518
ke Naam, Literacy campaigns, Sports days, Innovative learning, Shiksha Saptah,
etc.
OPPORTUNITIES FOR PERSONS WITH DISABILITIES
87. SHRI AMRINDER SINGH RAJA WARRING:
Will the Minister of LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT be pleased to state:
(a) whether any measures have been adopted by the Government to encourage
employers in the Public and Private Sectors across States, particularly in Punjab to
provide more opportunities for persons with disabilities;
(b) if so, the details thereof;
(c) whether the Government has initiated any training programme for such
establishments and their staff towards disability; and
(d) if so, the details thereof and if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM
ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF LABOUR AND
EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a) to (d) Employment generation coupled with improving employability is the priority
of the Government. Government has taken various steps for generating employment
across the country including State of Punjab and to encourage employers in both
public and private sectors to provide more opportunities for persons including those
with disabilities(PwD) through different employment generation schemes/
programmes, such as Pradhan Mantri Mudra Yojana (PMMY), Aatmanirbhar Bharat
Rojgar Yojana (ABRY), Stand-UP India Scheme, Startup India, Prime Minister’s
Employment Generation Programme (PMEGP), Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Grameen
Kaushalya Yojana (DDU-GKY), Rural Self Employment and Training Institutes
(RSETIs), Deendayal Antyodaya Yojana-National Urban Livelihoods Mission (DAY-
03.02.2025 519
NULM), Production Linked Incentive, etc. The details of various employment
generation schemes/ programmes being implemented by the Government of India
may be seen at https://dge.gov.in/dge/schemes_programmes.
Further, to enhance skills of Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) and to enable
them to have gainful employment, to become self-reliant, productive members of
society, Government has launched the National Action Plan for Skill Development
of Persons with Disabilities (NAP-SDP) in March 2015. Since launch of NAP-SDP
scheme, skill training has been provided to 1.42 lakhs PwDs till date, across the
country.
The Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities has also signed
20 MoUs (non-financial) with various companies/ Job aggregators to create and
enhance the employment opportunities for persons with disabilities.
Ministry of Labour and Employment has established 24 National Career
Service Centres for Differently Abled (earlier known as Vocational Rehabilitation
Centres for Handicapped). These Centres provide Vocational Guidance, Career
Counselling, Non-formal Vocational Training, extend Referral Services. These
Centres also conduct Outreach Activities, and assists PwDs in the process of
Vocational Rehabilitation.
DEVELOPMENT OF NEW TOURIST SITES
88. SHRI SAPTAGIRI SANKAR ULAKA:
Will the Minister of TOURISM be pleased to state:
(a) the details of new tourist sites identified and developed across the country
since 2014, year-wise including their location and significance;
03.02.2025 520
(b) the total funds allocated and utilized for the development of these new tourism
projects under various Central Government schemes such as Swadesh
Darshan, PRASHAD and Dekho Apna Desh;
(c) the revenue generated from these newly developed tourist destinations, along
with their contribution to local economies and employment generation;
(d) the details of steps taken by the Government to promote these sites
domestically and internationally, including marketing strategies and
collaborations with private stakeholders; and
(e) the future plans of the Government for identifying and developing additional
tourist destinations, particularly in lesser-explored regions, to enhance India's
tourism potential?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI GAJENDRA
SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) to (e) While development and promotion of tourism destinations and products
is primarily undertaken by the concerned State Government (SG)/Union Territory
Administration (UTA), the Ministry of Tourism provides financial assistance to
States/UT Administration and Central Agencies under its schemes of ‘Swadesh
Darshan (SD)’, ‘Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual, Heritage Augmentation Drive
(PRASHAD)’ and Assistance to central Agencies.
Ministry of Tourism has revamped the Swadesh Darshan Scheme as Swadesh
Darshan 2.0 (SD2.0) with the objective to develop sustainable and responsible
tourism destinations.
Further, Under ‘Scheme for Special Assistance to States/Union Territories for
Capital Investment (SASCI) - Development of Iconic Tourist Centers to Global
03.02.2025 521
Scale’, Government of India has recently sanctioned 40 Projects in the country for
Rs.3295.76 Crore.
New sites, as identified by the SG/UTA are developed under these schemes. The
details of the projects sanctioned in the country under SD, SD 2.0, PRASHAD,
Assistance to Central Agencies and SASCI schemes is enclosed as STATEMENT.
Proposals are received from States/UTs from time to time for seeking financial
assistance under the various schemes of the Ministry of Tourism. These proposals
are extended for projects subject to fulfillment of the stipulated provisions and
availability of funds.
The responsibility of Operation and Maintenance of the projects developed under
various schemes rests with the concerned State/UT and the Ministry does not keep
record of the revenue generated by the respective State/UT through these projects.
However, the Ministry advises State Governments and Union Territories to ensure
due consultation with the local communities in preparing the projects and emphasis
is given on adoption of principles of economic sustainability so that the local
economy and local communities can benefit in terms of employment. The Ministry
also advises the SG/UTA to address the problem of overcrowding at a destination
and encourages them to promote lesser known destinations so that the tourism
potential of the country is tapped.
The Ministry of Tourism had launched the Dekho Apna Desh initiative in January
2020 for promotion of domestic tourism in the country. Under this initiative, the
Ministry promotes tourism destinations and products of India through various
activities such as Webinars, Quiz, Pledge, Seminars, Tourism Promotional Events,
Fam tours, Website, Social Media etc.
03.02.2025 522
Ministry of Tourism promotes tourism in a holistic manner. It releases domestic and
international print, electronic and online media campaigns. To promote various
tourism destinations of the country, it also organizes and extends support to tourism
related events, produces brochures, leaflets, maps, films and CDs on various
destinations, provides financial assistance to tourism service providers for
undertaking promotional activities.

DEVELOPMENT OF LIBRARIES
89. SHRI AZAD KIRTI JHA
Will the Minister of CULTURE be pleased to state:
(a)whether the Government has taken any measures to increase the number of
libraries under its purview during the last ten years, if so, the details thereof;
(b) the details of all libraries under the Ministry of Culture; and
(c)whether any funds have been allocated for the development of the existing
libraries and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI
GAJENDRA SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
03.02.2025 566
(a) Library is a State subject as per the Seventh schedule, under article 246th of
Constitution of India, and public libraries function under the administrative control
of the respective State/Union Territory Authorities.
Ministry of Culture through Raja Rammohun Roy Library Foundation
(RRRLF), an Autonomous Body under this Ministry, provides financial
assistance to public Libraries for various purpose e.g.
construction/renovation of library buildings, up-gradation of infrastructure
with furniture and equipment, modernization, procurement of books and
reading resources, organization of seminar/training and capacity building of
library professionals / workshop / outreach programmes, establishment of
children corner, opening of various sections, creating facilities for specially-
abled, brail corner etc.
Number of Library Assisted by RRRLF in last 10 years: -
Sl Year No. of
No. libraries
Assisted
1. 2014-15 19,707
2. 2015-16 23,306
3. 2016-17 22,744
4. 2017-18 19,084
5. 2018-19 12,070
6. 2019-20 6,735
03.02.2025 567
7. 2020-21 24,831
8. 2021-22 24,106
9. 2022-23 4,675
10. 2023-24 7,010
National Mission on Libraries (NML) Scheme, under this Ministry also
provides Financial Assistance for i) Up-gradation of Infrastructure, ii)
Modernization of Technology and Modernization of Services, iii) Creation of
Facilities for Specially abled users and iv) Subscription of E-journals/ E-
books for selected Libraries under NML, v) Network Connectivity for District
Libraries, vi) Procurement of Reading resources and Advocacy and
Outreach programs etc. to 35 State Central Libraries, 35 District Libraries
selected by the State Government of each State / UT and 6 libraries
identified by this Ministry. Under this Scheme, State Governments are
required to share the expenditure with National Mission on Libraries (NML)
in the form of Central Fund in the ratio of 25%: 75% for the other States and
in the ratio of 10%: 90% for NE States.
(b) There are 7 MoC libraries/organizations under the Ministry of Culture as follows:-
i. National Library, Kolkata
ii. Central Reference Library, Kolkata
iii. Central Secretariat Library, New Delhi
iv. Delhi Public Library, Delhi
v. Rampur Raza Library, Rampur. U. P.
03.02.2025 568
vi. Khuda Bakhsh Oriental Public Library, Patna, Bihar
vii. Raja Rammohun Roy Library Foundation, Kolkata
(c) With reference to (b) above, grants are given to these organizations by the
Ministry to meet their administrative as well as development needs. The details of
funds allocated to these organizations during the last 5 years may be seen at
STATEMENT.

(cid:255)ितभूित उ(cid:194)पाद(cid:332) के िलए कागज के िनमा(cid:170)ण/उ(cid:194)पादन का काय(cid:170) करता है। भारतीय (cid:255)ितभूित म(cid:254)ु ण तथा
म(cid:254)ु ा िनमा(cid:170)ण िनगम िलिमटेड भारत सरकार क(cid:236) पूण(cid:170) (cid:214)वािम(cid:194)व वाली कं पनी है। एसपीएम, नम(cid:170)दापरु म के
अलावा ब(cid:167)क नोट पेपर िमल इंिडया (cid:255)ाइवेट िलिमटेड (बीएनपीएमआईपीएल) मैसूर भी ब(cid:167)क नोट(cid:332) के िलए

भारतीय (cid:229)रजव(cid:170) ब(cid:167)क (आरबीआई) क(cid:236) सहायक कं पनी है, का एक संय(cid:265)ु उ(cid:299)म है।

लेनदने क(cid:236) (cid:255)विृ (cid:176), अ(cid:198)य कारखाने के िव(cid:214)तार क(cid:236) तलु ना म(cid:164) उपय(cid:265)ु ता आिद जैसे िविभ(cid:198)न कारक(cid:332) पर

अपनाने के िलए इन कारक(cid:332) को (cid:197)यान म(cid:164) रखती है।

92. DR. GANAPATHY RAJKUMAR P:

THE MINISTER OF LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT; AND MINISTER OF YOUTH
AFFAIRS AND SPORTS (DR. MANSUKH MANDAVIYA):
