SKILL DEVELOPMENT CENTRES IN KALAHANDI AND NUAPADA
169. SHRIMATI MALVIKA DEVI:
Will the Minister of SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND ENTREPRENEURSHIP be
pleased to state:-
(a) the details of the total number of skill development centres that will be set up
in Kalahandi and Nuapada for helping the youth under the Pradhan Mantri Kaushal
Vikas Yojana;
(b) the steps being taken by the Government to encourage specific skill
development programmes to help the unemployed youth of Kalahandi and Nuapada;
and
(c) the details of the skill development programmes to encourage the socially
backward SC/ST in the said districts?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) is implementing
its flagship scheme Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana (PMKVY), since 2015, for
03.02.2025 855
imparting skill development training across the country including Kalahandi and
Nuapada districts of Odisha through Short-Term Training (STT) and up-skilling and
re-skilling through Recognition of Prior Learning (RPL) to youth across the country
with aim to enhance the employability of the youth to access employment
opportunities by equipping them with industry relevant skills.
Under PMKVY 4.0, the latest version of the scheme implemented since 2022,
a demand-driven approach has been adopted for the empanelment of Training
Centres through the process of accreditation and affiliation through online portal Skill
India Digital Hub to enhance skill development among candidates. As on
31.12.2024. 01 Training centre has been empanelled in Kalahandi district and 03
Training centres have been empanelled in Nuapada District of Odisha for skilling in
STT type trainings under PMKVY 4.0.
(b) Under the PMKVY scheme, placements were tracked in the Short-Term
Training (STT) component in the first three versions of the Scheme which is PMKVY
1.0, PMKVY 2.0 and PMKVY 3.0 implemented from FY 2015-16 to FY 2021-22.
Under PMKVY 4.0, the focus was to empower our trained candidates to choose their
varied career path and they are suitably oriented for the same. Further various IT
tools like skill India Digital Hub (SIDH) also gives this opportunity.
Under the PMKVY scheme, as on 31.12.2024 a total of 14,343 candidates
have been trained/ oriented in Kalahandi District of Odisha, out of which, 1386
candidates have been reported placed during the first three versions of PMKVY.
Further, 5,338 candidates have been trained/ oriented in Nuapada District of Odisha,
out of which, 1028 candidates have been reported placed during the first three
versions of PMKVY.
03.02.2025 856
(c) Under PMKVY 4.0, National Skill Qualification Framework (NSQF)-aligned
courses are offered to enhance industry-relevant skills. Short-Term Training (STT)
provides courses ranging from 300 to 600 hours, while Recognition of Prior Learning
(RPL) certifies candidates with prior experience or skills through assessments,
offering courses between 30 to 132 hours. This initiative ensures youth gain valuable
skill certifications, improving their employability and career prospects.
Under the PMKVY scheme, as on 31.12.2024 details of SC / ST candidates
trained/ oriented and reported placed during the first three versions of PMKVY in the
said two districts is as under:
District No of SC Candidates No of ST Candidates
Trained/ Reported Trained/ Reported
Oriented placed under Oriented placed under
under PMKVY first three under PMKVY first three
since versions of since versions of
inception PMKVY inception PMKVY
Kalahandi 1562 222 1661 112
Nuapada 775 239 811 159
DROP IN ENROLLMENT RATE IN SCHOOLS
170. DR. RANI SRIKUMAR:
PROF. SOUGATA RAY:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
03.02.2025 857
(a) whether the Government is aware of the decline in enrollment rate of students in
school as per Unified District Information System for Education Plus (UDISE+) report;
(b) the number of children enrolled in schools across the country during the last 10 years,
year-wise and State-wise;
(c) the ratio of enrollment of girls and boys in the country during the last 10 years, year-
wise and State-wise;
(d) the ratio of the boys and girls drop-outs from school in the country during the last ten
years, year-wise and State-wise;
(e) the reasons for the drastic fall in enrollment of children in school;
(f) whether the school drop-out rate is going worse in some States; and
(g) whether the Government has taken any steps in this regard, if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) The Department of School Education and Literacy (DoSEL), Ministry of Education
has developed the Unified District Information System for Education Plus (UDISE+)
system to record data on indicators of school education provided by all the States and
Union Territories. As per UDISE+, State/UT-wise Gross Enrolment Ratio from the year
2020-21 to 2023-24 are available at STATEMENT-I at
https://www.education.gov.in/parl_ques.
(b) As per UDISE+, State/UT-wise number of children enrolled in schools across the
country from the year 2014-15 to 2023-24 are available at STATEMENT-II at
https://www.education.gov.in/parl_ques.
03.02.2025 858
(c) Gender Parity Index (GPI) is the ratio of GER of girls to boys. As per UDISE+,
State/UT-wise GPI from the year 2014-15 to 2023-24 are available at STATEMENT-III
at https://www.education.gov.in/parl_ques.
(d) As per UDISE+, State/UT-wise ratio of boys and girls drop-outs from school in the
country from the year 2014-15 to 2023-24 are available at STATEMENT-IV at
https://www.education.gov.in/parl_ques.
(e) to (g) UDISE+ Data Capture Format (DCF) has been aligned with NEP 2020, with
effect from 2022-23 to collect individual student wise data and to create student’s registry
for maintain dropout and learning outcome.
Further, Education is in the concurrent list of the Constitution and majority of the
schools are under the domain of respective State and UT Governments. Central
Government assist States and UTs through the Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Samagra
Shiksha. Financial assistance is also provided to the States and UTs for various activities
to reduce dropout in Govt. schools, which include opening/strengthening of new schools
up to senior secondary level, strengthening school infrastructure, setting up, up gradation
and running of Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalayas (KGBV) upto class 12, setting up of
residential schools/hostel named Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose Avasiya Vidyalayas,
transport allowance, undertaking enrolment drives, seasonal hostels/residential camps,
provision of vocational education and ICT facilities in schools, providing free text books
and free uniform, transport/escort facility, etc, financial assistance is also provided for
aids and appliances for children with special needs.
03.02.2025 859
ESTABLISHMENT OF IIT IN NASHIK
171. SHRI RAJABHAU PARAG PRAKASH WAJE:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government will consider establishing an Indian Institute of
Technology (IIT) in Nashik, given its location, cultural heritage and growing industrial
base;
(b) the manner in which the Government views the potential impact of an IIT in
Nashik on local job creation, entrepreneurship and the economy enhancing Nashik’s
status as an educational and innovation hub in Maharashtra;
(c) whether the Government has conducted/proposed to conduct any study to assess
the benefits of sitting up an IIT in Nashik, aligning with the Government’s focus on
education and innovation growth;
(d) whether there is any criteria in terms of numbers of IITs and IIMs set up in a State,
if so, the details thereof; and
(e) whether the size of the State in terms of area and population is considered in
deciding this number?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF EDUCATION; AND MINISTER OF
STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF DEVELOPMENT OF NORTH EASTERN REGION (DR.
SUKANTA MAJUMDAR):
(a) to (e) 16 IITs were functioning in the country till 2014. In pursuance to Budget
Announcement 2014-15 and 2015-16, six new IITs have been established in the
States/UT of Andhra Pradesh (IIT Tirupati), Kerala (IIT Palakkad), Chhattisgarh (IIT
Bhilai), Jammu and Kashmir (IIT Jammu), Goa (IIT Goa) and Karnataka (IIT
03.02.2025 860
Dharwad). Further, in pursuance to the Budget announcement 2015-16, the Indian
School of Mines, Dhanbad (ISM Dhanbad) was accorded the status of IIT in the year
of 2016. At present there are 23 IITs and 21 IIMs functioning in the Country including
IIT Bombay, IIM Mumbai and IIM Nagpur in State of Maharashtra.
IIT Bombay (Indian Institute of Technology Bombay) is one of India’s most
prestigious engineering and research institutions, established in 1958. Institute has
consistently been a leader in academics, research, and innovation with more than
14200 student strength. In the QS World University Rankings 2025, IIT Bombay has
achieved notable position and placed at 118th Rank.
With an aim to ensure high quality education in Maharashtra , Government
of India has established several premier Institutes like Visvesvaraya National
Institute of Technology, Nagpur (VNIT Nagpur); Indian Institute of Management,
Nagpur (IIM Nagpur); Indian Institute of Information Technology, Nagpur (IIIT
Nagpur); Indian Institute of Information Technology, Pune (IIIT Pune); Mahatma
Gandhi Antarrashtriya Hindi Viswavidyalaya, Wardha and Indian Institute of Science
Education and Research, Pune (IISER Pune). Further, National Institute of Industrial
Engineering (NITIE) in Mumbai has been converted into the Indian Institute of
Management Mumbai (IIM Mumbai), in 2023.
DEARNESS ALLOWANCE OF CENTRAL GOVERNMENT EMPLOYEES
DURING COVID-19
172.SHRI ANAND BHADAURIA :
Will the Minister of FINANCE be pleased to state :
03.02.2025 861
(a) Whether dearness allowance and dearness relief for Central Government
employees and pensioners during the Covid-19 were withheld for 18 months but
not seized;
(b) If so, the details thereof and the reasons for its seizure by the Government;
(c) Whether the Government would release the same to ensure spending by the
Government employees and thereby help in boosting the faltering economy of the
Country;
(d) If so, the details thereof ;
(e) If not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF FINANCE (SHRI PANKAJ
CHAUDHARY):
(a)and (b) The decision to freeze three installments of Dearness Allowance (DA) /
Dearness Relief (DR) to Central Government employees/ pensioners due from
01.01.2020, 01.07.2020 and 01.01.2021 was taken in the context of COVID-19,
which caused economic disruption, so as to ease pressure on Government finances.
(a)No.
(d) Does not arise.
(e) The adverse financial impact of pandemic in 2020 and the financing of welfare
measures taken by the Government had a fiscal spill over beyond FY 2020-21.
Therefore, arrears of DA/DR were not considered feasible.

BIRDS POPULATION IN THE COUNTRY
175. SHRI JAGDAMBIKA PAL:
Will the Minister of ENVIRONMENT, FOREST AND CLIMATE CHANGE be
pleased to state:
(a) the current status of bird populations in the country over the past decade,
particularly among species that rely on vertebrates, carrion, and
invertebrates;
(b) whether bird species endemic to the Western Ghats, The Himalayas, The
Indo-Burma region and The Sundaland biodiversity hotspot, such as the
Great Gray Shrike, Sarus Crane, Yellow-crowned Woodpecker,
experienced significant declines in their numbers during the said period, if
so, the details thereof;
(c) the details of initiatives taken by the Government during the said period to
address the challenges facing bird populations, particularly those at risk
due to habitat loss and changing ecological conditions; and
(d) whether there are any notable improvements in the population of specific
bird species, particularly those at risk, as a result of the Government's
conservation efforts and initiatives, if so, the details thereof?
03.02.2025 865
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT, FOREST
AND CLIMATE CHANGE; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EXTERNAL AFFAIRS (SHRI KIRTI VARDHAN SINGH):
(a) To (d) The Ministry undertakes population estimation of flagship
species like Asian Elephant, Tiger, Leopard and Snow Leopard.
Population assessments are also carried out by the respective
States/Union Territories for many other wild animals including birds.
The steps taken by the Ministry for protection of wildlife including bird
species of the country include:
i. The Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972 lists 208 species of birds
in Schedule I and 1134 species of birds in Schedule-II, thereby
according them protection from hunting.
ii. Protected Areas, viz., National Parks, Wildlife Sanctuaries,
Conservation Reserves and Community Reserves covering
important wildlife habitats have been created all over the
country under the Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972.
iii. The ministry has notified the Wetlands (Conservation and
Management) rules, 2017, for better protection of wetlands in
the country.
iv. A specific component of ‘Recovery programmes for saving
critically endangered species and habitats’ is included in the
ongoing Centrally Sponsored Scheme of ‘Development of
Wildlife Habitats’ for focused conservation action on 22
03.02.2025 866
critically endangered species including Bustards, Edible nest
Swiftlets, Nicobar Megapode, Jerdon’s Courser and Vultures.
v. Financial and technical assistance is provided to the State/UT
governments for protection and management of Protected
Areas under the Centrally Sponsored Scheme of
‘Development of Wildlife Habitats’.
SCHOOL DROP-OUTS AGED 6 TO 14 YEARS
176. SHRI K RADHAKRISHNAN:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has taken any assessment of the drop-out of children
between 6 and 14 years of age from school during the last three years;
(b) if so, the details thereof; and
(c) whether the current budget includes any specific plan for bringing out-of-school
children back to school, if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) and (b) Education is in the Concurrent List of the Constitution and majority of
the schools come under the domain of the State/UT Governments. Under the
Samagra Shiksha scheme, the annual plans are prepared by the States and UTs
based on their requirements and priority and this is reflected in their respective
Annual Work Plan and Budget (AWPandB) Proposals. During the appraisal of
AWPandB proposals, all States and UTs are requested to conduct household survey
03.02.2025 867
annually for the purpose of identification of Out of School Children (OoSC). This
includes dropout children and never enrolled. The identified OoSC are brought back
to school and assessed for their learning gaps.
This Department has also developed an online module for compiling the data
of Out of School Children (OoSC) identified by each State/UT and their mapping with
Special Training Centres (STC) on the PRABANDH Portal. The concerned State/UT
validates the child wise information of the identified OoSC and STC uploaded by the
concerned Block Resource Centre of the State for monitoring the progress of
mainstreaming of OoSC. The drop out rate of children at elementary level for the
last three years is as under:
Year Primary (1 to 5) Upper Primary ( 6 to
8)
2021-22 1.5 3
2022-23 7.8 8.1
2023-24 1.9 5.2
Source: UDISE+
(c) Under the Samagra Shiksha scheme, financial assistance is provided to
States and UTs for undertaking various activities for reducing the number of out of
school children. For out of school children at 16 to 19 years of age, support is
provided to SC, ST, disabled children, upto ₹ 2000 per child per grade to complete
their secondary/senior secondary levels through NIOS/SIOS.
Further, special training for age appropriate admission of out of school
children and residential as well as non-residential training for older children,
seasonal hostels / residential camps, special training centres at worksites, transport/
03.02.2025 868
escort facility are also supported as follows to bring out of school children to the
formal schooling:
Up to ₹ 6,000/- per child per annum for non-residential courses.
 Up to ₹ 20,000/- per child per annum for residential courses.
The data in respect of out of school’ children (OoSC) for the last three years
is as under:

FOREIGN UNIVERSITIES IN INDIA UNDER UGC GUIDELINES
179. SHRI APPALANAIDU KALISETTI:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) the details of number of foreign universities that have applied to establish
campuses in India under the University Grants Commission (UGC) guidelines,
State-wise, particularly for Andhra Pradesh;
(b) the details of the current status of these applications, including the number of
institutions that have been granted approval and issued a letter of intent under these
guidelines with a focus on Andhra Pradesh;
(c) the proposed academic infrastructure and programs that these foreign institutions
plan to offer in India;
(d) the details of funds allocated by the Government to support the establishment of
these foreign campuses in the country, State-wise with specific attention to funds
allocated to Andhra Pradesh; and
(e) the oversight mechanisms put in place by the Government under the UGC
guidelines to monitor the functioning of these foreign campuses in India?
03.02.2025 879
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF EDUCATION; AND MINISTER
OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF DEVELOPMENT OF NORTH EASTERN
REGION (DR. SUKANTA MAJUMDAR):
(a) to (c) The University Grants Commission (UGC) has notified the UGC (Setting
up and operation of Campuses of Foreign Higher Educational Institutions in India)
Regulations, 2023. As informed by UGC, it has received online applications,
complete in all respects, on its portal for setting up of their campuses in India from
the University of Southampton, UK; Lincoln University, Malaysia; Life Chiropractic
College West, USA; and Istituto Europeo di Design, Italy. The application of
University of Southampton, UK has been approved and Letter of Intent has been
issued by UGC.
As per the application received from University of Southampton, the University
plans to have its campus in Gurugram, Haryana and to offer courses in subjects
focusing on business and management, computing, law, engineering, art and
design, biosciences and life sciences.
(d) The aforesaid UGC Regulations, 2023 do not specify for allocation of funds by
the Government to support establishment of campuses of Foreign Higher
Educational Institutions in India.
(e) Clause 10 of the aforesaid UGC Regulations, 2023 provides that the UGC shall
have the power to visit the campus and examine its operations to ascertain the
infrastructure, academic programmes and overall quality and suitability.
03.02.2025 880
कौशल िवकास क(cid:164) (cid:254)(cid:332) के मा(cid:197)यम से रोजगार के अवसर
180. (cid:174)ीमती गनीबेन नागाजी ठाकोर:
(cid:179)या कौशल िवकास और उ(cid:299)मशीलता म(cid:253)ं ी यह बताने क(cid:236) कृपा करग(cid:164) े िक:
(क) (cid:179)या कौशल िवकास क(cid:164) (cid:254)(cid:332) के मा(cid:197)यम से रोजगार के अवसर उपल(cid:202)ध कराए जा रहे ह(cid:167); और
(ख) यिद हां, तो गजु रात म(cid:164) िपछले तीन वष(cid:334) के दौरान उ(cid:265) क(cid:164) (cid:254)(cid:332) के मा(cid:197)यम से िजलेवार कुल िकतने

NATIONAL ADAPTATION FUND FOR CLIMATE CHANGE (NAFCC)
181. DR. SHASHI THAROOR:
Will the Minister of ENVIRONMENT, FOREST AND CLIMATE CHANGE be pleased
to state:
(a) whether the projects under National Adaptation Fund for Climate Change (NAFCC)
are decided based on the areas identified under the State Action Plan on Climate
Change (SAPCC);
03.02.2025 893
(b) if so, the areas identified as most vulnerable by Kerala’s State Adaptation Fund for
Climate Change 2023-2030 (Kerala’s SAPCC 2.0);
(c) whether it is true that NAFCC’s sanctioned project in Kerala, (Promotion of
Integrated Farming system of Kaipad Pokkali in Coastal Wetlands of Kerala) only
includes two (Kannur and Alappuzha) of the nine identified districts; and
(d) if so, whether the Union Government in coordination with the State Government of
Kerala, is considering to sanction additional projects under the NAFCC covering
other districts in Kerala identified as most vulnerable by the SAPCC?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT, FOREST AND
CLIMATE CHANGE; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EXTERNAL AFFAIRS (SHRI KIRTI VARDHAN SINGH):
(a) One of the major objectives of the National Adaptation Fund for Climate Change
(NAFCC) is to fund concrete climate adaptation projects/programmes aligned with the
relevant Missions under National Action Plan on Climate Change (NAPCC) and the
State Action Plans on Climate Change (SAPCCs) to address climate change related
issues. The respective State Governments in alignment with their SAPCCs formulated
projects under NAFCC.
(b) As per Kerala’s SAPCC 2.0, the following districts have been identified as highly
vulnerable, sector wise:

Palakkad, Wayanad, Kasaragod, Idukki, Kozhikode, Malappuram, Kottayam,
Kannur and Thiruvananthapuram in terms of agriculture sector;

Palakkad, Kasaragod, Idukki and Kollam in terms of livestock;
03.02.2025 894

Kasaragod, Kannur, Malappuram and Thiruvananthapuram in terms of coastal
fisheries;

Kollam, Kottayam, Wayanad and Kasaragod in terms of forest;

Kasaragod, Malappuram, and Palakkad in terms of human health;

Wayanad, Alappuzha, Kottayam and Kozhikode in terms of water sector.
(c) and (d) The NAFCC project “Promotion of Integrated Farming System of Kaipad and
Pokkali in coastal wetlands of Kerala” was implemented in the districts of Kannur,
Alappuzha, Thrissur, and Ernakulam. The NAFCC was established in 2015 for scaling
up of concrete and pilot adaptation interventions in alignment with NAPCC and SAPCCs.
Various components of the individual projects financed under NAFCC meant towards
achievement of that objective. Union Budget 2020-21 states that India is implementing
its climate actions in various sectors through the normal budgetary process with the
participatory support from the stakeholders. The learnings are mainstreamed into the
respective SAPCCs.
PROMOTION OF ODISHA CULTURE IN OTHER STATES OF THE
COUNTRY
182. SHRI SUKANTA KUMAR PANIGRAHI:
Will the Minister of CULTURE be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the current programmes and initiatives taken by the
Government to promote Odisha Culture in other States of the country
03.02.2025 895
especially key Heritage Sites, Temples, Monuments, Tribal art and Centres
etc.;
(b) whether the Government has any plans to organize an inter-State cultural
exchange progamme, if so, the details thereof and the methods to be used
for these initiatives;
(c) whether the Union Government proposes to extend financial and logistics
support for promoting unique Tribal extravaganza under National Culture
fund; and
(d) the funds allocated for promoting Odisha Culture and Festivals in the current
financial year including proposal for next four years?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI GAJENDRA
SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) and (b) Government, under National Cultural Exchange
Programme, organizes programmes/festivals through Zonal Cultural
Centres (ZCCs), where artists from other zones/states are invited to
participate and audiences get the chance to enjoy and understand art
forms of different regions.
Further, Archaeological Survey of India organizes cultural awareness
programmes on various occasions like World Heritage Day, World
Heritage Week, International Museums Day, Republic Day,
Independence Day, Gandhi Jayanti, etc. through exhibitions,
workshops and other programmes involving school and college
03.02.2025 896
students and general public to promote culture and monuments
including monuments of Odisha in various states of the country.
(c) and (d) National Culture Fund (NCF) set up by Government, is
mandated to preserve and promote tangible and intangible culture
heritage of India, including tribal cultural heritage.
Ministry of Culture is administering two schemes namely “Cultural
Function and Production Grant Scheme” and “Scheme of Scholarship
and Fellowship for Promotion of Art and Culture” wherein financial
assistance is provided to the eligible beneficiaries with the broader aim
of Promotion and Preservation of Art and Culture. Brief of these
schemes alongwith number of beneficiaries (belonging to the State of
Odisha) supported during last three years and current financial year
are at STATEMENT.
STATEMENT
BRIEF OF SCHEMES ALONGWITH NUMBER OF BENEFICIARIES (BELONGING TO
THE STATE OF ODISHA) SUPPORTED DURING LAST THREE YEARS AND
CURRENT FINANCIAL YEAR
1. Cultural Function and Production Grant Scheme
Under the scheme of Cultural Function and Production Grant (CFPG), financial
assistance is provided to NGOs/ Societies/ Trusts/ Universities for organizing Seminars,
Conference, Research, Workshops, Festivals, Exhibitions, Symposia, Production of
Dance, Drama-Theatre, Music etc. The details of the number of organizations and
03.02.2025 897
financial assistance provided during last 03 years in the State of Odisha under the
aforesaid scheme are given below: -

108 171.40 89 129.60 90 93.60 33 55.80
2. Scheme of Scholarship and Fellowship for Promotion of Art and Culture: The
scheme consists of three components. The names and details of the scheme
components are as given below:
A. Scheme for Scholarships to Young Artists in Different Cultural Fields:
Upto 400 Scholarships are awarded in a batch year. Under this scheme
financial assistance is given to young artists of outstanding promise in the
age group of 18-25 years for advanced training within India in the field of
Indian Classical Music, Indian Classical Dance, Theatre, Mime, Visual art,
Folk, Traditional and Indigenous Arts and Light Classical Music etc. @ Rs.
5,000/- p.m. for 2 years. The Scholarship is released in four equal six
monthly installments.
03.02.2025 898
B. Scheme For The Award Of Fellowship To Outstanding Persons in The
Field Of Culture: Upto 400 fellowships (200 Junior and 200 Senior) are
awarded in a batch year to outstanding persons in the age group of 25 to 40
years (Jr.) and above 40 years (Sr.) in different cultural fields @ of Rs.
10,000/- p.m. and 20,000/-p.m. for the period of 2 years for cultural research.
The Fellowship is released in four equal six monthly installments.
C. Tagore National Fellowship for Cultural Research: The purpose of the
Scheme Component is to invigorate and revitalize the various institutions
under the Ministry of Culture (MoC) and other identified cultural institutions
in the country, by encouraging scholars/academicians to affiliate themselves
with these institutions to work on projects of mutual interest. Upto 15
Fellowships (Rs.80,000/- p.m. + Contingency Allowance) and 25
Scholarships (Rs.50,000/- p.m. + Contingency Allowance) for a maximum
period of 2 Years. The Fellowship is released in four equal six monthly
installments.
The details of the number of organizations and financial assistance provided
during last 03 years in the State of Odisha under the aforesaid scheme are given below:

(b) if so, the details thereof;
(c) whether the Union Government has any plan to announce such incentives to the

(d) if so, the details thereof; and
(e) if not, the reasons therefor?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM

EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a) and (b) Labour being in the Concurrent List, the data is not maintained centrally.

and Employment.

IMPLEMENTATION OF SASCI SCHEME WITH SPECIAL REFERENCE TO PALI
REGION
184. SHRI P P CHAUDHARY:
Will the Minister of TOURISM be pleased to state:
(a) the specific criteria used for selection of projects under Special Assistance to
States for Capital Expenditure/Investment (SASCI) scheme, particularly for
heritage and religious tourism sites along with the proposals if any, considered
from Pali district thereof;
(b) the strategies being implemented for development of lesser-known destinations
including plans for promoting religious tourism circuits connecting Ranakpur Jain
Temple and other heritage sites in Pali region;
(c) whether any assessment has been conducted regarding tourism potential of
lesser-known destinations in Pali district, particularly the ancient temples and
traditional craft centres and if so, the details thereof; and
(d) whether any specific international promotion initiatives have been planned to
showcase religious and cultural heritage of Pali region along with major tourist
circuits of Rajasthan and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI GAJENDRA
SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) to (d) The Ministry of Tourism issued operational guidelines for ‘Special Assistance
to States for Capital Investment – Development of Iconic Tourist Centres to Global Scale’
(SASCI) with the objective to comprehensively develop iconic tourist centres in the
country, branding and marketing them at global scale.
03.02.2025 901
Salient features of this endeavour include developing end to end tourist experience,
funding support to the shortlisted proposals, strengthening all points of the tourist value
chain, harnessing quality expertise for design and development, sustainable operations
and maintenance etc. The projects have been identified based on the project proposals
submitted by the concerned State Governments, its examination on the prescribed
parameters such as connectivity to the site, tourism eco-system, carrying capacity,
sustainability measures, sustainable operation and management, project impact and
value created, tourism marketing plans etc. The project will be implemented and
managed by the concerned State Governments.
A total number of 40 tourism related projects have been sanctioned in this scheme by
Government of India across 23 States of the country for an amount of ₹3295.76 Crore
including 2 projects in the State of Rajasthan during the financial year 2024-25 as per
the details given below:-
S. No. Interventions Name Cost in ₹ crore)
1 Development at Amber-Nahargarh and surrounding 49.31
Area, Jaipur
2 Development at Jal Mahal, Jaipur 96.61
The State Government of Rajasthan did not submit any project proposal related to Pali
Region under SASCI Scheme.
The Ministry of Tourism has not undertaken any assessment regarding tourism potential
of lesser-known destinations in Pali district. However, the Ministry under its Swadesh
Darshan and ‘Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual, Heritage Augmentation Drive
03.02.2025 902
(PRASHAD)’ complements the efforts of tourism infrastructure development of the State
Governments/UT Administrations by extending financial assistance to them in synergy
with the scheme guidelines, availability of funds etc. The detail of the projects sanctioned
under the Swadesh Darshan and PRASHAD scheme in the State of Rajasthan is
enclosed as STATEMENT.
The Ministry under Swadesh Darshan 2.0, the revamped destination centric version of
Swadesh Darshan scheme, has sanctioned the project ‘Spiritual Experience,
Keshovraipatan’ for ₹17.37 Crore in Bundi, Rajasthan.
Ministry of Tourism promotes India in a holistic manner, as a tourist destination including
destinations of Rajasthan, in domestic and international markets through its various
promotional initiatives such as website, social media promotions, participation in events,
assistance to State Governments for organizing fairs and festivals etc.

NO DETENTION POLICY
186. SHRI EATALA RAJENDER:
SHRI CHAMALA KIRAN KUMAR REDDY:
03.02.2025 905
SHRIMATI D K ARUNA:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is planning to remove ‘no detention’ policy for students from
Classes V to VIII with a aim to improve the quality of education and learning outcomes
among students;
(b) whether Gross Enrolment Ratio has increased in some States including Tamil Nadu;
(c) whether the quality of education measured through learning outcomes among school
students had been deteriorating; and
(d) if so, the details thereof, State-wise especially in Andhra Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh and
Telangana and also the current status of the same?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) The Right of Children to Free and Compulsory Education (Amendment) Act,
2019 amending sections 16 and 38 of the RTE Act, 2009, was notified on 11th January,
2019. RTE (Amendment) Act, 2019 states as under:
“16(1) There shall be a regular examination in the fifth class and in the eighth class at
the end of every academic year.
(2) If a child fails in the examination referred to in sub-section (1), he shall be given
additional instruction and granted opportunity for re-examination within a period of two
months from the date of declaration of the result.
03.02.2025 906
(3) The appropriate Government may allow schools to hold back a child in the fifth
class or in the eighth class or in both classes, in such manner and subject to such
conditions as may be prescribed, if he fails in the re-examination referred to in sub-
section (2):
Provided that the appropriate Government may decide not to hold back a child in any
class till the completion of elementary education.
(4) No child shall be expelled from a school till the completion of elementary
education.”
The Central Government has issued notification, duly amending the Right of Children
to Free and Compulsory Rules, 2010 to give effect to the amended provision of
Section 16 of the RTE Act, 2009 introduced vide RTE (Amendment) Act, 2019. The
rules are applicable to the Central Government schools and the schools established,
owned or controlled by the Union Territories having no legislature, for which the
Central Government is the appropriate Government as per section 2(a) of the RTE
Act, 2009. These rules were notified vide G.S.R 777 (E) dated 21st December, 2024.
As per Rule 16A of RTE(Amendment) Rules, 2024, the manner and conditions subject
to which a child may be held back is:
“(1) There shall be regular examination in the fifth class and in the eighth class at
the end of every academic year.
(2) After the conduct of regular examination, referred to in sub- rule (1), if a child
fails to fulfil the promotion criteria, as notified from time to time, he shall be given
03.02.2025 907
additional instruction and opportunity for re-examination within a period of two
months from the date of declaration of results.
(3) If the child appearing in the re-examination referred in sub -rule (2), fails to fulfil
the promotion criteria again, he shall be held back in fifth class or eighth class, as
the case may be.
(4) During the holding back of the child, the class teacher shall guide the child as
well as the parents of the child, if necessary, and provide specialised inputs after
identifying the learning gaps at various stages of assessment.
(5) The Head of the school shall maintain a list of children who are held back and
personally monitor the provisions provided for specialised inputs to such children
and their progress with respect to the identified learning gaps.
(6) The examination and re-examination shall be competency-based
examinations to achieve the holistic development of the child and not be based on
memorisation and procedural skills.
(7) No child shall be expelled from any school till he completes elementary
education.”
(b) As per UDISE+, the Gross Enrolment Ratio (GER) of Tamil Nadu in the year 2013-
2014 for Secondary (Class 9-10) was 73.8 which has increased to 97.5 in the year 2023-
2024. Details of GER National/State wise are available on the UDISE+ portal. The same
may be accessed at “https://dashboard.udiseplus.gov.in/udiseplus-
archive/#/reportDashboard/sReport”.
03.02.2025 908
(c) and (d) Ministry of Education has been implementing a rolling programme of sample
based National Achievement Survey (NAS) at classes 3, 5, 8 and 10 at an interval of
three years to assess children’s progress and learning competencies. The latest round
of NAS (now referred to as PARAKH Rashtriya Sarvekshan) was conducted on 4th
December, 2024 across the country to assess the competencies developed by students
at the end of foundational, preparatory and middle stages (i.e., Students currently in 3rd,
6th and 9th Grades) in the subjects, Language, Mathematics, the World Around Us,
Science and Social Science.
State-wise report card indicating the grade-wise and subject-wise performance of
students from all States/UTs including Andhra Pradesh, Telangana and Uttar Pradesh
in NAS 2017 and NAS 2021 is available in the public domain and may be accessed at
https://nas.gov.in/report-card. The NAS 2021 report card of Andhra Pradesh, Uttar
Pradesh and Telangana is enclosed as STATEMENT.

ENVIRONMENT PROTECTION (END-OF-LIFE VEHICLES) RULES, 2025
187. SHRI DHAIRYASHEEL SAMBHAJIRAO MANE:
SHRI RAVINDRA VASANTRAO CHAVAN:
SHRI SUDHEER GUPTA:
Will the Minister of ENVIRONMENT, FOREST AND CLIMATE CHANGE be pleased to
state:
(a) whether the Government has notified Environment Protection (End-of-Life
Vehicles) Rules, 2025 and if so, the details thereof and the key features/highlights of
the said Rules;
(b) the aims and objectives behind implementation of the said Rules;
(c) whether the Government has outlined the specific roles for both Central and State
agencies/authorities in implementation of these Rules and if so, the details thereof;
(d) whether the Government has started any online portal to ensure transparency in
implementation and providing real time data on the disposal and recycling of end-of-
life vehicles and if so, the details thereof; and
(e) whether the Government has established any Implementation Committee to
oversee the implementation of these Rules and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT, FOREST AND
CLIMATE CHANGE; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF EXTERNAL
AFFAIRS (SHRI KIRTI VARDHAN SINGH):
03.02.2025 912
(a) to (e) The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFandCC)
notified the Environment Protection (End-of-Life Vehicles) Rules, 2025 vide S.O. 98(E)
dated 06th January, 2025 for environmentally sound management of end-of-life vehicles.
The rules are based on the principle of extended producer responsibility (EPR) where
producers of vehicles are given mandatory EPR targets for scrapping of end-of-life
vehicles. The rules cover all types of transport and non-transport vehicles, except
agricultural tractor, agricultural trailer, combine harvester and power tiller.
Under the said rules, producers have been mandated to fulfil the obligation of Extended
Producer Responsibility for the vehicles that the producer has introduced or introduces
in the domestic market, including vehicles put to self-use to ensure the specified
scrapping targets.
Producers have been provided annual targets for scrapping of End-of-life vehicles
starting from the year 2025-26 for the vehicles placed in the market 15 years ago in case
of transport vehicles and 20 years ago in case of non-transport vehicles. They have been
mandated to fulfil the Extended Producer Responsibility either through purchase of
Extended Producer Responsibility certificate generated by its own Registered Vehicle
Scrapping Facility or by any entity having Registered Vehicle Scrapping Facility.
Producers are required to obtain registration from Central Pollution Control Board
through the centralised online portal. They are required to declare its Extended Producer
Responsibility obligations for the current year by 30th April of the same year to the
Central Board and furnish annual returns in Form 1 on centralised online portal to Central
Board by 30th June regarding vehicles placed in the market, including vehicles put to
03.02.2025 913
self-use in previous financial year and in respect of fulfilment of Extended Producer
Responsibility obligations during the previous financial year.
Producers are required to take necessary measures to encourage End-of-Life vehicles
are deposited at the Registered Vehicle Scrapping Facility or designated Collection
Centres. They must make necessary arrangements to receive the End-of-Life vehicle
from the registered owner at any designated Collection Centre, including its sales outlet
and shall upload such list of designated Collection Centre, sales outlet on its website
and at a conspicuous place of its sales outlets and service centres.
Producers are required to fulfil the Extended Producer Responsibility obligations by
floating schemes such as buy back scheme, deposit refund scheme etc, or designate
any other entity to fulfil the same. Every producer shall organise awareness campaigns
and encourage customers by offering incentives to safe deposit of End-of-Life vehicles
at the Registered Vehicle Scrapping Facility or the designated Collection Centre.
Bulk consumers and Registered Vehicle Scrapping Facilities (RVSFs) have been
mandated to obtain registration from State Pollution Control Board through the
centralised online portal. RVSFs have been mandated to receive unfit vehicles or End-
of-Life vehicles for scrapping and must carry out treatment, depolluting, dismantling,
segregation and scrapping activities.
RVSFs can exchange the Extended Producer Responsibility certificates upon
processing of every End-of Life vehicle based on the quantity of steel from the End-of-
Life vehicles on the centralized online portal for issuance of Extended Producer
Responsibility certificates by Central Board for purchase by the producers.
03.02.2025 914
Under the rules, Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has been mandated to
establish a centralized online EPR portal within six months of the publication of the Rules
for implementation of the provisions of the Rules inter-alia include registration of
Producers, Bulk consumers and RVSFs, generation and exchange of EPR certificates,
filing of returns regarding EPR obligations and scrapping of end-of-life vehicles,
uploading the list of designated collection centres for receiving End-of-life vehicles.
Central Pollution Control Board has been mandated to carry out the registration of
producers on the centralised online portal. Rules enable CPCB to issue guidelines for
handling, processing and recycling or refurbishing of materials of End-of-Life vehicles in
environmentally sound manner and for implementation of these rules. CPCB is required
to undertake periodic inspection and audit of the producer to ensure that such facility is
complying with the requirement under the provisions of these rules. CPCB may
undertake or cause to be undertaken by an authorised agency the periodic inspection
and audit of the Registered Vehicle Scrapping facility. CPCB may take necessary
actions against violations or for non-fulfilment of obligations under these rules against a
producer or Registered Vehicle Scrapping Facility or any other person.
State Government and UT Administration is required to take steps to ensure that
adequate number of RVSFs and Automated Testing Stations are established in the
respective State and Union Territory, organise awareness campaigns, and encourage
and incentivise customers to engage in safe deposition of End-of-Life vehicles at the
Registered Vehicle Scrapping Facility or the designated Collection Centre.
State Pollution Control Board have been mandated to register the RVSF and bulk
consumer on the centralized online portal. SPCB State Pollution Control Board is
03.02.2025 915
required to undertake periodic inspection and audit, or cause to be inspected by an
authorised agency, of the RVSF to ensure compliance of the rules. SPCB is required to
undertake periodic inspection and audit, or cause to be inspected by an authorised
agency, of the Registered Vehicle Scrapping Facility to ensure the compliance of these
rules and may take necessary actions against violations or for non-fulfilment of
obligations under these rules against a Registered Vehicle Scrapping Facility or bulk
consumer or any other person.
In case, producer or Registered Vehicle Scrapping Facility or bulk consumer fails
to comply with the provisions relating to handling and scrapping of End-of-Life vehicles
in environmentally sound manner under these rules, are liable to pay environmental
compensation for causing loss, damage or injury to environment or public health.
In line with the Rule 17, MoEFandCC has vide Order dated 30.01.2025 constituted
the Implementation Committee under chairpersonship of Chairman, Central Pollution
Control Board, with representatives from the concerned Ministries/ Departments/
stakeholders representing producers, recyclers and scrapping facilities, for effective
implementation of these rules.
PM EVIDYA DTH CHANNEL
188. SHRI SURESH KUMAR KASHYAP:
SHRIMATI KRITI DEVI DEBBARMAN:
SHRI CHANDRA PRAKASH JOSHI:
SHRI DINESHBHAI MAKWANA:
SHRI SHANKAR LALWANI:
03.02.2025 916
SHRI DEVUSINH CHAUHAN:
SHRI VIJAY BAGHEL:
SHRI VISHNU DAYAL RAM:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) the type of contents broadcasted by the PM eVidya DTH channel;
(b) whether the channel broadcast in regional languages also and if so, the details thereof and
if not, the reasons therefor;
(c) whether the Government plans to integrate the PM eVidya DTH with other digital education
initiatives such as Diksha platform;
(d) the expected outcomes in terms of educational accessibility for the hearing impaired
students; and
(e) the details of the outcome in Himachal Pradesh by this initiative ?
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) PM eVidya DTH TV Channels telecast primarily curriculum-based educational video
programs in Indian languages. These programs cover various subjects including
languages and vocational education as per the school curriculum prescribed by
NCERT/CBSE and the SCERTs and school education boards of the States/Union
Territories. In addition, PM eVidya DTH TV Channels also broadcast various programs
having curricular/co-curricular/ extra-curricular values such as art and craft, health
education, general knowledge, general awareness, value education, cultural heritage,
yoga, sports, etc.
03.02.2025 917
(b) PM eVidya DTH Channels currently telecast e-contents in 30 Indian languages
including Hindi and English. The other languages are 1. Assamese, 2. Bengali, 3. Galo,
4. Garo, 5. Gujarati, 6. Idu, 7. Indian Sign Language (ISL) 8. Kaman, 9. Kannada, 10.
Khasi, 11. Kok Borak, 12. Konkani, 13. Malayalam, 14. Manipuri, 15. Marathi, 16. Mizo,
17. Nepali, 18. Nyishi, 19. Odia 20. Punjabi, 21. Sanskrit, 22. Tagin, 23. Tamil, 24.
Tangsa, 25. Taraon, 26. Telugu, 27. Urdu, and 28. Wancho
(c) Yes, PM eVidya initiative is aimed at convergence of all efforts related to
digital/online/on-air education to enable multi-modal access to education in a resilient
and coherent way. To this effect, DIKSHA platform has been enabled for coherent
access of the QR code embedded educational audio/video/multimedia programs
disseminated through 200 PM eVidya DTH TV Channels and 400 radio stations and on
iRadio in a simultaneous manner.
(d) PM eVidya DTH TV Channels’ initiative includes India’s first television channel in
Indian Sign Language (ISL), on Channel # 31 to promote accessible education for
students with hearing disabilities. The expected outcomes in terms of educational
accessibility to be achieved through this novel initiative involves to disseminate learning
content for school children, teachers, teacher educators, and other stakeholders in areas
such as career guidance, skill training, mental health, class wise curricular content,
communication skills, as well as the promotion of sign language as a subject, just like
any other languages.
(e) The State of Himachal Pradesh has been allotted 5 DTH TV channels under PM
eVidya (viz. DD PM eVIDYA HP88, 89, 90, 91, and 92). As on date, the State is
03.02.2025 918
disseminating 3,683 numbers of e-content approximately equalling 638 hours of telecast
based on NCERT/State syllabus.
In DIKSHA, Himachal Pradesh has its own tenant in which around 3542 e-resources
(including 294 energized textbooks, 868 text documents, 2058 audios/videos and 320
interactive contents) have been uploaded. The DIKSHA platform use cases in Himachal
Pradesh includes 1.9 Lakhs registered users and 829 daily active users. Also using
NISHTHA courses on DIKSHA 100% teachers from Himachal Pradesh are trained on
NISHTHA ECCE (342 master trainer teachers) NISHTHA Elementary (12020 teachers)
and NISHTHA Secondary (19580 teachers) courses. In NISHTHA FLN, 5199 teachers
completed certification course through DIKSHA platform.

03.02.2025 919
पर, क(cid:164) (cid:254) सरकार (cid:300)ारा इस संबंध म(cid:164) (cid:255)ािधकृत भारतीय (cid:229)रजव(cid:170) ब(cid:167)क (आरबीआई) और (cid:255)वत(cid:170)न िनदशे ालय
(डीओई) के अिधका(cid:229)रय(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा शमन िकया जा सकता है। क(cid:164) (cid:254) सरकार ने इन (cid:255)द(cid:176) शि(cid:265)य(cid:332) का (cid:255)योग करते
ह(cid:242)ए, िवदेशी म(cid:254)ु ा (शमन काय(cid:170)वाही) िनयम, 2000 जारी िकए थ,े िज(cid:198)ह(cid:164) िदनांक 12 िसतंबर, 2024 को
अिधसूिचत िवदेशी म(cid:254)ु ा (शमन काय(cid:170)वाही) िनयम, 2024 (cid:300)ारा अिध(cid:248)िमत िकया गया था। इन िनयम(cid:332) म(cid:164) इस
(cid:255)कार क(cid:236) शमन क(cid:236) (cid:255)ि(cid:248)या का (cid:255)ावधान िकया गया है और आरबीआई तथा डीओई म(cid:164) िविभ(cid:198)न (cid:214)तर(cid:332) पर
अिधका(cid:229)रय(cid:332) को उ(cid:265) उ(cid:208)लंघन म(cid:164) शािमल रािश के अनसु ार शमन करने के िलए (cid:255)ािधकृत िकया गया है।
(ग) और (घ) िवदेशी म(cid:254)ु ा (शमन काय(cid:170)वाही) िनयम, 2024 श(cid:322)ु होने क(cid:236) तारीख को शमन (cid:255)ािधकरण के सम(cid:177)
लंिबत कोई भी शमन आवेदन िवदेशी म(cid:254)ु ा (शमन काय(cid:170)वाही) िनयम, 2000 के (cid:255)ावधान(cid:332) (cid:300)ारा शािसत होगा।
OBJECTIVES OF PRASHAD SCHEME
190. SHRI MALAIYARASAN D:
SHRI C N ANNADURAI:
SHRI G SELVAM:
SHRI NAVASKANI K:
Will the Minister of TOURISM be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government has implemented the Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and
Spiritual Heritage Augmentation Drive (PRASHAD) scheme;
(b) the objectives, scope and key targeted areas of the PRASHAD Scheme, including
the criteria for selecting pilgrimage sites;
(c) the steps taken under the PRASHAD Scheme to promote spirituals/sustainable
tourism and improve infrastructure at pilgrimage sites;
03.02.2025 920
(d) the total funds allocated for the scheme particularly for Tamil Nadu and the
progress made so far in its implementation including Kallakurichi Constituency of
Tamil Nadu;
(e) the key achievements of the PRASHAD Scheme since its inception along with
challenges by the Government in implementing PRASHAD projects;
(f) the number of pilgrimage sites identified and developed under the PRASHAD
Scheme in Tamil Nadu;
(g) the list of pilgrimage sites included in the PRASHAD Scheme along with the funds
allocated to each State, State-wise;
(h) whether there are any States where the PRASHAD Scheme is yet to be
implemented and if so, the reasons therefor; and
(i) the manner in which the Government monitor the progress of projects and conduct
regular evaluations under the PRASHAD Scheme?
THE MINISTER OF CULTURE; AND MINISTER OF TOURISM (SHRI GAJENDRA
SINGH SHEKHAWAT):
(a) to (i): Yes Sir, Ministry of Tourism, Government of India is implementing Pilgrimage
Rejuvenation and Spiritual Heritage Augmentation Drive (PRASHAD) scheme.
The ‘National Mission on Pilgrimage Rejuvenation and Spiritual, Heritage Augmentation
Drive’ (PRASHAD) has been launched by the Ministry of Tourism with the objective of
integrated development of identified pilgrimage and heritage destinations. Under the
scheme, Ministry of Tourism provides financial assistance to the State Governments and
Union Territory Administrations for development of tourism infrastructure at important
pilgrimage and heritage destinations. The vision of the scheme is to rejuvenate
03.02.2025 921
pilgrimage and spiritual experience of the tourists through availability of well- planned
tourism infrastructure enabling tourist convenience, accessibility, security, cleanliness,
experience and revitalize/preserve the soul of the pilgrimage/heritage city through
integrated, inclusive, and sustainable developments that would spur employment
opportunities for the local communities.
The selection of pilgrimage sites under PRASHAD Scheme is based on various
parameters, which, inter-alia, include the number of footfalls, cultural, historical and
heritage significance of the locations, equitable representation to ensure nationwide
development, availability of funds and other factors. This process is undertaken in
consultation with State Governments (SGs)/UT Administrations (UTs).
Since the launch of the Scheme, the Ministry has sanctioned 47 projects
in 27 States/UTs with an estimated cost of Rs.1594.40 crore and a total amount of
Rs.1047.92 crore has been released for these projects till date.
Details of the projects sanctioned/sites identified along with the details of funds
released till date under the scheme including the State of Tamil Nadu is given in the
STATEMENT.
Receiving proposals from the State Governments/UT Administrations for financial
assistance for tourism projects is a continuous process. The proposals received are
examined with reference to the prescribed guidelines and financial assistance is
extended for such projects subject to fulfilment of the stipulated conditions and
availability of funds.
The primary issue faced in the projects sanctioned under the scheme is ensuring
their timely completion. Although the projects are implemented by the respective State
03.02.2025 922
Governments/UT Administrations, the Ministry of Tourism regularly monitors their
progress and actively encourages the concerned authorities to complete them within the
stipulated timeframe.
In order to monitor and expedite progress of the sanctioned projects and to guide
the State Governments/UT Administrations, the Ministry of Tourism holds regular review
meetings at different levels. Further, Central Sanctioning and Monitoring Committee and
Mission Directorate meetings are also held in accordance with the scheme guidelines.

TREE PLANTATION
192. SHRI DEEPAK ADHIKARI (DEV):
SHRI JANARDAN SINGH SIGRIWAL
Will the Minister of ENVIRONMENT, FOREST AND CLIMATE CHANGE be pleased
to state:
03.02.2025 934
(a) the details of the trees cut and deforestation caused due to construction of
roads/highways under National Highway Authority of India in the country, State-
wise;
(b) whether the Government provides any financial assistance to the States for
executing tree plantation programmes;
(c) if so, the details thereof, State/UT-wise and if not, the reasons therefor;
(d) the details of the schemes proposed by the Government to carry out plantation
drives in villages across the country; and
(e) whether the Government is contemplating to launch tree plantation programme in
the country on large scale and if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ENVIRONMENT, FOREST
AND CLIMATE CHANGE; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EXTERNAL AFFAIRS (SHRI KIRTI VARDHAN SINGH):
(a) The proposals for diversion of forest land including those for
construction of roads/ highways under National Highways Authority
are considered for approval on case to case basis under the
provisions of Van (Sanrakshan Evam Samvardhan) Adhiniyam,
1980. As per the conditions of approval, the States/UTs concerned
have to ensure that minimum number of trees are felled and
wherever possible translocation of trees is done. The permissions
for felling of trees are given by the concerned States/UTs under
various Acts, Rules, Guidelines and the directions of Hon'ble
03.02.2025 935
Courts. The data of trees felled in such cases is not maintained at
the level of the Ministry.
(b) to (e) National Mission for a Green India is one of the eight
Missions outlined under the National Action Plan on Climate
Change. It aims at protecting, restoring, and enhancing India’s
forest cover and responding to Climate Change by undertaking
plantation activities in the forest and non-forest areas. GIM activities
were started in the FY 2015-16. So far, a sum of ₹962.87 crores has
been released to seventeen States and one union territory for
creation of plantation/eco-restoration. The State/UT-wise funds
released/financial assistance provided under Green India Mission
is enclosed at STATEMENT-I.
Nagar Van Yojana (NVY) is being implemented to create
forest/green spaces in urban areas by developing Nagar
Vans/Vatikas and to protect forest land within cities/towns or its
fringes from degradation and encroachment. The scheme is
designed to actively engage local residents and different agencies
in developing bio-diverse forests for social and environmental
benefits in an urban landscape. The detail of the funds released to
the States/UTs under the Nagar Van Yojana (NVY) scheme during
last five years is attached at STATEMENT-II.
The Ministry is implementing the 'School Nursery Yojana' with an
aim to provide an environment for the students to understand and
03.02.2025 936
appreciate the significance of plants in maintaining and sustaining
the natural eco-system.
The Ministry is also implementing 20 Point Programme (TPP)
scheme wherein, Afforestation targets of Item No 51-Afforestation -
51(a) - Area Covered under plantation on - Public and Forest Lands
(Hectare) and 51(b)- Number of Seedlings to be planted on - Public
and Forest Lands (Number) are fixed annually by MoEFandCC to
all States/UTs. These targets are achieved collectively by the
States/UTs through various programmes of the Central Govt.
Schemes, State Govt. plan and non-plan schemes and plantation
efforts by NGOs, Private organizations, Civil Societies etc.

Further, on the occasion of World Environment Day 5th June, 2024
the Hon'ble Prime Minister has launched 'Ek Ped Maa Ke Naam'
(एक पेड़ मा ँ के नाम) campaign and urged all citizens to contribute to
make our planet better and called upon everyone, in India and
around the world, to plant a tree as a tribute to our mothers. The
initiative encouraged people to plant a sapling as a tribute to their
mothers, intertwining the themes of environmental care with the
universal bond of maternal love.

STRATEGIC PLANS FOR UPCOMING OLYMPICS AND PARALYMPICS EVENTS
194. SHRI KODIKUNNIL SURESH:
Will the Minister of YOUTH AFFAIRS AND SPORTS be pleased to state:
(a) the details of funding allocated and utilized for India's participation in the recent
Olympics and Paralympics, including expenditures on training, coaching, infrastructure
and international exposure;
(b) the criteria and process followed to distribute funds among various sports federations
and athletes in preparation for the Olympics and Paralympics;
(c) the specific challenges faced in the funding and preparation process for these events
and whether any corrective measures are being implemented to improve efficiency and
support and if so, the details thereof;
03.02.2025 945
(d) whether the Government has any strategic plan for the upcoming Olympics and
Paralympics including projected funding requirements to enhance the training facilities
and measures to support emerging athletes; and
(e) the details of the targets set by the Government for medal outcomes in the next
Olympics and Paralympics and the initiatives in place to achieve these goals?
THE MINISTER OF LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT; AND MINISTER OF YOUTH
AFFAIRS AND SPORTS (DR. MANSUKH MANDAVIYA):
(a) to (e) Sir, preparation for International events including Olympics and
Paralympics is an ongoing and continuous process. The Ministry of Youth Affairs and
Sports supports the Indian sportspersons/teams through various schemes, such as
Scheme of Assistance to National Sports Federations and Target Olympic Podium
Scheme (TOPS).
The plans / proposals of the National Sports Federations (NSFs) relating to training,
competitive exposure, coaches and support personnel including foreign coaches, for
preparing sportspersons/teams for the international events including mega-sports
events such as Olympics, Paralympics etc. are deliberated and finalized in the Annual
Calendar for Training and Competition (ACTC) meetings. Further, the proposals
received, under TOPS, are evaluated and approved on a case-by-case basis by the
Mission Olympic Cell (MOC), and on approval, the funds are directly disbursed to the
athletes to facilitate their training and participation in competitions.
For the Olympics 2024, athletes were provided facilities of coaching camps, exposure
to international competitions, etc, which was funded to the tune of approximately Rs.
03.02.2025 946
470 crore, through various Government Schemes and CSR funding from the Public and
the corporate sector.
For the Paralympics 2024, overall funding to the tune of approximately Rs.74 crore was
utilized for the preparation and participation of athletes, covering key areas such as
training and coaching, International exposure and competitions, Sports infrastructure
development.
The Sports Authority of India (SAI) creates / upgrades infrastructure in its SAI Centres.
Over the last five years, Rs. 533.55 crore have been spent towards improving the
infrastructure and facilities of SAI Centres, where Olympic bound athletes of different
sports get trained.
After conclusion of the Paris Olympics 2024, the following learnings have emerged:
• Need for a clear selection policy and training plan of athletes by NSFs.
• Talent identification through strong domestic competition structure.
• Integration of sports science to enhance sporting performances.
• Development of coaches and Technical Officials and improving their
competencies.
The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports, the NSFs, including the Indian Olympic
Association, and the SAI are working in close coordination for providing best facilities,
training, equipment support as also a wholesome nutritious diet required for preparation
of the teams and sportspersons for the forthcoming international sports events, including
Olympics and Paralympics. Furthermore, Government has also established the National
03.02.2025 947
Centre of Sports Science and Research (NCSSR) at New Delhi to better integrate sports
science into the overall sports ecosystem.
FAKE UNIVERSITIES
195. SHRI Y S AVINASH REDDY:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) whether the University Grants Commission (UGC) has declared 21 universities as
“fake” and not empowered to confer any degree, majority of which are in Delhi followed
by Uttar Pradesh;
(b) if so, the details thereof and the action taken thereon, State-wise;
(c) whether the UGC has warned students not to take admission in these universities
and among them, at least 21 self-styled, unrecognised institutions which are functioning
in contravention of the UGC Act and are violating UGC guidelines, have been declared
as fake universities;
(d) if so, the details thereof including the action taken by the Government thereon; and
(e) the steps taken/being taken by the Government to punish the fake universities in
order to curb them along with action taken/penalty imposed since 2014, State-wise?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF EDUCATION; AND MINISTER OF
STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF DEVELOPMENT OF NORTH EASTERN REGION (DR.
SUKANTA MAJUMDAR):
(a) to (e) The list of fake universities is available on the website of the University Grants
Commission (UGC) at https://www.ugc.gov.in/universitydetails/Fakeuniversity.
03.02.2025 948
Currently, 21 Institutions figure in the list. Maintenance of Law and order is a subject
matter of State Governments. Accordingly, the Central Government has requested to
Chief Secretaries of all the States and U.T. Administration to take legal action for closure
of these institutions and take appropriate action against those involved in defrauding and
cheating students by misrepresenting themselves as "Universities", awarding degrees
and using the word "University" with their name. It was also requested to intimate the
Central Government /UGC if there are other fake universities functioning in their
States/UTs which are not included in the UGC list of Fake Universities.
Apart from issuing public Notices for general awareness through social media and
UGC website for cautioning the general public, students, parents and other stakeholder,
the following steps have also been taken by the UGC/ Government against such fake
Universities:
i. The Government as well as the UGC have requested the Chief Secretaries of all
State/UT Governments to take appropriate action against those involved in
defrauding and cheating students by misrepresenting themselves as
“Universities”, awarding degrees and using the word “University” with their name.
ii. FIRs have been lodged against several self-styled institutions/universities.
iii. Show Causes Notices/Warning Notices have also been issued to the
unauthorized institutes awarding invalid degrees.
As a result, 12 fake universities were closed since 2014.
03.02.2025 949
IMPROVEMENT OF SPORTS INFRASTRUCTURE IN RURAL AREAS OF KERALA
196. SHRI RAJMOHAN UNNITHAN:
Will the Minister of YOUTH AFFAIRS AND SPORTS be pleased to state:
(a) the details of the key initiatives the Government is currently implementing to empower
youth across the country, especially in Kerala;
(b) the steps taken/being taken by the Government to improve the infrastructure for
sports in rural areas in Kerala; and
(c) whether the Government plans to implement new scheme for the promotion of sports
development programmes for differently-abled youth, if so, the details thereof?
THE MINISTER OF LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT; AND MINISTER OF YOUTH
AFFAIRS AND SPORTS (DR. MANSUKH MANDAVIYA):
(a) to (c) ‘Sports’ being a State subject, the responsibility of development of sports,
including development of sports infrastructure and youth empowerment, implementation
of new schemes for the promotion of sports development programmes for differently-
abled youth in the country, rests primarily with the State/Union Territory Governments.
The Union Government supplements their efforts by bridging critical gaps. The Ministry
of Youth Affairs and Sports implements the following schemes to promote sports in the
country, including in the State of Kerala:
(i) Khelo India- National Programme for Development of Sports; (ii) Assistance to
National Sports Federations; (iii) Special Awards to Winners in International Sports
Events and their Coaches; (iv) National Sports Awards; (v) Pension to Meritorious
Sportspersons; (vi) Pandit Deendayal Upadhyay National Welfare Program for
03.02.2025 950
Sportspersons; (vii) National Sports Development Fund; and (viii) Running Sports
Training Centres through Sports Authority of India.
Details of the above schemes are available in the public domain on the websites of this
Ministry and of Sports Authority of India (SAI).
Under the “Creation and upgradation of sports infrastructure” component of the Khelo
India Scheme, this Ministry renders financial support for creation of basic sports
infrastructure such as sports complex, synthetic athletic tracks, synthetic hockey fields,
synthetic turf football grounds, multipurpose halls, swimming pools, etc. along with
sports equipment. Further, under National Sports Development Funds (NSDF),
Government supports various institutions and individuals for creation and upgradation
of sports facilities across the country, including in the State of Kerala. The details of the
sports infrastructure sanctioned under Khelo India Scheme and NSDF, across the
country, including in the State of Kerala, are available in public domain on the dashboard
of the Ministry at https://mdsd.kheloindia.gov.in and http://www.nsdf.yas.gov.in/nsdf-
glance.html.
The Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports implements various schemes to promote youth
empowerment in the country, including in the State of Kerala viz (i) Mera Yuva Bharat
(MY Bharat); (ii) Nehru Yuva Kendra Sangathan (NYKS); (iii) National Service Scheme
(NSS). Further details may be seen on https://yas.gov.in/youth-affairs.

INTERNSHIP TO YOUTHS UNDER PMIS
198. SHRI EATALA RAJENDER:
SHRI CHAMALA KIRAN KUMAR REDDY:
SHRIMATI D K ARUNA:
Will the Minister of CORPORATE AFFAIRS be pleased to state:
(a) whether the pilot scheme of the Prime Minister Internship Scheme received less
applications against opportunities while it aims to provide internship to one crore
youths in 500 companies;
(b) the details of youths who have gained exposure to real life business environment
across varied professions and employment opportunities;
(c) the details of the interns who are provided with financial assistance of Rs. 5,000 per
month and the details of the Departments/Ministries which have provided a one-time
grant of Rs. 6,000 for incidentals to each intern; and
(d) if so, the details thereof, State-wise particularly in Telangana and Andhra Pradesh
along with the funds sanctioned/spent so far?
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF CORPORATE AFFAIRS; AND
MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF ROAD TRANSPORT AND HIGHWAYS
(SHRI HARSH MALHOTRA):
(a) to (d) No. The number of applications received were higher than the opportunities
provided by the Companies in the first round of the Pilot Project of the Prime Minister
Internship Scheme.
03.02.2025 954
The Prime Minister’s Internship Scheme (PMIS) announced in the Budget 2024-25,
aims to provide internship opportunities to one crore youth in top 500 companies in
five years. As an initiation to this Scheme, Ministry of Corporate Affairs has launched
a Pilot Project of the Scheme on 3rd October, 2024, which is targeted to provide 1.25
lakh internship opportunities in the Financial Year 2024-25.
In the first round of the Pilot Project, which commenced from 3rd October, 2024, more
than 1.27 lakh internship opportunities were provided by the partner companies
across the country. Against these, approximately 6.21 lakh applications were
received. In the first round of the Pilot Project, partner companies have made 82,077
internship offers to 60,866 candidates, out of which 28,141 candidates have accepted
offers to join the internship. State wise details of internship opportunities provided,
and internship offers received by the candidates is provided at STATEMENT- I.
Under the Scheme, a one-time grant for incidentals of ₹6,000 is to be disbursed to
each intern by the government upon the intern's joining at the internship location,
subject to verification by the company. In addition, monthly assistance of Rs. 5,000 is
to be paid to the interns for the entire duration of the internship. Every month, the
company, based upon the intern’s attendance and related company policies regarding
good conduct, releases Rs.500/- to each intern from its CSR funds. Once the
company makes the payment, the government would release Rs.4,500 per month to
intern through Direct Benefit Transfer. No other Ministry other than Ministry of
Corporate Affairs is releasing any funds under this Scheme.
03.02.2025 955
Against the accepted offers of 28,141 candidates in first round, total one-time grant
for incidentals amounting to Rs.4.38 crore has been sanctioned and paid directly into
the Aadhaar Seeded Bank Accounts of the Interns who have joined so far. State-wise
details are at STATEMENT-II. Monthly assistance of approximately Rs 1.3 crore has
been sanctioned so far to the Interns who have completed one month.
The second round of the PM Internship Scheme Pilot Project has started from 9th
January, 2025, and the companies are in the process of posting new as well as editing
unfilled internship opportunities.

INVESTMENT IN THE CARE ECONOMY
199. SHRI GAURAV GOGOI:
Will the Minister of LABOUR AND EMPLOYMENT be pleased to state:
(a) whether the Government is aware of the International Labour Organization's (ILO's)
report highlighting that 53% of women in India are outside the labour force due to care
responsibilities, if so, the details thereof;
(b) the specific steps taken by the Government during the last ten years to promote
investment in the care economy and the progress made in this regard;
(c) whether the Government has any plans to develop a robust care workforce including
training and skill development programmes for workers in the care sector, if so, the
details thereof;
(d) the measures are in place to improve the working conditions, wages, and social
security benefits for care workers, particularly for women; and
(e) the steps taken by the Government to promote the use of technology in enhancing
the delivery of care services and improving the efficiency of the care sector?
03.02.2025 962
THE MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF MICRO, SMALL AND MEDIUM
ENTERPRISES; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF LABOUR AND
EMPLOYMENT (KUMARI SHOBHA KARANDLAJE):
(a) to (e) ILO’s flagship report, World Employment and Social Outlook: Trends
2025, highlights a significant increase in the labour force participation rate in Southern
Asia, driven by increase in female participation, particularly in India. According to
Periodic Labour Force Survey (PLFS), India's female Labour Force Participation Rate
(LFPR) for ages 15+ has increased from 23.3% in 2017-18 to 41.7% in 2023-24.
The Government of India has undertaken various steps to promote care
economy and increase women participation in the workforce.
Paid maternity leave has been increased from 12 to 26 weeks under the
Maternity Benefit (Amendment) Act, 2017. The Act also requires the workplaces with 50
or more employees to provide day-care/creche facility.
Further, the Palna sub-scheme under Mission Shakti provides for
Standalone crèches and Anganwadi cum crèches for all States/UTs w.e.f. 01 April 2022.
In order to promote use of technology in this area, the Mission Shakti Dashboard was
launched on 22.01.2025 for monitoring and implementation of the scheme.
In addition, the Pradhan Mantri Matru Vandana Yojana (PMMVY) is being
implemented with the objective to provide cash incentive for partial compensation of the
wage loss so that the woman can take adequate rest before and after delivery of the first
child.
03.02.2025 963
In order to promote skill development in this sector, the Home Management
And Care Givers Sector Skill Council -HMCGSSC, is an apex body, which is working
under the aegis of Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship (MSDE) and
National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC).
INITIATIVES TO INCREASE GIRLS STUDENTS IN SCHOOLS
200. SHRI C N ANNADURAI:
SHRI G SELVAM:
SHRI NAVASKANI K:
Will the Minister of EDUCATION be pleased to state:
(a) the current enrollment and retention rates of girls in schools under the Samagra
Shiksha Abhiyan, State/UT-wise;
(b) the specific initiatives taken by the Government under Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan to
increase the enrollment of girls, particularly at secondary and senior secondary levels;
(c) whether there are any scholarships, incentives or financial assistance available for
girls under the Scheme, if so, the number of girls benefited during the last five years,
year-wise;
(d) whether awareness campaigns by engaging communities are conducted under the
scheme to promote girls' education; and
(e) the steps taken by the Government to ensure gender-sensitive infrastructure in
schools, such as separate toilets, hygiene facilities and safe spaces to encourage girls’
attendance in schools?
03.02.2025 964
THE MINISTER OF STATE OF THE MINISTRY OF SKILL DEVELOPMENT AND
ENTREPRENEURSHIP; AND MINISTER OF STATE IN THE MINISTRY OF
EDUCATION (SHRI JAYANT CHAUDHARY):
(a) State/UT-wise details of the current enrollment and retention rates of girls in schools
under the Samagra Shiksha Abhiyan, are at STATEMENT.
(b) and (c) Under Samagra Shiksha, various interventions have been targeted for girls,
which include opening of schools in the neighbourhood to make access easier for girls,
free uniform and text-books to girls up to Class VIII, Self-Defence Training to Girls of
class VI to XII of Govt. Schools, additional teachers and residential quarters for teachers
in remote/hilly areas, appointment of additional teachers including women teachers,
stipend @Rs. 200 per month for 10 months in a year to CWSN girls from class I to class
XII, separate toilets for girls, teachers’ sensitization programs to promote girls
participation, gender-sensitive teaching-learning materials including text books etc.
Further, special state specific projects for varied interventions under equity are
also emphasized for enhancing access, retention and quality for girls by promoting
enrolment drives, retention and motivation camps, gender sensitization modules etc.
Such projects include life skills, awareness programmes, incinerators, sanitary pad
vending machines etc.
To reduce gender gaps at all levels of school education, there is a provision of
Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalayas (KGBVs) under Samagra Shiksha. KGBVs are
residential schools from class VI to XII for girls belonging to disadvantaged groups such
as SC, ST, OBC, Minority and Below Poverty Line (BPL).
03.02.2025 965
Year-wise details of CwSN girls who received stipend during last five years are as
under:
(in lakh)
2020-21 2021-22 2022-23 2023-24 2024-25
4.65 6.12 5.8 5.57 5.50
(Source: Prabandh)
(d) National Education Policy (NEP), 2020 emphasizes the participation of community
and parents in helping the child to learn appropriately and in achievement of learning
outcomes. Under Samagra Shiksha, there is a provision of Community Mobilization
under Quality and Innovation interventions. With the help of Community, every year
enrollment drives are conducted at the beginning of the academic session for identifying
and bringing out-of-school or dropout girls back into the formal educational system.
Parents are informed about various provisions of Samagra Shiksha which helps to
reduce financial barriers in education through awareness programs.
(e) Under Samagra Shiksha, there is a provision of Separate toilets for girls in
schools. As per UDISE+ 2023-24, 97.2% Govt. Schools have Separate toilets for girls
in schools.
03.02.2025 966
