SHRI E. T. MOHAMMED BASHEER (MALAPPURAM): Thank you very much,
Madam. On behalf of my party, Indian Union Muslim League, and on my own
behalf, I express my shradhanjali for the sadful demise of a large number of
brothers, sisters and kids.
Coming to the Motion of Thanks, there is nothing substantial in this. It is
superficial and far away from the Indian reality. Most of the points are reproduced
from the websites of the various Departments. There is nothing important in it. As
far as development is concerned, there is a definition in the speech which I would
like to reproduce, and I quote:
“Development is truly meaningful when its benefits reach the person standing
at the last rung of society.”
It is a correct definition. But I would like to ask whether there is any kind of
honesty in your saying. There is nothing like that. I understand that even though
we have said so many things, we have forgotten the ill fate of the downtrodden
section and the marginalised section of the society. When we are measuring the
wealth, the progress, and the prosperity, we are aiming at the upper class, the
multinationals and things like that. Poor people, who are living below the poverty
line, have not been considered properly in that. Regarding Emergency also, you
have given an emphasis. You are saying about 1975 in your speech. You have
said about Emergency. But you have conveniently forgotten that undeclared
emergency is continuing in this country during your period. That is happening. I
do not want to go into the details of it.
03.02.2025 1379
Similarly, what is happening in the country now? The entire democracy is at
the doldrums. Take the example of the Parliament. This House is having a glorious
tradition. Unfortunately, what is happening? Law-making in this House has
become a mockery. That is for victimisation. That kind of thing is going on.
Similarly, the Executive is in a deplorable condition. Judiciary also is moving like
that. There is a lot of criticism about the credibility of the Judiciary also. Similar is
the case with the freedom of expression, freedom of the media. All these things
are condemnable; that is what is happening in our country now.
My complaint is small one. You have not even mentioned a single word, a
single sentence about the minorities in this country. Why have you forgotten them?
What is their ill fate? You have to realise what is happening in this country.
Legislations are made against them. For them only, legislations are coming. There
are many examples. I do not go into it. What is happening? The topmost leaders
in this country have been put in jails just before the election by fabricating stories
against them. That is happening. Similarly, activists, environmental activists,
student leaders, media persons and cultural leaders were put behind the bar
without any reason, for political reason only. Is it not a shame for this country?
There are these kinds of things which should be curtailed.
There are atrocities against the minorities. What is happening in this
country? They are demolishing the houses. People are thrown to streets. In North
India, that is happening. In UP, that is happening. Nobody is there to question this.
Similarly, in the name of cow protection, how many people are killed in this country?
There is nobody to question it. You are saying wonderful things. But this country
03.02.2025 1380
is getting a bad shape. It is going from bad to worse. Those kinds of things are
going on. In seven States in the country, they have made their own legislation.
What is anti-conversion? There is a provision in the Indian Constitution, Article 25.
When that is there, what is the locus standi of the States, except with the BJP’s
blessing, to have this kind of a legislation. Those kinds of things are going on.
Actually, the Government is watching as a silent spectator. All these kinds of things
are going on. They are not reacting.
Why is it so? That is with the blessing of the Ruling Party.
Madam, I do not want to go into the details. Now, I come to Waqf Act. What
is this for? The Waqf Act is only to victimise the minorities. There is nothing more
than that. The Home Minister has already declared that the Government will go for
Uniform Civil Code and that kind of things. You are talking about unity. It has been
mentioned in the speech. But instead of unity, you are developing enmity in this
country. You have to realise one thing that in this country, you are taking a partisan
attitude. It is a very sad thing.
I am quoting from the President’s Address. It says, “A significant measure of
‘Viksit Bharat’ is balanced development of the country. No region should feel left
behind in the journey of progress”. It is a beautiful sentence. But is there any iota
of truth in dealing with all these things? You have no moral right to talk about the
unity.
HON. CHAIRPERSON : Please conclude.
SHRI E. T. MOHAMMED BASHEER : Madam, I am concluding.
When you are talking about balanced development and when you are
03.02.2025 1381
distributing the wealth, programmes, projects, etc., are you giving justice? There
also there is a partisan attitude. I would like to say one thing. In the southern end
of India, there is a beautiful place; a tiny place; a green State, which is Kerala. That
is my own home State. Are you aware of it? Your plans; the speech of the hon.
President; and your Budget, are totally ignoring the State. I express my vehement
objection to this negative stand taken against Kerala.
Madam, now, I am going to conclude. We have to understand one thing.
Towards the end, I would like to say one more thing which is about Haj. I met the
hon. Minister, who is the incharge of Haj. Calicut is the biggest embarkation point
for Haj. After U.P., Calicut is the biggest embarkation point. Cochin, Kannur and
Kozhikode are embarkation points. The flight charge at Kozhikode is above Rs.
40,000. What is the justification? They are going for a good thing. They are
victimised. You have to find out a solution for that. I met the hon. Minister. I am
really thankful to him. He had given me a sympathetic response.
What I am saying is that there may be politics; there may be ideas; there may
be ideologies; and there may be a fight over an ideology, but justice should be
ensured. That is what is lagging behind in this country now.
Through you, Madam, I once again request that justice should be given. The
minorities are denied justice. There must be justification for these kinds of activities
also. There should not be a denial of justice.
With these few words, I conclude.
Thank you very much.
03.02.2025 1382
DR. ANGOMCHA BIMOL AKOIJAM (INNER MANIPUR): Thank you, Madam
Chairperson for the time and for allowing me to take part in this discussion on the
hon. President’s Address in this House.
Very honestly speaking, I was expecting the President’s speech to mention
something on Manipur because I thought that the Government will outline through
her speech the steps being taken by the Government of India to deal with the issues
and this tragedy that we have been witnessing in my home State. But call it
arrogance or a reflection of a peculiar form of ‘Rastriya Chetna’ followed by the
Ruling Party, they have chosen to remain silent in an effort to make the crisis
invisible to the rest of the country as well as to the globe. So, I expect, Madam
Chairperson, to give me a little more time than the usual so that I can register the
pain and anguish of the people of my State, in particular, and those fellow
countrymen who are worried about the happenings in my home State. It is my
sincere appeal to you. Please do give me some extra time.
The President’s speech says that the Government is trying to take along all
these people; an inclusive and an all-encompassing approach based on the
principle enshrined in the Constitution.
But the fact of the matter is this Government and their ideological moorings
clearly do not believe in inclusive politics. I say this for one reason not only for what
has happened vis-à-vis with the Manipur crisis and the manner in which the
Government of India have responded but also generally the manner in which they
have tinkered the Constitution principles and the manner in which they have
imagined this nation to be. Their attitude and response to Manipur is nothing but a
03.02.2025 1383
specific form of reflection of their general conception of what this nation is all about.
Then, we start with a particular tinkering that this Government has done
which we do not see. Babasaheb Ambedkar said we have this document now but
how we inject flesh and blood to the soul, to this document, is what matters through
our practice, what has been happening since the last ten years. Like all liberal
Constitutions, we have this beautiful phrase in our Preamble called ‘We the people’
and this ‘We the people’ is like old liberal Constitution that conceptualized the
population. The nation has a stratified population. But this Government is pushing
for a kind of imagination of a nation which is cultural and organic in nature. When
the Prime Minister took over in 2014, he has said that on many occasions across
many platforms in the West, in UK, UAE, Netherlands or in various meetings in this
country. He invoked a very familiar phrase that we have seen in the Nazi Germany
in the 1920s and 1930s, that is, Blut-und-Boden ‘Blood and the soil’. He said in his
speech in Wimble as well as in Netherland or UAE or in my part of the world in
Silchar. He asks whether one should be given emphasis on the colour of the
passport or the colour of the blood or in our relation to the soil. This invocation of
the blood and the soil is what the Nazis have shouted in the 1920s and 1930s. It
is these conceptions of the people of this country that lead to various exclusions.
So, there is no surprise that today we have a party which swears by nationalist
ethos and you have none of the Members from the Muslim communities which
constitute 15 per cent of the population. That is the reflection of that imagination of
the nation. It is a similar kind of an exclusion that you have continued through your
response through the crisis in Manipur.
03.02.2025 1384
Allow me to cite four specific issues that tell you that the crisis in Manipur is
not an isolated issue. Its gravity is such that it is a national crisis. Let me explain
the four specific issues. Under the Indian Constitution, we have the State
Government and the Assembly which has been reduced to a farce. We had the
shortest Assembly session in August 2023 which barely lasted less than half an
hour. Instead of discussing the crisis, the Assembly was cut short. The Government
of Manipur openly claims that its power has been shrivelled by the Union
Government. We do not know who is in charge of this State of Manipur. The leaders
of the Government in the State say that the police did not report to the hon. CM and
the Home Minister. To whom are they reporting then? And the appointment of big
officials to deal with law and order, such as the Chairman of the Unified Command
which takes care of the security and law and order situation and coordinates with
the various security agencies, has been done away and they appointed some ex-
bureaucrat from the Centre.
For instance, the CM says that some part of Manipur is under my command
and some part of the State is under the command of the Union Home Minister. Can
you tell the Chief Minister of Maharashtra that Mumbai is under my control and the
rest of Maharashtra is under the control of the Union Government? That is an illegal
subversion of the Constitutional mechanism and democracy in this country in the
75 years of our experience. That is the subversion that we have experienced. We
do not realize this. The Chief Minister orders something. The Central Security
Forces refuses to obey the order.
There are images where the Central Armed Police Forces stopped the
03.02.2025 1385
Manipur Police from responding to armed attacks on innocent villagers. We
witnessed this complete chaos in the 75th year of our Constitution on which we have
been given lectures in this House as well. It is a reality which is happening in
Manipur. But who cares?
You have sent 60,000 plus troops there. Under whose command those troops are
working? If Article 355 or Article 365 has been invoked, where is the written
document? There is nothing of that kind. So, it is an extra constitutional subversion
of the Constitution of our country in a State called Manipur. The Indian state has

become nothing but a ... in the State of Manipur.
Madam Chairperson, have you ever seen elected Members being beaten up
publicly in this country and the country thinks that constitutional order is in order in
Manipur? The dignity of the elected Members, the authority of the Assembly –
everything has been subverted by this Union Government in collusion with their
satraps who are running the show on their behalf in Imphal. Please keep in mind
that this is a complete subversion of a constitutional order on the 75th year of our
Constitution.
Incidentally, this has happened not for the first time in Manipur. When the
Constitution was adopted 75 years ago, we had an Assembly of our own and a
constitutional monarchy which was bulldozed on 15th October, 1949. That insult
should be bygone and we should look forward. But the manner in which the crisis
has been handled and the contemptuous manner in which this has not been talked
 Expunged as ordered by the Chair.
03.02.2025 1386
about even in our hon. President’s Address is making us to remember that

and unfortunate part as we are celebrating the 75th year of our Constitution.
When Babasaheb Ambedkar was asked, which Article in the Constitution do
you think is the most important one? He pointed out Article 32 and said that this
Article is the soul and the heart of the Constitution … (Interruptions) Madam, I have
seen people getting more time. Please give me two or three minutes more.
Habeas Corpus under Article 32 is one of the legal rights which the citizens
have. But in the most secured place in Manipur, the Red Shield Division, 57
Mountain Division, an army garrison, a civilian, called Laishram Kamal Babu,
disappeared without a trace since last November. The Defence Minister kept quiet;
the Army Chief kept quiet. How secured are the people of Manipur even when you
are not secured in the military garrison, the biggest army headquarters, 57
Mountain Division in the State of Manipur? Who will answer this?
I am elected by my people and I am supposed to voice in this House as a
part of a democratic culture, as a part of a constitutional scheme of things. Now,
how do I speak? People have been killed. Children and women have been
assaulted. We have seen the situation of hostage. Women and children were not
only taken hostage but they were killed in cold blooded manner and in a most
gruesome manner in Jiribam including an 8-month-old infant. The post-mortem
report is a chilling reminder. People have been killed in gruesome manner
throughout this 21-month-old crisis. This kind of hostage and killing has never
happened.
03.02.2025 1387
I, as an MP, cannot go to some parts of Manipur. Where is my right to free
movement which comes under Article 19? People are not able to go everywhere.
The Chief Minister himself cannot go to certain places. Some MLAs belonging to
tribal groups cannot come and attend the Assembly and we think that Article 19 is
working in the State of Manipur and we are celebrating 75th year of the Constitution!
Where is Article 19?
Madam Chairperson, there are still thousands and thousands of people in
relief camps. Partition is a living reality in the State of Manipur and we think the
constitutional order is intact in the State of Manipur!
I have been hearing our ministers and leaders saying that Kukis and Meiteis
should talk to each other. Have you ever heard, when the communal violence
happens, Hindus and Muslims should talk to each other? It is the responsibility of
the State. First, you should rein in the violence. The authority of the Indian State
has been challenged by armed groups. Many of them have been propped up by
the Indian security agencies, as many observers have pointed out. Many of these
Kuki armed groups are under Suspension of Operations (SoO). They have been
propped up by Indian security forces, and, unfortunately, on media you see BJP
entering into an understanding with them to win elections. Now, they are playing
havoc. They are running wild.
Similarly, in the valley areas, there are armed groups which are reportedly
nurtured by political leadership from the Ruling Party. This is where the
Government wanted to keep silent. The Government does not want the rest of the
country to know about this. Hindus and Muslim conflict in India was a product of a
03.02.2025 1388
colonial process during the Britishers time. It is not inherent in our country. Many
scholars have written this. The Government should not essentialize this conflict. It
is a part of a colonial State process. Unfortunately, while we are talking about
decolonization, and trying to change the law created by the British, we are talking
like the Britishers saying that this is an essential conflict between the Hindu just like
the Britishers have done as if the Kukis and the Meiteis have an essentialized
conflict. It is a product of a State process. We need to decolonize our mind.
Last but not least, we talk about progress and development in Viksit Bharat.
I must inform the House that the highest inflation rate in the country today is in the
State of Manipur. All the small-scale, medium, micro-entrepreneurs have shut
down. We are losing investment. People are losing jobs. In unemployment rate,
Manipur is among the top five States in this country. We talk about the Eastern
States. Many of the Eastern States including Andaman are in the list of the top ten
States where most of the people are unemployed in this country. You are not talking
about this economic crisis. There is no relief, tax or otherwise. You are trying to
push it under the carpet as if nothing has happened.
Despite all this, you know what happened. People are not able to travel
because of this communal violence. The flight fare is soaring to the sky. They are
looting the tragedy-stricken people. A flight fare from Delhi to Kolkata is around Rs.
6,000, which is a two-and-a-half hours journey, but a flight from Imphal to Kolkata
is around Rs. 13,000 or Rs.14,000. It has been going on for the last 20 months.
The Government of India is not interested in that.
The Government thinks that we are talking about Manipur as if it is an
03.02.2025 1389
exception. I want to end with this remark. The Danish philosophers have reminded
us. Søren Kierkegaard said: “If you want to understand the general, pick up an
exceptional, true exception. Because the exception will explain the general as well
as the exception itself.” If you want to understand what this country is and how it is
imagined, how this constitution is working, and how it is being lied to the rest of the
country, take Manipur as an exceptional case. The moment we are honest about it;
the Government will be able to answer. We think that alienation of the North-East
people will be okay, but the Government should check how the youth are asking
about the relationship between India and Manipur because of this crisis. It is a
danger written on the wall, and I draw the attention of all our Members cutting
across party lines to please respond. We need a JPC to inquire into this crisis and
do justice to the people of the State of Manipur.
Thank you, Madam Chairperson.
SHRI BALASHOWRY VALLABHANENI (MACHILIPATNAM): Madam, thank you
for giving me an opportunity to extend my thanks to the President of India for her
Address.
It was indeed a pleasure to listen to her speech. The hon. President spoke
at length about the collective achievements of the Government during the last one
decade, and has given a roadmap of what is going to happen in the coming years.
I, on behalf of my party under the leadership of Shri Pawan Kalyan garu, strongly
believe that hon. President’s speech is a visionary blueprint of the country’s path
towards building a Viksit Bharat.
The hon. President has highlighted various initiatives, programmes and
03.02.2025 1390
schemes of the Government. This Government is committed to give ‘Housing for
All’, particularly poor and economically weaker sections of this society with Rs. 5
lakh crore plus budget for the scheme.
Madam, it is one of the great initiatives taken by the NDA Government
through which many families have been benefited till date. The NDA Government
will continue to run this programme.
Madam, the financial assistance to our entrepreneurs through MUDRA
scheme have been increased to Rs. 20 lakh from Rs. 10 lakh. As we all know with
rising population, unemployment is becoming one big concern in the country. With
such schemes, more and more youth are attracted to become self-sufficient.
Madam, I only wish to focus on two issues. The first is, interlinking of rivers.
Firstly, I would like to speak regarding the Godavari-Banakacherla Interlinking of
River Project. In Para 19 of her Address, the hon. President has mentioned about
the importance of interlinking of rivers. As we all know, water is a big concern for
every State, particularly drinking water and irrigation water. There is a drought like
situation in many States during summers. This has to be taken up very seriously
and immediately. The hon. President has rightly mentioned about it in her speech.
Madam, I come from Andhra Pradesh, and we have understood its
importance. One such interlinking project in Andhra Pradesh also needs to be taken
up. This would provide irrigation to new ayacut of three lakh hectares, stabilizing
9.1 lakh hectares of area. Importantly, it will provide drinking water to 80 lakh people
of Uttarandhra and Rayalaseema districts, and 20 TMC water for industrial use.
This project links Godavari and Krishna rivers and gives immense benefits.
03.02.2025 1391
Madam, this interlinking is needed, because Krishna River does not have
reliable inflows due to excessive use by upstream States and full utilization by the
Krishna Basin States. But Godavari has reliable inflow, and it is very unfortunate
that nearly 3000 TMC of average water flows into the sea every year. It means,
3000 TMC of water is going waste, which is unacceptable. So, to address the
irrigation and drinking water needs of the whole State of Andhra Pradesh, Krishna
Delta, Uttarandhra and Rayalaseema, the Government of Andhra Pradesh has
proposed to divert Godavari flood waters of 200 TMC at 2 TMC per day from
Polavaram to Banakacherla regulator to overcome drought in the State. The project
is proposed in three Phases. One is, Godavari River to Krishna River; second is
from Krishna River to Bollapalle Reservoir; and the third is from Bollapalle Reservoir
to Banakacherla Regulator. The cost of the project is estimated to be Rs. 80,000
crore. Andhra Pradesh has successfully linked Vamsadhara and Nagavali Rivers,
and now there is a need to take up the Godavari-Banakacherla Link Project.
The hon. Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh, Shri Chandrababu Naidu, and
the hon. Deputy Chief Minister, Shri Pawan Kalyan Garu discussed about this
project with the Minister concerned in the Government of Andhra Pradesh. So, I
appeal to the hon. Prime Minister and the hon. Finance Minister to approve this
Project and provide required funds in the form of grant to complete this project
which would make Andhra Pradesh drought-proof State in the country.
Madam, secondly, I would like to talk about cyclones which occur more often
in Andhra Pradesh. My entire Lok Sabha Constituency of Machilipatnam in Krishna
district is prone to floods, and cyclones have become the order of the day.
03.02.2025 1392
I still remember the 1977 Super Cyclone, which hit Diviseema and
devastated the entire Krishna delta region. Thousands of people lost their lives and
properties. Whatever cyclone shelters are there, they are old and have got
damaged. Therefore, I appeal to the hon. Prime Minister, who is also the Chairman
of the NDMA, to get the new cyclone shelters constructed in Machilipatnam and
Diviseema regions. It will help the people of those areas.
Finally, before I conclude, I would extend my thanks to our leader Shri Pawan
Kalyan Garu and every karyakarta of my Jansena Party to walk over the NDA
mantra – Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas, Sabka Prayas. I believe that we will definitely
achieve our dream of Viksit Bharat.
Thank you, Madam. Namaskar.

Bhartiyas. We are Bharatvasis.

We heard the best speech from our President, and a Vote of Thanks to her
for her speech -- thank you, Madam!
DR. KALANIDHI VEERASWAMY (CHENNAI NORTH): Madam, thank you very
much for giving me an opportunity to speak on the Motion of Thanks on the
President's Address.
Madam, we all know it is customary. Though it is her Address, we know it is
03.02.2025 1397
not her views and it is the views of the Government which has prepared the speech
for her. So, whatever comments or criticisms are made, it can be understood that
it is not made against the hon. President and it is against the Government which
has prepared this draft for her. … (Interruptions)
I cannot hear you. Anyway, let me continue with this. Actually, I am surprised
that not many senior Ministers are here to listen to what the Opposition has to
speak. This has been the problem where anytime when the Opposition Members
are raising some issues, neither is it answered nor is it reflected upon.
Coming to the speech, the first point that the President has spoken is about
the 75th Anniversary of adoption of our Constitution. In that we are talking so much
about the Constitution, but it is sad to note the 17th Lok Sabha and the 18th Lok
Sabha have not had a Deputy Speaker. The Deputy Speaker is a Constitutionally-
provided post, which has to be filled. This Government, in the first paragraph itself,

have broken the Constitution. They have made it a ... thing that they do not have
the confidence in having even a Member from one of your allies like TDP or JD(U)
to be made the Deputy Speaker. So, I hope that the Government will look into this
and make sure that a Deputy Speaker is appointed for the Lok Sabha.
The next paragraph talks about the Mahakumbh. Everybody is talking about
the Mahakumbh, which happens once in 144 years. And it is very sad to note that
so many deaths have happened. So far, what has been reported is 30 deaths and
60 people injured. But we are suspicious of these news articles because whenever
 Expunged as ordered by the Chair.
03.02.2025 1398
a major calamity of this extent happens in this country, we expect the Home Minister
to come and give a statement in this House to mention what exactly has happened
and what measures have been taken for relief and for support of the people over
there, and what safety measures are taken to ensure that it does not happen again
in the future. Actually, I am sad that this Government has not bothered about it.
Though they claim themselves to be a Hindutva Party, they do not care about the
people who have died over there.
The next point that they are talking about is ‘housing for all’. When they are
talking about ‘housing for all’ the Government says that the Union Government is
providing funds for ‘housing for all’. But actually, if you look into the amount of
money which has been sanctioned, most of the amount which has been sanctioned
is from the State Governments. The State Governments are the ones which are
doing it. If you note the amount which has been allocated, the funds are not being
utilised because only in southern States or developed States these funds are fully
utilised and most of the other States are not utilising it, and these funds are totally
wasted.
The Government is also talking so much about trains -- the Vande Bharat
and so many other trains that this Government has launched. Earlier, in my speech
I had spoken when I was talking about Chandrayan, I had said that we can always
boast saying that we are the first nation to land in the south pole of the moon, but
you have to take into account that development of a nation is not based only on
one particular event. So, you also have to see how many accidents are happening
in these trains. We see in the news, of late, every month there is some kind of an
03.02.2025 1399
incident like there is a derailment or there is a collision where we are losing so many
lives and the Government has been mum about that. Usually, in the earlier years
we have noticed that when some major calamity happens, the Minister concerned
takes responsibility for it and steps down. But here, we are not even bothered about
the loss of lives and we continue as if nothing has happened though hundreds of
people lose their lives.
We are comparing ourselves with developed nations like Japan. They are
running something called ‘Bullet Train’ which they have been doing it for several
decades.
19.00 hrs
Not even a single instance of fatality or minor crash has ever happened. I
hope the Government would be able to achieve something like that so that we can
call ourselves a developed nation.
The hon. Prime Minister constantly talks about Viksit Bharat. I would like to
understand what they mean by ‘Viksit Bharat’. The economic criteria for a
‘developed nation’ is that you have to have a per capita income of 15,000 US
dollars. Though China has achieved a per capita income of 6,500 US dollars, it is
not considered as a developed nation. We are at a paltry amount of 2,500 US
dollars and the Government says that we are going to achieve the target of five
trillion dollars by 2030. Even if you achieve five trillion dollars, your per capita
income will only be 3,250 US dollars. When you are talking about this, I cannot
understand how the Government can say that they are working towards a Viksit
Bharat.
03.02.2025 1400
The hon. Prime Minister is always mentioning ‘Make in India’. When you say
‘Make in India’, you can only become a manufacturing hub. The present
requirement for the whole world is to think in your own country, to be able to think
and come out with some kind of intellectual properties. If you look at the largest
companies which are performing well, if you look at the amount of development and
growth that has happened in the developed nations, it is not because they are
manufacturing goods in their own country. Most of the manufactured goods have
been exported by countries like China, Indonesia, India, and Vietnam. The
developed nations are developing things like Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter
which generate a lot more revenue. So, I hope the country will focus on developing
artificial intelligence and making sure that we will be able to achieve heights of
growth by 15 per cent or 20 per cent. Unless you do that, we can never dream of
becoming a developed India in the next 100 years also.
Madam, our hon. Chief Minister has brought out a book called ‘Antiquity of
Iron’ which is translated in Tamil – ‘Irumbin Thonmai’. This pushes the Iron Age
back to 3,450 BC. I am surprised that the hon. Prime Minister has not even
mentioned this in any of his social media accounts like Twitter etc. There was also
no mention of it in the President's Address. This is something which is not a proud
moment for Tamils alone. It is a proud moment for the whole of India that the Iron
Age started in India. It started in Tamil Nadu. The archaeologists world over are
looking at us with awe. They are so shocked to know that it was our technology in
3,450 BC when we could smelt iron. This is sad that it is not being appreciated and
acknowledged by the hon. Prime Minister. I hope that the Government passes a
03.02.2025 1401
resolution over here congratulating the archaeologists and all the people who are
involved in getting this happening.
Madam, if you look at China and India, China has an economy of almost 18
trillion dollars or more. We are still struggling to achieve five trillion dollars and you
are talking about making India ‘Atmanirbhar Bharat’, where you want to make
everything here. But sadly in the last 10 years, our trade deficit was 600 billion US
dollars where you have lost out the amount of money which could have made India
export surplus. We could have been trade positive, but that has not been achieved
in the last 10 years. I hope this country focuses on ensuring that we are a trade
surplus country. The hon. President in the last line of her Address told that this is a
country of 140 crore people, but the Government is not behaving in such a way.
The Government has ensured that the minorities, the Dalits and the other
communities, are oppressed. You are treating us as though India has only 60 crore
people and you are neglecting all of them. I am hoping that you will be able to rectify
that. There is a Muslim community of 20 crore people. When the Government is
talking about bulldozers, they are pushing those people into a brink where you are
inciting their emotions, which is not healthy for this nation. I hope the Government
will stop this.

SHRI S. VENKATESAN (MADURAI): Hon. Chairperson, Vanakkam. Although
India constitutes 20 per cent of the population of the world, it has only a share of 2
per cent in the world trade. India’s share is just 4.6 per cent in Services sector and
1.5 per cent in International tourists’ arrival. We are the reason for this
backwardness. DeepSeek AI of China has made an impact worldwide recently.
Even countries like USA have started taking actions aimed at overcoming the
impact of DeepSeek AI. But what are we doing in India? The Director of a very
reputed institution of higher learning, IIT-Chennai talks about drinking cow urine
everyday as it has medicinal values. There are such people spreading the fake
practices of science and medicine. You talk very high about ‘One Nation One Tax’.
One per cent of the population has 40 percent of the income. If you levy adequate
tax on this 1 percent super rich, there is no need to levy tax on the rest of 99 percent
of our population. But on whose side you stand is very much visible from your
actions. Where is One Tax? You are levying three types of taxes for just a Popcorn.
If popcorn is not pre-packaged, 5 percent GST is levied. If Popcorn is pre-packaged
and labelled in a carton box, then the GST rate is 12 per cent and caramelised
Popcorn has a GST of 18 per cent. Friends, while talking on the President’s
Address I must say that this Government is ignoring the needs, rights and pride of
the Tamil Nadu government. The Union Government claims that they have created
a metro rail network for 1000 kilometres in the country and India is the third nation
to have such a big network of metro rail. But only a metro rail network of 54
 English translation of the speech originally delivered in Tamil.
03.02.2025 1411
kilometres is operational in Tamil Nadu. We have metro rail network in 5 cities of
Uttar Pradesh. We have metro rail network in 4 cities of Maharashtra and 2 cities
of Gujarat. We have metro rail connectivity in 2 cities of Madhya Pradesh. But we
have metro rail only in one city of Tamil Nadu, i.e. Chennai spreading over just 54
kilometres. Where is the metro rail network for Madurai? Where is the metro rail
network for Coimbatore? Tamil Nadu government has been raising these questions
time and again. We also raise this question in this august House. But the Union
government has failed to answer these questions till now. It refuses to allocate
adequate funds for metro rail expansion in Tamil Nadu. At the same time, we can
say one thing with pride in this august House. We the Hon. MPs from Tamil Nadu
are here standing with pride in this House after upsetting your conspiracy relating
to Tungsten mining. Hon. Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu passed a resolution in the
State Assembly of Tamil Nadu against Tungsten mining. We even passed
resolutions in all the Panchayat Unions and local bodies of Melur in Tamil Nadu
against Tungsten mining. Even then the Union Minister issued statements not once
or twice but thrice saying that the Union Government will not give up Tungsten
mining activities. But we never gave up. After organising non-stop agitations for 77
days we have come up victorious now. After this victory of ours, the Hon. Union
Minister says that the people of this area can sleep peacefully. It is true that people
of this area will have peaceful sleep. But actually who disturbed their sleep? You
are the ones who disturbed their peace and sleep. After cancelling the Tungsten
mining activities, hon. Union Minister now says that the people of that area can
hereafter sleep peacefully. But who gave this suffering to them? It is none other
03.02.2025 1412
than you. That is why people through their non-stop agitations have forced you to
withdraw Tungsten mining activities. But what do you say in hon. President’s
Address? You mentioned in the Address that the private sector will be allowed to
get into mining activities of rare mineral wealth. After taking away one piece of meal,
you are giving them a feast rather. This is in favour of the private companies. We
saved one Arittapatti from mining activities. But you say that you will create many
number of such Arittappatis. I want to raise another important issue here. As
mentioned with great pride by our hon. Member of Parliament of Tuticorin, the
legacy of iron technology in history is very worth-mentioning. Qutub Minar in Delhi
is a rust-free Iron Pillar. Use of iron in Kohinoor temple and iron pillar of Thar are
examples of attraction for the great legacy of India in the world history. Now another
prideful aspect has been added to the glory of this legacy. The era of iron
technology, as announced by hon. Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu, dates back to 5300
years. This is an important message to the world history that there was use of iron
and its technology in Tamil Nadu almost 5300 years ago. Below the Vindhyas at
the time when copper was only in use, iron has been into use particularly in Tamil
Nadu. There existed a knowledge based society in Tamil Nadu who rationally
approached the nature and created their life and wealth. I want to make a request
to the hon. Speaker of Lok Sabha. Lok Sabha Secretariat has informed that there
will be a special screening of an animation film on Ramayana in the Parliament
House complex exclusively for the MPs. I say that you can very well screen this
animation film on Ramayana. But prior to that I humbly request in this august House
that you have to screen the documentary film produced by Tamil Nadu on the
03.02.2025 1413
finding of existence of iron technology in Tamil Nadu about 5300 years ago.
Similarly, Governors are making the rights of the State Government a question
mark. They think themselves as kings of a small kingdom. Even after the hon.
Courts have pronounced on the functioning Governors within the limits of the
Constitution, they do not pay heed to such directions. One cannot become a king
by just wearing the dress of a king. He can only become an actor in a drama. How
can we make him understand this truth? Governors are questioning about the rights
of the Legislatures and make frequent walk out from the Legislative Assemblies. If
we elected members of this House walk out of this House then it can be called as
‘walk out’. How can it be called a ‘walk out’ when anyone and everyone coming to
this House walks out. The people of Tamil Nadu are not knowing as to how one
can wipe out his ignorance as he thinks that he has all powers. I wish to say in this
august House once again. Hon. President has mentioned about classical language.
I wish to humbly state here that what transpired during the debates in the
constituent assembly during the framing of our Constitution. There was a long
debate that took place in the Constituent Assembly on how to name the post of
hon. President. When many MPs said that particularly that position should be called
as ‘Rashtrapati’, hon. Ambedkar mentioned that it should be mentioned as
“President of India” as he was writing the Constitution in English. He refused to
mention as “Rashtrapati” in the Constitution. He further said that it will be written in
English and one can translate it in their respective mother tongue later. This was
the statement of Dr. Ambedkar. But when we celebrated the 75th year of adoption
of the Indian Constitution, in this very House, in the Joint Session of Parliament,
03.02.2025 1414
hon. President read out the Preamble in Hindi. As we were waiting for hon.
President to read the Preamble in English, it was read in Hindi by the hon.
President. We the MPs from non-Hindi speaking constituencies were totally
shocked to see this. This was a challenge to the democracy of India. I want to
categorically stress that if you want to bring this House under the control of one
particular language, the representatives of other major languages in this House
would never ever allow.
Thank you. Vanakkam.

SHRIMATI PRATIBHA SURESH DHANORKAR (CHANDRAPUR): Hon’ble
Chairperson, thank you, I rise to express my views on the motion of thanks on
President’s Address. In the President’s Address there was a mention about the
extension of PMAY. But I am sorry to say that the Aid under this scheme has not
been released yet and it is always distributed very late. The monetary assistance
given is very meager in rural area in comparison to urban area.
In the President’s Address, there was a mention about distribution of property
cards for ownership rights. But, in my district, the farmers who have been doing
farming for generations, still have not received permanent ownership rights. We
must think about how would they get the benefits of this scheme?
There was a mention about the Government’s focus on education and
employment for youths in President’s Address, but the Modi Government’s promise
to provide 2 crore jobs proved to be fake and baseless. The youths are continuously
registering their names on Rojgar Portal, but they are not getting the jobs. This
government has registered a record of the highest unemployability during its tenure
of last 10 years.
This government has started the trains like Vande Bharat, Amrit Bharat and
Namo Bharat from various places, but no new trains from the tribal district of
Chandrapur have been started. So, it is my request to the Government to restart
 English translation of the speech originally delivered in Marathi.
03.02.2025 1434
the trains for Pune and Mumbai which were discontinued during Corona Pandemic.
Hon’ble President has mentioned about the Digital India. We can see that
cybercrimes are increasing day by day. These cyber criminals are fooling the
people at large, but no concrete action is being taken against these criminals.
Hon’ble President of India talked about the pensionary benefits to the
employees, but the old pension scheme is not being implemented for the State
Government employees of Maharashtra. It is very important to them and that is why
the Central Government needs to take a concrete decision in this regard.
Our President talked about Nari Shakti Vandan, but in our Maharashtra
there is an urgent need to implement ‘Shakti law’. We have been repeatedly
requesting the Union Government in this connection but nothing has been done
in this regard. If you really want to grant equal rights to women, it is necessary to
implement this law.
The Union Government took the credit for crop insurance scheme, but it is
now more than a year, the crop insurance benefits have not been credited to
farmer’s account. This issue of MSP is also still pending. The MSP has not been
declared by the Union Government but it is very important for farmers survival.
In the President’s Address, it is talked about air connectivity and new airports.
In my Chandrapur district, it was proposed to construct a new airport at Murti, but
it is still a distant dream. My district is a mineral rich district with deposits of iron
ore. A steel hub is there. In the adjoining district of Gadchiroli, Surjagarh Mega
Project is there. Many coalfields are located in my district and hence, air
connectivity is very much needed there.
03.02.2025 1435
Many goals were set about modern and self-reliant farming in President’s
Address, but in reality, the story is totally different. No relief and assistance was
given to the poor and needy farmers when they faced natural calamities like,
unseasonal rains and hailstorming, floods and draught. They neither received crop
insurance benefits nor the loan waiver relief. We, as Government, must stand with
them in distress.
The Union Government has spent crores of rupees under Amrit Yojna to
provide tap water. We have also spent the same amount of rupees under Jal Jiwan
Mission, but it has provided no benefits for irrigation and drinking water facilities. If
we are spending this much of public money, then the positive results should also
be delivered to the people.
Sir, I have also served Maharashtra Assembly as an MLA for five years term
and today I am an MP here. But the Government is providing only Rs 5 crore under
MPLADS Fund. It should be increased up to Rs.30 to 40 crore, so that more
developmental works can be done for lacs of people.
This President’s Address depicts only the hollow promises made by the Modi
Government and hence I register my protest. I oppose this motion of thanks on the
President’s Address.

SHRI BALYA MAMA SURESH GOPINATH MHATRE (BHIWANDI): Hon.
Chairperson, thank you. I am speaking in this august House today for the first time
after the oath taking ceremony. I am really thankful to Hon. Shri Sharadchandra ji
 English translation of the speech originally delivered in Marathi.
03.02.2025 1436
Pawar for his blessings, due to which I have been elected as an M.P., Lok Sabha.
I am also thankful to Supriyatai Sule. Before, I start my speech, I want to
make it clear that I would like to focus on my State Maharashtra and my Lok Sabha
Constituency Bhiwandi.
Sir, we all are well aware that the maximum tax collection is generated from
Maharashtra. But, as you know, every time Maharashtra is ignored and injustice is
done to Maharashtra State. Today, at Centre as well as at State, BJP is in power
but still nothing has been changed. Out of total tax collection, at least 50% funds
should be given to our State. But unfortunately, it is not more than 10-15% and I
register my protest against this injustice.
Secondly, bullet train route is passing through my Lok Sabha constituency.
Its car shed is also situated in my constituency. Sir, thousands of crores have been
spent for this project. But there are cases of corruption in this Mega project. I have
talked to collector and SP. This is a dream project of Modiji and I would like to
congratulate Modiji.
There are rampant cases of improper valuation of lands and structures
related to this project. They are changing the valuation of the construction every
year and in this way, they have minted money. These corruption cases must be
unearthed. You should look into it properly and you would come to know how they
are doing corruption in bullet train project.
Late Nana Shankarsheth is regarded as a pioneer of railway system in India.
He was born in Murbad. But this Murbad Tehsil is waiting for railway connectivity.
In the year 2022-23, the railway line project worth Rs. 864 crore was sanctioned.
03.02.2025 1437
But it needed Rs. 1300 crore for land acquisition to complete this project. Still, this
amount has not been sanctioned. I would like to request the Union Government to
sanction this fund to complete this Rail project. By completing this project in
Murbad, we can pay homage to Nana Shankarsheth.
On one hand, we are talking about bullet train, but on the other hand, we are
ignoring the basic facilities to railway passengers. At many railway stations like
Washind, Asangaon, Adgaon, Badlapur, no basic amenities like, benches, toilets
and sheds are available there. I request railway administration to provide these
facilities to the middle-class railway passengers.
You talked about farmers’ welfare. But this Government is doing nothing for
farmers. It is completely ignoring the issues like doubling the farmers’ income,
increasing the farm yield and MSP. We know that the number of farmers suicide
cases is highest in India. The geographical condition is different in Maharashtra.
So, it is Government’s duty to support these farmers in this condition.
Government should also try to change these geographical conditions following the
other countries in the world.
Recently, there was a Kumbh Mela and a historic event took place at
Prayagraj after 144 years. We cannot make arrangement for such a huge gathering
within 2-3 months’ time. It takes years together for preparation. Many people have
been died there due to stampede. I pay my homage to them but, who is responsible
for this?
The Government school’s condition is very pathetic. All schools should be
made digital. The school students should get free medical treatment. If we care
03.02.2025 1438
for better health and education, it would definitely help to build our country’s future.
There were no law and order in Beed and Parbhani districts of Maharashtra
and same kind of situation is developing in my Bhiwandi region too. There is one
notorious criminal in my Lok Sabha Constituency. He has committed all kinds of
crimes like murders, attempt to murder etc. But he is still going scot-free and
nobody dares to arrest him.
Lastly, I would like to request to increase the MPLADS fund.
You heard me patiently. I am really thankful to you.

Sir, within the first year of this third-term Government, the Government has
trampled upon the spirit of the Constitution by introducing controversial legislations
like the Waqf Bill and the Constitution (One Hundred and Twenty–Ninth
Amendment) Bill, 2024 and the Union Territories Laws (Amendment) Bill, 2024
The Waqf Bill marked as a step towards empowerment and modernisation is
in reality a direct assault on the secular fabric of our nation. It is yet another attempt
to tighten Government’s control over the religious and cultural traditions of the
Muslim community, undermining their autonomy in the name of religion.
Meanwhile, the proposal for ‘One-Nation, One-Election’ poses an existential
threat to India's federal structure. It is nothing more than an attempt to centralize
power, diminish the political, economic and social autonomy of the States. Even
today, the States are struggling under an unfair fiscal arrangement, particularly after
the introduction of the GST. The Centre's political agenda has overshadowed the
rightful allocation of tax revenues, pushing many States into financial distress,
forcing them into debt rather than empowering them with their due resources. This
Government claims to uphold the Constitution, but its action speaks otherwise. It is
our duty to call out these contradictions and defend the very principles that define
India's democracy.

SHRI SANJAY UTTAMRAO DESHMUKH (YAVATMAL-WASHIM): Hon.
Chairman, thank you. Through the President’s Address, this Government talked
only about tall promises and assurances, but if we look at the actions of this
Government closely, we will find them completely baseless, misguiding and without
any substance. Today, we can see that the unemployment rate is at the highest
level in our country. Inflation has brought an ill effect on the people. The number
of farmers suicides is increasing day by day. Crimes against women are also
increasing. No employment opportunities are being provided to the youth and the
Government of the day is busy with making only hollow promises and misleading
slogans.
Chairman Sir, this Government declared many mega schemes like Make in
India, startup India, Digital India, but in vein. These Schemes proved to be bogus
and formed only the part of Government’s advertisement and propaganda policy.
Even today the MSME sector is struggling for its existence.
This Government promised to generate employment but, unfortunately no
vacancies are being filled. On the contrary, Government is pushing for
privatization.
In my Lok Sabha Constituency, Yavatmal-Washim, many students are still
unemployed even after taking higher education. Farmers’ children are looking for
jobs in cities but it is of no use. This Government talked about development of
 English translation of the speech originally delivered in Marathi.
03.02.2025 1451
trade and industry but the industrial units are shutting down. There is a depression
in manufacturing sector and hence it stops new investments.
India is an agriculture dominated country and farmer is a backbone of our
economy. But, they continue to suffer and their condition is deteriorating day by
day. The concept of MSP is on the paper only. The benefits of crop insurance
scheme are not being provided to farmers. And these debt ridden farmers had to
commit suicide because that is the only option left for them.
These farmers desperately need the MSP, loan waiver and a long term
agriculture policy. If you look at the number of farmers’ suicide cases, you can
easily understand that the agriculture policies of this Government have been failed
completely.
In Yavatmal district, 1,91,326 farmers suffered the losses over the 1,76,600
hectare of land due to heavy rains. But still dues of approximately Rs. 214 crore
are pending. The farmers did not receive a single penny.
In Amravati Division, at many places like Amravati, Akola, and Buldhana, the
standing crops were washed away completely and the farmers had to suffer heavy
losses.
Around, Rs. 386 crore are still lying with Maharashtra Government. But it did
not distribute it till date.
In Washim District, there were also huge losses due to heavy downpour. But
this district was not included under the Scheme. Farmers suffered losses, but they
were not included in Government list.
In the President’s Address, the Government talked about Jal Jiwan Mission
03.02.2025 1452
Scheme and also made tall claims. Through the ‘Har Ghar Jal’ Scheme, it is
claimed to provide tap water to every household. But the situation in my Lok Sabha
Constituency is totally different. They never check ground realities because people
in rural area still roam here and there to fetch water. This Government also talked
about interlinking of rivers project. In Vidarbha region they are making tall
promises.
Vainganga and Nalganga rivers interlinking project is really a visionary
project, but an injustice done to Washim district because this district is excluded.
This is an aspirational, backward and drought ridden district. If this project is a life
changing project, for the Vidarbha region, then why Washim district is ignored
completely?
In Pusad Tehsil, the people of 42 villages, are searching for water endlessly.
During summer, they had to suffer a lot even for a single drop of water. The people
of Mahapathar area are urging the Government to kindly look into it sympathetically.
Water is needed for drinking and irrigation purposes. Availability of water can
change everything in this region. Why this Govt. is giving step motherly treatment
to the people of Washim district?
The Union Government should consider the problem of unemployed and
educated youth of this country seriously. Do not indulge only in sloganeering. You
should know about the ground reality and actual problems of the people at large.
You cannot run this country only by false propaganda and marketing tactics. Your
advertising skills would not help you in long run.
So, face the reality and know what is happening actually. This country can
03.02.2025 1453
be run only by honesty, truthfulness and democratic values.
Thank you.

DR. THOL THIRUMAAVALAVAN (CHIDAMBARAM): Hon Chairman,
Vanakkam. I sincerely thank you for giving me this Opportunity to speak on the
Motion of Thanks to the Hon President’s Address. We can say that the Hon
President’s Address can be called as a prelude the General Budget. It is so natural
that the Hon President’s Address remains a prelude announcing the aspects of the
forthcoming Budget in advance.
I should say that both the Hon President’s Address and Budget are not
boosting our confidence. I am pained to state that the Hon President’s Address is
very disappointing for the marginalised sections of the people including the
Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, minorities and OBCs. I want to pinpoint
several aspects of the President’s Speech here. Hon President has taken pride for
having rescued about 25 Crore people from poverty. But we still have Crores of
people suffering in poverty and facing difficulties due to price rise.
I am left with sadness in mind to ask whether or not Hon President is aware
of this? Hon President has also stated with pride that 3 Crore new houses would
be built by the Government. Madam President has made a repetition of several
announcements that were uttered by her during the previous Joint Session of
Parliament. One such announcement pertains to the construction of Houses under
PM Awas Yojana. Last time, as much as Rs 54,500 Crore was allocated for this
 English translation of the speech originally delivered in Tamil.
03.02.2025 1454
Scheme and only Rs 32,400 Crore was spent by the Government. The spending
was cut almost to half of the total allocation of this Scheme. The Government did
not spend Rs 22,100 Crore allocated for this Housing Scheme. As a result this
Scheme could not be completed in several places. Many such works are
incomplete. Funds allocated are spent without reaching completion. People have
not been given constructed Houses as the targets were not achieved. This is a
matter of great concern.
I request you to ponder over this. Union Government allocates 1 lakh and
20 thousand for constructing one Unit. Remaining 40 per cent has to be borne by
the State Government which comes to Rs 70,000. Whereas the State Government
gives an additional Rs 50,000 matching the grant of Union Government and makes
its contribution as Rs 1,20,000 per unit. Along with this under MGNREGA Scheme,
Rs 27,000 is also allocated by the State Government.
I want to ask you that how a House measuring 280 sq. ft. can be constructed
with just a meagre amount of Rs 2 lakh and 67 thousand. That is why I say this
President’s Address seems very ornamental and very disappointing. If we take for
instance, at least Rs 2500 is required for constructing one sq. ft., Rs 5 lakh would
be required for constructing a House in an area of 280 sq. ft. Kalaignar Dream
House Scheme in Tamil Nadu provides Rs 5 lakh for constructing a House as this
was implemented successfully by the Hon Chief Minister of Tamil Nadu. The State
Government has taken up this responsibility. I therefore urge that at least Rs 5 lakh
should be allocated for the PM Awas Yojana for construction of Houses besides
monitoring at all levels of its execution.
03.02.2025 1455
Next important point I wish to raise is about the SC and ST Sub-Plan. We
have separate Sub-Plans for SCs and STs. As per the population of pour country,
15 percent of the funds are to be allocated to the welfare of SCs and 7.5 percent of
funds for the welfare of STs. This forms the basis of these Sub-Plans. Our total
spending is of Rs 50 lakh and 65 thousand. This was announced by the
Government. If we calculate 15 percent of this amount. It comes to Rs 7, 59,081
Crore. This is the justifiable amount of allocation. But only 1, 68,478 Crore was
allocated to the welfare of SCs. By this reduced allocation, the Union Government
takes away the share of Rs 5, 91,000 Crore, almost 6 lakh Crore. This amount they
do not want to allocate or spend for the welfare of SCs. This is against the interests
of the people belonging to SC community. Similarly the STs are also ignored.
I will conclude by saying an important thing. Pre-matric and Post-matric
Scholarships are being provided to the students belonging to SCs, STs and
minorities. During the previous Budget, Rs 6, 360 Crore was allocated for this.
But Rs 7, 60 Crore remained unspent. There is inadequate or very less
allocation of funds made for Overseas Scholarships for such students. This is not
doing justice to the students belonging to these communities. Particularly druing
the pervious Budget, there was an allocation of Rs 326 Crore for the pre-matric
scholarships meant for the minority students. That too, out of this Rs 326 Crore only
as little as Rs 90 Crore was spent for this purpose. Now they have reduced this
allocation for Pre-matric scholarships to just Rs 195 Crore. As regards Post-matric
Scholarships, as against the allocation of Rs 1, 145 Crore during previous Budget,
only Rs 344 Crore could be spent by this Government. Even that allocation has
03.02.2025 1456
been reduced now to Rs 413.9 Crore for providing Post-matric Scholarships. This
has seen a drastic reduction of almost Rs 700 Crore.
I must say that this will lead to a planned destruction of denying education to
minorities and it may be seen as a worst conspiracy to keep them in darkness. I
therefore want to say that this Hon President’s Address is just ornamental and
appreciative of BJP Government in power and nothing is visionary. I urge that this
Government should think of uplifting the common masses including SCs, STs,
OBCs and Minorities. Thank you.

SHRI ASADUDDIN OWAISI (HYDERABAD): Thank you, Sir, for giving me this
opportunity. I stand to oppose the Motion of Thanks on the President’s Address.
Sir, I start by putting to this House how the foreign policy of our country has
been compromised from what its traditional position has been. Take the case of
Palestine. About 61,709 Palestinians have been killed, and about 1,11,588 have
been injured. India is a party to Genocide Convention. The Genocide Convention
says that anyone who is party to the Genocide Convention must contribute to
03.02.2025 1460
ensure that it prohibits from contributing to genocide and also requires to take steps
to prevent it.
Sir, not a single hospital is there in Gaza. This country, this great nation of
mine had constructed two floors of a library in a university. Those universities and
that library has been finished. You have 1.2 million people being made homeless,
deliberate starvation has happened, and people do not have access to drinking
water. And, what do we see now? We have heard the Prime Minister saying, ‘Stop
the war in Ukraine.’ Where is my Prime Minister; where is this Government? Why
are they keeping quiet on this genocide which is being perpetrated on the
Palestinians? What has been the traditional stance of our country, from Mahatma
Gandhi up to all the Prime Ministers? Why can we not oppose genocide? We are a
party to Genocide Convention. I am warning this Government that when the
genocide history of Gaza will be written, this Prime Minister will also be on the list
of accomplices. I demand from the Government that let this Government, let our
country take the responsibility of constructing the health sector in Gaza, invite more
Palestinians to India on scholarship; let them rebuild Gaza. We cannot be a party.
How can we allow arms to be exported to Israel? An Indian ship was stopped in
Spain. We are signatory to this Genocide Convention.
How will history remember us? With what face can we face the world?
Secondly, Sir, India's moral voice is dead in Palestine. It is completely dead.
With regard to China, the Army Chief in an interview to an English newspaper
ruled out any troop cut along LAC. He said, ‘no troop normalization can happen’.
Why are we back to business as usual? Mansarovar Yatra will start, direct flights
03.02.2025 1461
will start, visas will be issued, and Chinese investments will be welcomed with open
arms. Can we patrol those 25 patrolling points which we have lost? Can we go and
patrol in the buffer zones of Galwan, Pangong Tsu, Gogra, Hot Spring and Kailash?
No. We have lost 2,000 square kilometres of land, and it is back to business with
China! I strongly condemn this step which this Government is taking in the fear of
China.
Now, let us come to USA. A Pew Research Study says that 7.25 lakh
unauthorized immigrant Indians are in USA, and about 18,000 are in the final list
for removal. Are we going to accept all these 7.25 lakh people? Are we going to
accept these 18,000 people who are in the final list for removal? How many aircraft
do you require? You would require about 300 aircraft. Can this Government
imagine the loss of remittance? What will be their employment over here? They
have no policy.
Sir, with respect to Bangladesh, I would like to say that we are destroying our
relationship with Bangladesh for the sake of one person. When will my Prime
Minister meet his counterpart in Bangladesh? I fear that we will be out of the game.
When I say the term ‘game’, the Government understands that.
So far as minorities are concerned, I would submit that in 2023-24, out of a
budgetary allocation of Rs.3,000 crore, an amount of Rs.2,064 crore was
surrendered by the Ministry of Minority Affairs. For the last about 10-11 years, I
have been consistently demanding that the Pre-Metric, Post-Metric and Merit cum-
Means Scholarships should be made demand-driven because only Rs.2,000 crore
are required. Because of its not being demand-driven, you have thousands of
03.02.2025 1462
Muslim students who are out of education. There have been empirical studies like
the Kundu Committee which said that the reason for Muslim out-of-school children
is financial wherewithal. You are not interested to ensure that Muslim children
study.
My community has the highest illiteracy rate in women. Out of 100 students,
I have only three graduates. You are after Jamia Millia; you are after AMU and you
have cut down the funding for Madarsas. Yes, you do not read. You have cut down
the money on Madarsas also. I mean, if you read, you will know it. Why can you
not do it, Sir?
Then, I would ask you to come to Mustafabad and Okhla and see. You have
the Mount Everest of garbage which is being dumped right inside the Muslim area.
Why? Because it is a Muslim area. Then, you have this person who was given birth
politically through surrogacy by an institute which has an allegiance to the person
who wrote ‘We, or Our Nationhood Defined’ and ‘Bunch of Thoughts’. That person
day in and day out lies and lies. His start and end points are lies only. But what I
am hearing from the ground, which I have seen, a pure, organic Muslim leadership
will emerge on Wednesday.
Sir, I am cautioning and warning this Government. If you bring in and make
a Waqf law in the present form, which will be a violation of Articles 25, 26 and 14,
it will lead to social instability in this country. It has been rejected by the entire
Muslim community. No waqf property will be left. Nothing will be left, I say. If you
want to make India a Viksit Bharat, we also want a Viksit Bharat. Do you want to
take this country back into the early 80s and 90s? It will be your responsibility
03.02.2025 1463
because as a proud Indian Muslim, I will not lose an inch of my Masjid. Forget about
a Masjid, I will not lose an inch of my dargah. I will not allow that.
We will no more come and give a diplomatic talk over here. This is the House
where I have to stand up and say honestly on behalf of my community, which says
that we are proud Indians, that it is my property, not given by anyone. You cannot
snatch it away from me. Waqf is a form of worship for me.
Sir, let me talk about unemployment. Youth unemployment has increased to
45 per cent. I will give you an example. At Mumbai airport, for about 1800 jobs,
15,000 graduates turned up.
I must congratulate the Prime Minister. In Bihar and UP, train stations
overflow with aspirants every time there is an exam for a Government job. In public
sector, in oil and gas sector, 15,000 jobs have been lost even though their revenue
doubled. Central PSUs eliminated 2.7 lakh jobs over the past decade. During April-
August 2024, 11,500 MSMEs have been shut down. Employment in manufacturing
sector fell from 12.6 per cent in 2011-12 to 11.4 per cent in 2022-23.
Sir, in conclusion, I would like to say this. What is the Government showing
us? I really feel pity for the allies of NDA. Despite the fact that they are 240 in
number, they are irrelevant now. You can take something here and there but what
about the country at large? What about the youth of this country? The only nation
in the world which has demographic dividend is India. This Government has failed
to uplift the youth. That is why, on the grounds which I have submitted, I oppose
the Motion of Thanks on the President’s Address.
Thank you.
03.02.2025 1464

SHRI NILESH DNYANDEV LANKE (AHMEDNAGAR): Hon. Chairman Sir, thank
you very much. Today, I rise to participate in the discussion on motion of thanks on
the President’s Address. Sir, our life is very busy nowadays and that is why our
health is also very important. In this year’s budget, there are many announcements
made by Central Government regarding health schemes, but still there are some
shortcomings.
In this budget, Many major health related issues have been completely
ignored. The total allocation for health has been increased but, it is still very less in
comparison to the GDP. We have a population of 140 crore people and that is why
it is a meager allocation. More primary health centers in rural areas and District
 English translation of the speech originally delivered in Marathi.
03.02.2025 1492
Hospitals in urban areas are needed as the number of health centers is very less
and not up to the mark.
In many hospitals and health centers, doctors and medical staff is not
available. So, the recruitment of doctors and nursing staff in sufficient numbers is
very necessary. All kind of medical infrastructure should also be developed at such
centers.
This Government is implementing Ayushman Bharat Scheme, but it is beneficial
only to the private hospitals and doctors. Public health service is deteriorating day
by day and a common man is not benefitted out of it, but the private medical
institutions are taking advantage of it.
Sir, we must focus on research in pharmaceutical sciences and new medical
techniques and more fund should be allocated for this purpose. As you know, we
had to face Corona Pandemic in the year 2019-20 and lakhs of people died during
Covid. Keeping this in mind, the Government should allocate maximum fund for
health sector. There is a medical emergency in our country because small children
falling prey to the diseases like cancer. Neonatal and newborns are also facing the
liver and kidney diseases. Considering this situation, we must allocate maximum
fund for Health Ministry. In western countries, they prefer public health service and
not the private one. These countries are providing all kinds of medical facilities to
their citizens. But, in India, we are lacking behind and not providing sufficient and
satisfactory health services to our citizens. Many schemes like Mahatma Jyotirao
Phule Jan Arogya Yojana, Mukhyamantri Sahayata Nidhi, or Ayushman Bharat are
being run by state and central Governments. But the financial assistance which is
03.02.2025 1493
provided under these schemes is meagre and it cannot even match the hospital
expenses. The cancer like diseases require Rs. 30-40 lakh for treatment, and for
kidney and liver transplant you need Rs. 20-25 lakh.
A common poor person cannot afford this huge medical expresses. Hence,
it is need of the hour that we must allocate more funds commensurate with our GDP
growth. I seriously feel that this government has completely ignored the interests
of farmers. No concrete decisions have been taken for farmers’ welfare in this
year’s budget. There was no clear announcement regarding granting the legal
sanctity to MSP.
There is a significant cut back in the subsidy for fertilizers, diesel and seeds
and due to this, there is a higher possibility of increasing the production cost. But,
the loan waiver and improved crop insurance still remains a distant dream for crores
of farmers. Everybody was waiting for it, but it has been completely ignored while
drafting the budget. This government is inviting big corporate players in agriculture
business and that is why there is a potential threat to small and marginalized
farmers. So, it is clear that, there was no focus on farmers in this budget.
The Union Government should start new schemes to increase milk rates.
MSP should be given for the milk production and its procurement. It is at Rs. 35-40
per litre considering the production cost. But, the procurement rate should be based
on the actual production cost of milk. Government should act strictly against the
milk adulteration because the milk adulteration is causing cancer to small children.
It is also very important to look into it seriously.
Please give me half a minute more.
03.02.2025 1494

ह(cid:167) । बीते वष(cid:334) म(cid:164) शहरी (cid:177)े(cid:253)(cid:332) म(cid:164) सरकारी सं(cid:214)थाओ ंका िव(cid:214)तार सरकार (cid:300)ारा िकया गया है । सरकार के

था, निदय(cid:332) से निदय(cid:332) को जोड़ना, उसी को (cid:197)यान म(cid:164) रखते ह(cid:242)ए सरकार ने सूखा (cid:250)(cid:214)त इलाक(cid:332) म(cid:164) िसंचाई

ह ै । 40 हजार करोड़ (cid:321)पये क(cid:236) लागत स े के न-बेतवा िलंक प(cid:229)रयोजना से म(cid:197)य (cid:255)देश और उ(cid:176)र (cid:255)देश के

माननीय सभापित : नागर जी, एक िमनट (cid:321)िकए । सभा का समय तीन िमनट और आग े बढ़ाया गया ।
अब आप बोिलए ।
(cid:174)ी रोडमल नागर : जो लोग अंितम पंि(cid:265) म (cid:164)खड़े ह(cid:167), उनके चेहर ेपर इस सरकार के काय(cid:170)(cid:248)म से खुशहाली
आ रही ह ै । बड़ी खुशी क(cid:236) बात है िक मेर े (cid:214)वयं के िजले म(cid:164), जो आका(cid:177)ं ी िजला है, आदरणीय (cid:255)धान मं(cid:253)ी
जी के सहयोग से वहां एक मेिडकल कॉलेज क(cid:236) (cid:214)थापना क(cid:236) गई है । िपछले दस वष(cid:334) म(cid:164) आिदवासी बह(cid:242)ल
इलाक(cid:332) म(cid:164) तीस नए मेिडकल कॉलेज खोले गए ह(cid:167) । िवशेष रा(cid:213)(cid:367)ीय िमशन चलाकर आिदवासी समदु ाय

के (cid:322)प म(cid:164) मनाया जा रहा है । चाहे धरोहर एवं सा(cid:214)ं कृितक चेतना के पुनजा(cid:170)गरण क(cid:236) बात हो, िव(cid:309) के

LIVE TELECAST OF PROCEEDINGS OF LOK SABHA
Lok Sabha proceedings are being telecast live on Sansad T.V. Channel. Live telecast begins at 11
A.M. everyday the Lok Sabha sits, till the adjournment of the House.
